1253312222.title: Rita Skeeter

choices: Rita waits for Harry to turn up.
Harry is uneasy as he waits for Rita to show up.
Harry, Ron and Hermione are ready for her arrival.
12533122221.content:
Rita Skeeter was at her desk at the Daily Prophet when she was hit by the revelation. She was getting old, and her contacts were getting old. The magical journalism business was cut throat enough, without the problems of being behind, too far behind, with the headlines.

It was clear that a new generation of wizards was rising, and rising fast.

There were a host of students at Hogwarts, that were likely to shape the future, and take on considerable power in the coming days, months and years. She ground her teeth at her missteps during the Tri-wizard tournament. She'd dismissed them as children, treating them with undisguised contempt.

She no longer thought of Harry Potter as 'the Boy who lived', a lucky child who survived an attack by the Dark Lord, and was she no longer dismissed him as lacking talent. Her experience had cautioned her against inherited magic superiority. A view she never voiced, but she knew parentage was no guarantee for a good wizard.

A few years ago, she had had to spike a story. Too much pressure from above, she'd 'discovered' Draco Malfoy's magical rating, his inherent abilities. His magical potential was barely more than a squib. She'd presented her evidence and story to her editor, eager to please, and zealous for a by-line.

It was meticulously researched, and corroborated. She rubbed her hands in anticipation, but when the editor had returned to her desk. Her original notes were heavily annotated. She was told in no uncertain terms to lose the story. The Prophet would never publish it, and she'd be very unwise to try and sell it elsewhere.

Her own magic skills were quite formidable, as well as being an unregistered animagus, she had incredible discovery magical powers. She analysed her own notes. The pages took on a glow that came from her own Quick Quills, with a gesture to the side, she swept those away.

The other magical touches became clearer. The pages were much dimmer, and she recognised her editor's magic, another gesture and those were swept away too.

What remained was the touch of a powerful magic user. He had notes had been scried, someone was looking for the source of her work. The people who had talked to her. A scowl crossed her face, her editor had undermined a basic principle of journalism. Hell, it was true even of muggle journalism, you don't reveal your sources.

She pulled apart the traces of the magic on her notes. Detecting the alignment, and preferences of the user. It confirmed her suspicions, the notes, HER notes had been handed over to a Malfoy. Probably Lucius, but Narcissa was not without her methods, and was fierce in the defence of her child.

Even now, she found herself grinding her teeth. The protection that the powerful had, was unjust. She hated the privilege that came easily to some. Controlling her anger, she wondered if that had coloured her judgement, her actions in the past.

She wrote off a note, an apology, to Harry Potter. It was sincere, and she realised that she had to mend bridges to the current generation. She asked for an audience... a meeting, a chance to set the record straight.

She gave it to her owl, and set him off on the long journey to Scotland, and the castle.

The reply came back the next day. It was better than she'd hoped. Harry had agreed to meet her in Hogsmeade, his note was polite, and well-written. She had a good eye for these things, and thought he'd make a good journalist, or writer if he wanted to turn his skills in that direction.

There was a time and a location.

The Tea shop in the High Street in two days time.
12533122221.title: Rita Skeeter

choices: Harry wants to squash Rita like the bug she is.
Harry gets her to take a binding magical contract.
Hermione and Ron discuss what to do with Rita.
125331222213.content:
Harry, Ron and Hermione were sharing a large pot of tea. Hermione and Harry rather enjoying it. While Ron looked more like he was involved in some arcane ceremony. Rita went a little closer. She was still nervous, and not surprised that Harry had brought along some company... some witnesses.

Her instincts kept her on edge. But that didn't stop her from being covered with an upturned jar. She scuttled about, but was unable to escape. Harry looked down on her. His face distorted through the glass. But his face was triumphant, and unhappy. He slide a postcard underneath. It was slow enough, that she didn't risk getting her spindly legs broken.

She fell to the bottom, when he up-ended the bottle. With a clap, the lid was put in place, and a twist was enough to secure it.

Her antennae twitched as she raced around the jar. The sides were too slick to climb, but at least there were some air holes in the top.

Hermione took the container from Harry's hands and put it on the table out of reach.

"See. I told you we could make our own Marauder's Map for Hogsmeade."

"I didn't say we couldn't. I just said maybe we shouldn't."

"Well, it turned out useful here and now."

She turned towards the trapped little bug, "It is useful, isn't it."

Tapping the glass, the effect was deafening inside the little jar. Hermione slid her finger down the side until she tapped the map, spread across the tabletop.

Rita saw a plan of Hogsmeade, an active map, with the moving position of the various wizards and witches marked on it. Their little feet leaving trails as they passed.

Her eyes were drawn to the end of Hermione's finger. The position of the three friends was marked. But more revealing, she was still present. If an insect's shoulders could drop, they would have dropped here. Rita couldn't even deny she was there.
125331222213.title: Harry, Ron and Hermione are ready for her arrival.

choices: Rita glows as she thinks of agreeing.
She bites Harry's palm, and makes a break for freedom.
Ron suggests that Harry adds more conditions to the binding promise.
1253312222132.content:
Harry points to a quieter corner of the Tea-room. "If you get the map, we can talk privately."

It was darker in the corner, a feature that appealed to a number of customers.

"I'm... We're investigating animagi." Harry told her.

Ron sat down, still tucking the map away. Hermione looked around before joining the boys. The three students drew absolutely no attention.

"You could have just come and talked to us." Ron added. "But no. You had to spy first, didn't you?"

Hermione settled Ron with a hand, or rather distracted him from Rita.

"You probably know ... or suspect that my father was an animagus."

The beetle gave a quick tilt of the head. Harry took that to mean yes. It didn't really matter. "His friends were also animagi."

Rita was surprised. Her information was good on James Potter and his friends, so this was a revelation.

"I'm working... We're working on a potion, and it requires some animagus DNA., some of your hair."

The beetle shifted agitatedly. Clearly unwilling to submit.

"If you prefer, I could use an antenna. And when you return to human form... when we allow you back into human form, you'll just have to see how you manage."

The bug was powerless to resist. Her greatest gift as an animagus was stealth. A bug was ignored, it lacked the intimidation of a wolf, or the cuddliness of a cat.

"I appreciate that you might want to renege on our little deal. But I must caution you against that. I can keep you in that jar and feed you on scraps for a very long time. And I think you know what happens when a wizard or witch spends too long in their animal form."

Rita was rather a vain woman, even if she didn't like to admit it. She preferred to think of her attraction coming from her talent, her writing skills.

Harry shook the jar, and took the lid off while she was still bouncing from side to side.

"I'm thinking of a binding promise. That you will give us a lock of your hair, and also that you will never knowingly write lies about people... ever again."

As Rita settled herself, she found the jar upended and she was sitting on Harry Potter's hand.

"You'll glow when you agree to be bound by the promise."

Ron looked on seemingly enjoying the spectacle. Hermione was less happy about the situation. However, neither of the friends was ready to interfere. This was well earned on Rita's part. Her poisonous words had made life at Hogwarts a living hell.
1253312222132.title: Harry gets her to take a binding magical contract.

choices: They head back to Hogwarts.
They finish their tea, and discuss the potion ingredients.
As they sit there, they spot a curious figure enter.
12533122221321.content:
Harry watches at the bug scampered about agitatedly, before surrendering. She turned to face Harry, and then she glowed, as Rita made the binding promise.

"I know you think you've been hard done by here. But you should remember, that you could have come openly, instead of spying on us."

Harry placed the jar on an empty seat, before unwinding the glass jar and lifting it clear. His hand was barely out of the way, as Rita changed back into her human form. Her mouth hinted at a snarl, while her eyes darted about in fear.

It took her a moment to settle down. She took a pair of nail scissors from her bag, and snipped at her fringe before passing the lock to Harry.

"Thank you." he said with a tilt of his head.

Rita hated when she was outmanoeuvred. But she had to admit that Harry wasn't merely a child born to unfortunate parents. He had genuine magical skill, and intelligence. Smoothing imaginary wrinkles from her skirt, she tried to bring things back on track.

"Sorry, Harry. You are right." Her words were painfully spoken, and carried little emotion.

Ron didn't believe her. While Harry was more familiar with painful admissions.

She leaned back in her chair, and addressed them all.

"I know we didn't get off to the best of starts... some journalistic advantages are hard to resist. But I wanted to make peace."

"You've not convinced me that you are a changed individual. But I await your next few columns with interest. If I see that you haven't tried to twist your promise, then we can work to build our trust."

Rita weighed up his words. It was a wasted journey, but should still hold promise. She decided to entrust the three students with her attempt to release Draco's less than impressive magic record.

The three friends shared an look. Draco was dangerous, treacherous and intelligent, but when it came to magic he was more bluster than reality. Using his family as intimidation.

Harry decided to make his own overture.

"If we are successful with this potion, then I will come to you with all the details, and that's a promise."

Ron shrugged, while Hermione was a little put out, at Harry giving that snippet away.

Rita was happy with the conclusion, and shook their hands before leaving. If she were quick she could catch the next train south.

She thanked them sincerely, and the friends smiled at her. Ron more so as she left the tea-room.
12533122221321.title: Rita glows as she thinks of agreeing.

choices: They head off to find Madam Rolanda Hooch.
The three friends head off into the attic spaces of the castle.
Unfortunately, they have a class to attend before they can search.
125331222213211.content:
Back at the castle the three friends, assembled the spell components. Rita's lock of hair being added to the 3 strands of Professor McGonagall's hair. Harry brought out a few coarse hairs from Sirius's fur.

Harry restrained a smile, as Ron was fascinated not by the ingredients but by the inventiveness of muggles. A cellophane bag, with a seal at the top. Hermione gave Harry a look, while Ron was sealing and unsealing the strip at the top. They'd both learned long ago, not to take the mickey.

As a present, Harry had sent Arthur Weasley a pack of empty bags. Ron hadn't stopped talking about how excited his father had been to receive them. Although it did invite a whole series of questions about other muggle artifacts that had caught his interest. Harry had had to request pictures to find out what he was talking about. He was endlessly fascinated with TV remotes... How did they work without magic?

With an effort Harry brought his concentration back to the task in hand. Putting them each in separate little baggies, he tucked them away.

"I think we should keep looking for something of your father's?" Hermione urged.

"We already have three different strands of DNA, different animals. Are you sure we need a fourth?"

Harry was eager to start work on the potion. There was also the fact that he didn't like this focus on his dead father. James had obviously loved his time at Hogwarts, and Harry felt the loss more intensely whenever his name came up. He also felt that it was personal and something he didn't want to share.

"We could try the old Quidditch supplies, if we check the snitch, and the bludgers, there might be some DNAs on that?" Ron said.

The Rivelio parentis spell to scan for DNA was something that Hermione had created. Something she was protective of, too. She'd confided in Harry that she was worried, that some wizards might abuse it. That it could be used to prove the difference between full-blood wizards, and others with muggle parents, or mixed muggle-wizard parents.

Ron liked the spell, but his knowledge of DNA was uncertain at best. The red hair of Weasley's was the simplest way she explained it to him. Hermione loved Ron, and knew he wasn't stupid. But there were certain things that were alien to him. That anyone else might find more intuitive.

She pecked Ron on the cheek, "Good idea."

They both blushed, and Harry studiously paid attention to the strands of hair.
125331222213211.title: They head back to Hogwarts.

choices: Just as they give up, Hermione spots a faint glow.
They have to go ahead without anything from James Potter.
Hermione suggests another place to look.
1253312222132112.content:
Ron's brothers had reported finding an old cache of school equipment high up in the second tallest tower of the castle. There had to be something there. Harry was hopeful, as the Captain he would have been awarded the match balls.

Harry tried not to think of the possibility that he'd taken them home to Godric's Hollow. The idea that that was something else lost from Harry past made him shudder.

Harry was first into the loft, his wand lighting the way. Ron followed, and Hermione brought up the rear.

Rivelio Parentis.

Harry cast the spell, and the light from the tip of his wand flickered and shifted. Hermione followed, and Ron spoke at the same time. Both touched their wands against the bare skin on the back of Harry's hand. They weren't looking for Weasley or Granger artifacts after all.

Lumos Maximus.

The light flared as Harry's wand grew to a blinding whiteness. It illuminated the whole room. The room was much bigger than it appeared on the outside, much like the Room of Requirement. Ron's groan was louder than he intended.

The three friends split up, and started working through the various boxes for anything that might have a stray hair from James Potter.

An hour of intense searching later, and the three were covered head to toe in dust. Beyond hearing Ron squeal whenever he thought he saw a spider they discovered nothing. Nothing was giving off the pale blue glow that would confirm James Potter's DNA.
1253312222132112.title: The three friends head off into the attic space...

choices: Moaning Myrtle watches with interest.
They open the door, and Hermione is standing there, looking disapproving.
"What will be your animagus form?" Ron asks.
12533122221321122.content:
Hermione wants to keep looking, and wants to ask Professor McGonagall if there's something of James's that she can recall. As they were both Quidditch players, perhaps she knows where something might be found.

With a sigh, Harry agrees.

Hermione sets off, with the idea of telling the teacher it's a surprise for Harry she goes alone.

Ron and Harry talk, once Hermione's footsteps retreat downstairs out of hearing.

"She's not going to find anything. There isn't anything to find." Harry said.

"But... we could wait. We should wait." Ron senses Harry's eagerness to move forward.

"Can't you see." Harry's frustration getting the better of him. "She'll have another objection, and another..."

Ron knew that Hermione was the most cautious of the three of them, and after thinking it over. He decided Harry would probably find an excuse to get him out of the way at some point and go ahead without any support. The spell was untried, and dangerous enough. If he couldn't stop his friend, then he should at least do what he could to help.

The two friends headed off to the toilets, and the simmering polyjuice potion awaiting the defining ingredients.

As they walked, Ron fired questions at Harry. It was him that would drink it, and it was his incantation. Harry was a great intuitive wizard, but there were risks to becoming an animagus.

This wasn't the way that anyone else had managed it. Well, at least as far as Ron knew.

Harry explained about the DNA from the three animagus, and his own spell that was tuned to his mind. All he needed to do, was cast the spell, drink the potion and concentrate on the form of animagus that he wanted.

"It's going to be fine, Ron." he assured his friend, and patted his shoulder. "Besides, if it doesn't you'll get a wonderful new pet."

"Harry!!!" Ron howled, truly not in the mood for jokes.

They walked the rest of the way, in silence both lost in their thoughts.

Outside the toilet, Ron blocked Harry with his arm. "Are you sure?" He spoke slowly, and clearly.

"I have to." Harry answered.
12533122221321122.title: They have to go ahead without anything from Jam...

choices: Ron spots a startled looking horse, shifting it's hooves nervously.
The talons of a dragon scratch and scrape the floor agitatedly.
There's a startled looking woman standing there.
125331222213211221.content:
Harry carefully takes one hair from each of his baggies. It's a fiddly job, but he slowly braids them together.

"Almost ready?" Ron asks.

Harry stares intently at the braided hairs. McGonagall, Skeeter, Black.

Despite his planning and preparation, he knew this was the moment. He grunted agreement to Ron, and gave the slightest of nods.

Ron took a sample of the potion, into a test tube and offered it to Harry. He had to steady his hand as he took it. Ron moved the cauldron back out of the way.

"How much space should I clear?" he asked half joking.

"I mean, do you want to be a big dog like Sirius, strong and intimidating. Or something more subtle like Rita's form?"

Harry didn't answer, but he released his pinched fingers, and the hairs dropped into the tube.

A tiny cloud shot up from the tube, and the mixture shifted in colour and consistency. The three samples were not settling well in the brew. It became violently purple, then just as suddenly shocking pink, and again shifted to emerald green.

"Are you ready for your part... are you ready with the incantation." Harry's eyes darted over to Ron an unfamiliar harshness visible in his stare.

Ron nodded, already his lips were moving silently, as he rehearsed the spell in preparation.

Harry needed to lighten the mood, "You know I've always been undernourished, and considered weak. So I thought something with clear strength and power... Something that ... Well, a horse." he looked over to Ron, then winked, "Although I always found Norbert to be an appealing form."

The mix had settled, the violence of it's changes slowing.

"I wonder if an animagus could be a ghost?" chipped in Myrtle as she watched. Her head perched atop her arms as she watched over the top of the cubicle.

Harry saluted the two with the test tube, first Ron, then Myrtle. Before turning back to Ron.

Before he could do anything else, say anything more. He tilted it back, and poured the contents into his mouth. The taste was a vile blend.... Harry swallowed it in two gulps to get rid of the taste. Ron watched as Harry's Adam's apple bobbed twice.

Rivelio Animagus... Rivelio Animagus...

He repeated the words in a steady chant, as Harry fell to his knees. He clutched his throat as his face contorted. His features shifted, as they became feminine. Hints of Rita's cheekbones and expression, then his skin rippled. The cheeks sank inward, and the stern expression of Professor McGonagall was visible.

Harry gritted his teeth, as the pain built. Harry's hair unravelled, forming wild curls around his face. The determination of Sirius was visible. Harry made eye contact with Ron.

As he did, his eyes lost their humanity. They shifted, becoming wide, chestnut and equine. Ron recoiled as they became reptilian, slitted and yellow. Harry shouldn't have joked about Norbert.... Ron really hoped it was a joke.

"Are you Okay?" Ron blurted out between incantations.

There was worry on Harry's face, and an undercurrent of panic and pain.

Harry's skin erupted, and his pores shot out jets of steam, obscuring him from view. Ron backed away as the cloud expanded. Myrtle leaned closer, and glowered at the mist, willing herself to see the shape within. She wasn't in danger. You can't die twice after all.

She was also curious and hopeful that Harry had listened to her suggestion. Maybe, just maybe he was going to have an ethereal animagus form.

A pulsing white glow started in the centre of the cloud. Ron hoped to make out a shape, but that wasn't possible.

The glow brightened and brightened with each pulse, until Ron had to shield his eyes.

There was a blast, and the cloud was blown away. It left only Harry in his new animagus form.
125331222213211221.title: Moaning Myrtle watches with interest.

choices: Harry quickly discovered that he was still a she.
The door slammed open, "I knew it." Hermione stormed in.
Myrtle swoops down and floats around the restored Harry.
1253312222132112211.content:
Harry spots his reflection in the bathroom mirrors above the sinks. Turning on the spot, he takes in his animagus form from different angles, as he dances like a dressage horse in the tight space. Harry's equine face is hard to read, but the joy as he prances around is clear.

Ron is impressed and falls silent taking in quite how large a horse is compared with a student. The next part might be just as difficult, the return to human form.

Even Myrtle is impressed, as she claps her spectral hands. They don't make any noise, but Harry appreciates the effort.

However, her clapping stops suddenly. As Harry sees her reflection, she raises one hand to her face and covers her mouth. But it's too late. She giggles and snorts her joy.

Ron approaches Harry as he finishes his manoeuvres. He has to reach up to stroke Harry's mane. But the transformation is real, his hands confirm it. He allows his hand to run down Harry's back to his wide equine ass as the tail there.

Myrtle finally gets her giggles under control.

"The boy who lived.... The filly who defeated he who must not be named... twice."

Harry nodded his massive horse head. Then stopped as suddenly as Myrtle's silent claps. Lowering his head, he carried out his inspection, dreading what he was going to discover.

His discovery.... Well. Her discovery sent a shiver down her spine. Her mane and tails swished agitatedly back and forth.

"Harry! Harry... What's wrong?" Ron could see something was wrong, but not what it was.

"He's a she...." Myrtle explained with smile.

"Do I need to explain the difference?" she added with a wicked laugh.

Ron's head instinctively bobbed down as he took a glancing look.

Ron and the new filly exchanged a look.

"Well... Well, nobody needs to know you're an animagus." Ron said encouragingly, "You can just change back."

Ron hoped his last words were true. As Harry closed her eyes the concentration was clear. Ron took a few steps backwards.

The horse outline shifted, and rippled. For a few seconds, it pulsated larger and smaller, until it shrank back down to human size.

Harry's hands and feet pressed against the cold floor of the toilets.

"It worked!" Ron jumped up, and punched the air, while Harry stood up straight.
1253312222132112211.title: Ron spots a startled looking horse, shifting it...

choices: Harry slips to the floor as her legs turn to jelly.
Lifting her gaze, Harry... Harriet watches as her features become female.
She shrieks when she feels Ron's hands at her shoulders.
12533122221321122111.content:
Harry gave a curt, "Yes!" and coughed. His voice was higher and sweeter than it should have been.

It didn't take Harry long to see why. Harry's reflection in the mirrors above the sinks was female. His hands roamed about his body, squeezing and groping. Her body... Her body was female, fully and completely. She staggered over towards the mirror, her gait unsteady, she wanted to accuse the mirrors of lying. Harry blamed her glasses and eyesight. But as she made her way, she felt her body move and shift, the weight on her chest unsettling her. It confirmed what she wanted to deny.

"Well, at least you belong in a girls toilet... now." Myrtle added.

Harry swatted at her spectral form as she swooped past her.

She felt the world spin as her breathing became laboured. Her fingers curled around the cold ceramic of the sinks. She felt her trousers shift.

"Ron." she snapped.

She wasn't in the mood for games.

But Ron wasn't near her. It wasn't his touch that Harry felt.

Myrtle flew closer again.

"Hogwarts is very strict about the school uniform." she chuckled.

Harry wasn't sure what the ghost meant, but it was becoming clearer. Harry trousers were retreating up his legs, and they were gaining pleats as they did. It was becoming a... becoming a skirt.

Reaching downwards she grabbed at the hem, but nothing worked, soon she was in a skirt. Her socks had grown longer and extended up her legs, stopping above her knees.

She rolled her shoulders, as she felt her shirt start to shift. The stitching moved around, and fit better around her narrowed shoulders. She gave a startled 'Eeek', as she felt her vest shift, it became a bra. It lifted and supported her new bosom, and stopped it from moving quite so dramatically.

Harry lowered her head over the sink, as she fought for consciousness. She felt queasy, and didn't like the new female look to her face. As she did she felt waves of hair cascade forward, off her shoulders and form a wall around her features.

"Shit. Shit. Shit." she repeated. Her voice sounding more like Hermione's than his own.

He'd have to.... -Shit- She'd have to switch dormitories.

Idly Harry wondered if her hair was now longer than Hermione's.
12533122221321122111.title: Harry quickly discovered that he was still a she.

choices: Harry fails and remains a dragon.
Harry finds out about a dragon's own magical abilities.
Harry returns to human form.
1253312222132112212.content:
Harry's reptilian form was hunched on the floor. As he stood up, there was a metallic plinking, as his talons danced on the cold floor of the toilet. Spreading his arms wide revealed an impressive wing-span, with a thin bat-membrane. Getting unsteadily up, Harry rose to over six feet tall.

About the size that Norbert had been when he'd been whisked away in the night. It was then that Harry wondered if the animagus form grew old... or in his case bigger was more of a concern. Already he could easily gouge the ceiling tiles with his razor sharp claws.

Ron watched from the side, shocked into silence, as Harry stretched and examined his body in the mirrors. Myrtle was also rather quiet too.

"Ron." Harry said.

Or rather failed to say, his throat was long and flexible, but no longer suitable for human speech. He let out a high, shrill squawk that was clearly not birdlike. Harry was startled and tried to clear his throat. The barbed tongue not meant for shaping words.

Coughing a couple of times, his claws rasped over his long neck. Their sharpness matched, by his impenetrable scales.

With the third cough, a gout of yellow flame burst out.

The flames shot out two feet, three feet, and stopped at four feet. As they licked against the wall.

His face wasn't expressive, but his manner became very sheepish.

Ron was losing a little of his nervousness, and a wide smile replacing his uncertainty.

"Awesome, Harry! Simply Awesome."

Harry took another look in the mirror. His scales were scarlet and his spine coated with sharp barbs. His wings were brown leather.

Ron's eyes narrowed when he spotted Hermione step out from behind Dumbledore's billowing clothes. She was open-mouthed as she spotted the well-behaved dragon sitting in the middle of the room. Equally rarely she wasn't rebuking Harry or Ron for their actions.

Dumbledore circled around the animagus, taking him in from all angles. He tilted his head upwards to make eye-contact with Harry, his piercing icy-blue eyes and Harry's emerald serpent eyes. The inner eyelid flicked over Harry's eyes reinforcing their cold inhumanity.

He took that as a sign that it was time, and with Dumbledore there, Harry felt confident of success. He turned to face the mirror, his body moving with a serpentine grace, and reptilian smoothness. He fixed his mind on his original body, and looked deep into his own reflected eyes.

Harry felt himself sinking into their depth, plummeting downwards into their inky darkness. He pictured his own body falling into this abyss, it wasn't something he wanted to imagine. There was a scream. A high human scream, and Harry stumbled backwards.

The room jumped back into focus and the abyssal darkness merely a terrifying memory. Harry staggered as he struggled for balance, taking a few steps forward.

Steadying himself on the ceramic coldness of the sink. Harry gulped the air to the pit of his lungs. His head still span and he threw up into the sink.

He turned to ask Dumbledore a question and had to turn back as bile rose in his throat once more. The water swept it away before it could offend his sense of smell.

"Hmmm... Human again. Well done." Dumbledore said.

Harry felt his friends arrive at his side, Ron on his right, and Hermione at his left shoulder.

Harriet swallowed, in her eagerness to get ahead, she'd resisted Hermione's calls to caution. Between Ron and Hermione, it would always be Ron who was the fun one, and Hermione the more measured.

The three friends exchanged looks, nobody knew that Dumbledore was an animagus. He certainly wasn't registered with the Ministry for any particular form. Each wanted to ask him what he had chosen, but equally none of them was willing to interrupt.

12533122221321123.content:
With a snap of her fingers, Hermione smiled at the boys. But before she could tell them she was interrupted by a tapping at the window. The window was small and round, and outside on the sill, an unfamiliar owl was sitting. The uneven roof meant all of the windows were askew.

"The Owlery's over there." Ron said, shooing it away.

"Wait. Don't be rude." Hermione chided.

"I think it's a message." Harry went to the window, and the owl hopped backwards. Opening the window, the friends could see the owl was quite old and rather dull brown.

He bobbed an approximation of a bow to Harry, and swooped inside. After a tight spiralling flight around the room, it landed atop a mirror.

Tapping one taloned claw, it stretched it's leg for Harry to retrieve the envelope it clutched. On one side Harry Potter was written crisply and cleanly with emerald ink, and the back of the envelope was sealed.

It was sealed with a vile green blob of wax, and when he saw the mark stamped there, he felt a moment of doubt. Perhaps it would be best to rip up the contents. But calming himself, he showed the mark to Ron and Hermione. It was a mix of D.P and R.S. carefully interwoven. The D.P. was stylised identifying it as 'the Daily Prophet', and the three friends knew who R.S. was.

"Vile woman." Hermione muttered.

"Treacherous witch." Ron agreed.

Harry kept his opinion to himself, and gave the wind-swept old owl a silver sickle in thanks. "You can rest in the owlery, if you need... or wish." he said.

Again giving it's bobbing bow, it shot off towards the window, only tucking it's wings and flying through with the slimmest of margins.

He pulled the dust cover off a sofa, and the three friends sat down, one each side of Harry.

Inside was a folded note, and an even smaller envelope. Putting the envelope aside he read the note.

Dear Mr Potter,
I had an idea once we went our separate ways. While I don't agree with your methods, I find them intriguing. A world with more animagi sounds delightfully exciting, and if you can make it possible I hope you remember any small role I played in helping.

He looked up, and they shared a look of understanding with his friends. Rita would always look for her angle.

While I don't have any other animagi hair, I have enclosed this little item from my earlier career. Please open the envelope with care, as boggart hair is both difficult to harvest, and even see. Hopefully this can make a useful contribution to your experiments.

Sincerely,
Rita Skeeter.

"Yes." Hermione said enthusiatically. "Boggarts are shape-shifters... and their changes are made by thought alone..."

She was nodding, as she explained, waiting for Ron to understand, even as a spark of understanding lit up Harry's face.

Inside the envelope was a tiny phial, with a tiny cork stoppering the contents. As Harry held it up, it looked empty. Twisting and turning he examined it from different angles, and it resolutely remained empty.

"It's a trick." snapped Ron, as the thought also entered Harry's head.

But when Harry blocked the Sun with one hand, there was a flicker of something inside... some activity... some movement.

Holding it inches away from his eye, he repeated the gesture. There was a tiny whirl of activity, the sort of motion that Harry recognised from Professor Lupin's class. As the whirling spun down, the phial looked empty once more.

"She wasn't lying." Hermione said, reading the smile spreading across Harry's face.

Carefully he tucked it into his shirt pocket.

"Do you think it'll help?" he asked.

"I'm not sure ..." Hermione answered.

Her reply cut short by Ron, "Do I hear a trip to the library surfacing?" he chuckled.

"Forgive me for not knowing the answer straight away." Hermione answered snippily.

"Sorry, 'Mione. We do appreciate your thoroughness." Harry said smoothing her ruffled feathers.

"Yeah..." Ron added after a slight pause.

"It's different for boys." she said feeling the need to justify herself. "Boys don't have to be studious. They can be sporty, or light-hearted..."

She gestured weakly towards Harry, and half-heartedly to Ron.

"But a girl who isn't academic.... well,a girl, she's a bimbo... or she'll make a great wife one day."

She was cupping her hands suggestively in front of her own bust, and suddenly she dropped her hands as Ron turned more beetroot, than before, and Harry found his shoelaces to be intensely fascinating.

Her gaze darted between the two boys, who were more confused than anything. Feeling she'd said too much, she calmly walked out of the room.

"The library." she said as she went.

Ron and Harry exchanged grimaces behind her back.

- - -

After a few hours in the library, Hermione was frustrated, Ron was confused and bored, and Harry was hopeful.

"It's never been done before." Hermione admitted with a sigh.

"It's never been done before." Harry said with a smile.

"So we still don't know." Ron's shoulders slumped.

Hermione looked across the table at Ron's tired expression.

"Just turn that frown into a sexy pout, and you'll find a wife who can take care of you.... make all the important decisions for you."

"Huh..."

She groaned her frustration, but at least she got a weak smile from Harry.

It was hard for Harry to hear, but it was true. Hermione had the knowledge, the book smarts, and he ... well ... he had an instinctive grasp of some magic. He didn't like to trust it. That felt like cheating, but like his ability to speak parseltongue, he put it down as a 'gift' from Voldemort.

"But we can't lie to him."

"I'm not suggesting lying." Hermione said, her voice getting louder, "He's my friend too." she finished more softly.

Harry had plenty of experience of the infirmary, as they all knew.

Hermione read it, silently, intently, a look of fierce concentration on her face.

Scooping out about a spoonful, it filled the little phial.

Or at least, not Ron. There was a mass of hair around her face, and he felt Hermione's soft hands both clutching his outstretched hand. Widening his stance, he slid his feet further apart for stability.

Ron's voice called from behind him. Feeling a mass of hair fall across his own face, he wondered if he were some kind of long-haired dog, as he swept it aside. But he knew the potion had failed. He felt the brush of a hand, his own hand as he swept it off his face. His scrambling mind, putting together some of the other facts.

He was standing on two feet, he had his hands still. Looking Hermione in the eye, even if he couldn't make out her face, he needed to know. He had to ask.

"That's the sink, Harry." Hermione informed her stiffly.

Harry felt her panic collapse, as she raised a trembling hand to her face. The relief she felt was palpable, she wasn't stuck as some blind witch. She felt stupid for her terror driving her to forget to take off those damned glasses. The glasses that defined him almost as much as that cursed scar.

But with a cold metallic clink, the frames made contact with the sink.

Wiping tears of relief from her face, she spotted an unfamiliar face. It was Hermione, and she looked more lost and confused than Harry had ever seen. But that became worse, as she turned, and spotted another Hermione beside her. This one looking quite furious, her lips pulled tight, her eyes as icy as the chill marble sinks.

"Err... Sorry, Hermione." she said a blush coming to her cheeks.

Over her shoulder, she saw Ron's face dart between the twins at the mirror. His face a stunned mask.

Harry did his best to put 'Professor' Umbridge from his mind, once she was taken by the centaurs deep into the Forbidden Forest.

Her delight in petty and vindictive punishments were an evil he'd rather just put behind him. Although he did like to relive her final words of denial.

With his experience of Dumbledore's Penseive, he had almost perfect recall. He picked out the snapping of her wand, as it was crushed under hoof. Her frantic cries of filthy half-breeds... and pleas... pleas (from her) for Harry's help.

Normally he didn't like to linger on the past. But there was an aroma in the air. Besides the muted light of the forest brought it to mind.

He was helping Hagrid find 'endangered' creatures, and also some of the rarer plants for Professor Sprout. Given the nature of the 'Forbidden' Forest, only Harry and a few others had spent any time in it.

The rumours and stories made it to be a more horrific and scary place than it truly was. A smirk crossed his face, he remembered hearing in his first year, that there were were-wolves in it. He would have been delighted to meet one particular were-wolf again.

"Tha's King's Foil, Harry." Hagrid said over his shoulder. "It's a weed."

Fang was peeing against a nearby tree trunk, as Harry got to his feet.

There was a cough, and Firenze stepped out from behind a tree himself.

"You know Hagrid, Pomona might still find a use for it." he chided gently.

He turned his elegant head, "Harry." he added with a nod.

"Firenze." the joy was clear in his voice.

"At least, you're not in mortal peril this time."

Turning back to Hagrid, "I do have a favour to ask. One of our herd is... she's... eager to be away."

The half-giant looked a little confused, the forest was one of the last places with centaurs that was safe.

"It's fairly mutual." Firenze added in a quieter voice.

He gestured someone forward, and from further into the darkness. Harry and Hagrid saw a centaur ...well... a centauress step out. As she approached, she left the darkness and passed into a more dappled light.

Harry had an idea who it was.

But he could only be sure once she was nearer.

She had a beautiful palomino body, a white coat, and salmon coloured tail. Cascading down her human back was a salmon mane.

She was wearing a very uncomfortable, disgusted look on her face, as if she were truly unhappy with everything.

It was Dolores Umbridge.

Harry hid his smile with a gesture, as he pushed his unkempt hair off his face.

"Harry." she said in a curt voice. It was only five letters, but she filled them with every emotion she could.

"Welllll, we can't have her being unhappy." said Hagrid with his usual bluff honesty.

"Can we please head back to the castle, now." she asked. The strain clear as she looked over her shoulder at her equine half.

"Yeah... I think we've done enough today, 'Arry." the half giant waved Firenze good-bye and started lumbering back towards the castle.

He gave a deafening whistle, "Fang. Come!"

The four of them headed back to Hogwarts, a tense silence between them.

Harry felt his chest expand out. Looking down at Hannah he saw that she was shrinking in on herself.

Her arms shrank into her torso. The sleeves of her blouse hanging limply as the blonde girl no longer had arms.

Soon Hannah Abbott stood wearing a male Gryffindor uniform, looking at year four potion books intermingled a female Huffelpuff uniform.

12533122222222222222222.title: Sybill Trelawney

choices: Sybill goes to her books to confirm what she read there.
She has the three students wait behind, as the rest troop out.
"Please, what did you see." Harry asked, sincerely.
125331222222222222222221.content:
Sybill Trelawney always hated the Gryffindor class. Well, that's not true, she just found this one particularly stessful. She spasmed as her gaze passed by three of them. They were perfectly nice, but the girl... Well, the girl, just didn't believe in magic, and at wizarding school. She put that from her mind.

"Now, that we're all here, it's time for more divination. You've already had your breakfast, and I hope you all remembered to bring you cup."

There was a sigh from the girl. 'Hermione' she thought her name was.

The boys were easier to identify, that coppery hair was a typical Weasley. Both in his dedication to mischief, and lack of attention. The other one, well, everyone knew 'The Boy Who Lived', the scar on his forehead, announced his name before he had the chance to say his name.

There was a round a round of chimes, as the students dutifully put their crockery on the tables. As Gryffindors, they were generally hard-working and honest. But there were always exceptions.

Picking a table at random, she headed to the opposite side of the class. She had to restrain herself from blurting out what she saw, instead asking the students to interpret the remaining tea leaves in the bottom of the cup.

It went well, as the students stumbled out their answers, doubts vanished beneath her encouragement. Soon they were more confidently expanding on what they could see.

Sybill turned, and her heart sank, as she saw who was next... who was last.

Hermione introduced herself, and even offered an apology for her behaviour from a few years ago for storming out the class. She took and clapped Hermione's hand in consolation, she wasn't going to hold a grudge.

This was why she liked Gryffindor students, although they made mistakes, they would apologise for any harm caused.

Unfortunately, the girl wasn't blessed with the sight, and struggled. Sadly, comfortingly, she shook her head, and told her what was visible to her eyes. It was good. Sybill was glad to see she had a bright future, and her powers and talent lead her onto a great position of power and responsibilty. There was one thing she didn't want to mention,... until it was confirmed.

Hermione took the news with grace, and let Sybill move on to Ron's cup. Ron Weasley was a good wizard, or rather could be with work. His second sight was a little hazy, and he added in a couple of guesses to complete what he had seen. She corrected him, and congratulated him on his improvement.

As she passed the cup back to him, she watched intently. She wasn't aware she was nodding, but her smile was clear. It was confirmed, even without the tea-leaves, the look exchanged between Ron and Hermione confirmed what she had seen.

Time was running out for her class, and there was a general murmur building as they became restless. She wasn't sure what she expected to see. Perhaps a boy with Harry's history, all the major events in his life would be behind him. She wasn't even sure of how powerful a wizard he was.

She let him speak, talk her through his reading. He saw change in the future, both wanted and unwanted. When he mentioned anonymity, Sybill was instantly skeptical. She took her first proper look at his tea-leaves. It sounded suspiciously vague and like wish-fulfilment.

Peering deep into Harry's cup, they bumped foreheads. Harry apologised, fell silent and backed away.

Sybill saw change and transformation, and anonmity, too. But she saw more... It couldn't be true. She lifted her gaze and scowled at the three of them. She didn't like mockery, seeing the future was always a difficult skill, and she was used to disbelief, and even being called a liar.

"Girl... -hm- Boy, I won't be mocked in my own class. You've set this up." Harry looked blankly back at her, incomprehension clear on his face.

"And you helped." she snapped, pointing to Ron.

"You probably put them up to it." she said to Hermione.

Ron and Hermione were flabbergasted. They started flapping and denying any plotting or mocking.

Harry asked her what she'd seen.

The class ended before she could answer.
125331222222222222222221.title: Sybill Trelawney

choices: Sybill explains everything she can see.
Ron leans forward, and asks if Harry will be a hottie.
"But, how... why..." Harry asks, as questions fill him.
1253312222222222222222212.content:
The class ended, and Prof. Trelawney allowed the students to stream out of her class. There were a few masked yawns, as the students left.

But Ron, Harry and Hermione were not among them. With a subtle gesture, the three friends remained seated as the others left. Hermione pretended to rummage through her bag. While Ron on seeing Hermione's ruse, scribbled jibberish in his notes, to look busy.

Harry watched, and allowed events to unfold. He kept his mug on the table. As folk left, he took glances deep inside his mug. But whatever, the teacher was talking about it was too subtle ... or too imaginary for Harry to perceive.

Most students were dismissive of Sybill's abilities as a seer. But Harry was willing to give her a lot more leeway, after the warning he had witnessed. The complete change in her manner, and speech as she delivered her prophecy.

The solid thunk, as the bolt slid into place told the friends, that they were alone.

Sybill was never an intimidating figure, but the purposeful way she strode across the room made them sit back, just a little.

She drew her wand and with a hiss asked her question.

"One chance... and one alone. Is this a prank?"

Her body was rigid, and her tone severe, despite her scruffy clothes and appearance she was doing a startling impression of Professor Snape.

The tip of her wand flickered and sparked with an angry energy.

"No! It's no joke! What's a joke? What did you see?"

The fact that it was Ron who spoke gave it more weight. As she took in his words, the magic dimmed and the light went out.

Like a puppet with it's strings cut, Sybill dropped to the floor. She barely managed to stay upright as she landed on a stool. She returned to herself.

She steadied her hands, and took up Harry's cup. She peered deep inside, staring intently, with a focus that looked out of place. She'd shake her head, and mutter. But then she'd scowl and look back at the pattern in the base of cup.

"When we see a transformation in a reading, it can come in many forms. Not merely a physical one, it can refer to a change in career, or family." Sybill spoke clearly, and forcefully.

"However, in this case... Harry you will face a significant change and difficult challenges ahead. You shall experience life on the distaff side."

"Distaff side?" Ron asked. Clearly having stopped from just grunting his incomprehension.

Hermione understood, and her cheeks took on a little colour.

"She's saying you're going to become female."

"Didn't I say that." the professor sniffed.
1253312222222222222222212.title: She has the three students wait behind, as the ...

choices: "Can you see her... in the future?" Ron asked, pointing to a mirror.
"When? How long.. until... and for?" Harry asked his voice cracking.
"It'll be fine." Hermione huffed, "Being female isn't a burden."
12533122222222222222222122.content:
Ron grunted as Hermione's elbow landed in his stomach. But the professor was oblivious to that particular action.

"Oh, yes... Well, I imagine so." she answered distractedly, "Well, you see, the form comes from the vict... from the person's thoughts."

She shrugged, "So if you can imagine an attractive woman, Harry, you can be her." she chuckled, "And I'm sure at your age, you don't need much encouragement."

Harry turned pink, as his imagination filled in every random, lewd thought he'd ever had. Every fantasy, every perfect woman, every enhanced version of anyone he knew. Momentarily, he couldn't even look at Hermione, even Ron brought up memories of Ginny, a perfected Ginny, with a fuller bust, wider hips, a tighter waist, a whiter smile.

Shaking his head, he tried to dismiss the thought. It was easier than he'd thought, just imagining those full breasts being his.... hers...

The confusion was unsettling.

As Hermione tried to comfort Harry, she was intensely aware of him not meeting her eyes, and suspected the reason. Ron however, was regaining his breath.
12533122222222222222222122.title: Ron leans forward, and asks if Harry will be a ...

choices: "Happy, now?" she asks snappily, an insecurity making her harsh.
"Is that? Will that..?" Harry babbles turning to Professor Trelawney.
Shaking his head in denial, Harry backs onto Sybil's feet.
125331222222222222222221221.content:
Harry's thoughts scattered like startled pigeons. His mouth instantly dry, he tried to dismiss his fantasies of Ginny. It was the last thing he'd want Ron to see in the mirror.

The mirror hung alone on the wall, it looked poorly made, the picture foggy and imprecise. It was only if you looked closer that you could see it was truly wispy and imprecise, and not a defect. Harry couldn't bring himself to answer. As fascination battled fear. As if saying 'yes', saying 'show me', would make it real, make it happen. His lips were dry and stuck together, but he managed a weak nod of his head.

Hermione's expression tightened, disapproval bubbling beneath the surface. Judgement looming for the boys' curiosity. But like Harry she wanted to know.

The three friends stood in front of the mirror, Hermione and Ron flanking Harry, Professor Trelawney behind them. Her eyes immense behind those glasses, her chin resting on Harry's shoulder.

Magic crackled from her fingers, as she swept the three of them into one embrace.

Harry's brow furrowed, as an imprecise face started to appear in the mist of the mirror. He felt relief, that even now, he could tell it wasn't a red-head. As the face drew closer it became more detailed. The familiar tawny mess of Harry's mop of hair, before moments later, cascading downwards. A woman's hair clear in the image, Harry swallowed, it was unsettling. He felt Hermione take his hand, give it a comforting squeeze.

Though as she squeezed, the mirror face rippled and rolled. A very attractive woman looked out at them.

"Bloomin' Heck..." Ron's words cut short as he caught himself.

The future Harry was indeed -er- 'womanly'.

https://78.media.tumblr.com/bb800943564e...

"I'm mean, she's... you're... you will make an attractive woman...." Ron's words trailed off weakly. The blushing Ron too obviously thinking the same things as Harry.

"Jeez, she's got big tits." the conclusion as cold as blunt and undeniable as Harry's future rack.

Hermione elbows Harry, and the mirror shimmers back to a fog, the face vanishing.
125331222222222222222221221.title: "Can you see her... in the future?" Ron asked.

choices: Hermione comes back to herself, and hugging Harry apologises.
"Wh... When... do we know when?" Harry stuttered out, uncertainly.
"Is it fixed?" Ron's question startling Sybil to peer in his direction.
1253312222222222222222212211.content:
Rubbing his ribs, he looked hurt, it wasn't his fault Ron's distraction. He was unsettled to see her future self too. Hermione was still angry, as she continued on, "Now. Now... you'll really have to study." she added.

Harry's mouth opened and closed silently not sure what to say, not sure what she meant.

With a snort of derision Hermione explained. "If you think people only see that scar rather than you, then you've never had big tits... have you?"

Harry flushed, hearing Hermione say 'big tits', made him feel insecure. The desire to cover his flat male chest surging in priority.

She continued her tirade, "Big tits are all some people see." the merest glance passing in Ron's direction, before moving on. Ron was being spectacularly small, quiet and allowing his friends as much chance to talk without him, as possible.

"Harry Potter. You are lazy at subjects you don't like, but you have an incredible sense of intuition when it comes to magic."

Some emotion flashed across Hermione's face, a hint of jealousy, pride, insecurity, her bookishness admitting a deeper knowledge.

"But, in the future, instead of the Boy who lived, you might be the Bimbo who lived."

Harry squirmed, he already felt guilty about relying on copying Hermione's work so often. He was just as disturbed by his fantasy future form. His own imagination crafting a prison of female flesh that he'd hate to inhabit. Admire, yes. But from the outside. He was still trying to find something that he could live with.

"I'm not a bimbo." he snapped back. "I'll not be a bimbo." he added.

Hermione smiled, "Good, now try saying it without pouting, and looking like you're about to stamp your foot in frustration."

Harry's squeak of horror, was answer enough for her. His imagination already telling him, how petulant he sounded, how insignificant, how cute and girly.

He clung to the last vanishing image from the mirror, at least he'd keep his lack of style. His future form didn't look to be a girly-girl. The figure pirouetting into the mist had been wearing jeans.

https://78.media.tumblr.com/ba2ab8a961dc...

The jeans and boots reminding him more of his Quidditch uniform.

choices: Hermione and Ron came through with her.
The mist faded away, and reality formed around the curvy Harry Potter.
Whirling around, Harry only found more fog behind her.
12533122222222222222222122111.content:
Her cheeks reddened at her insecurity, she was meant to be offering comfort, not retelling her nightmares as a prediction for Harry. With an effort she swallowed her words. She reached out tenderly to give him a hug. She spared a glance, looking for Ron's equally embarrassed face. With darting eyes she encouraged him to hug Harry from the other side.

But, Ron wasn't really a hugger. He gave an awkward shrug and patted Harry's shoulder.

Harry was only distantly aware of his friends, and their efforts to bring him peace. He was staring intently at the mirror as if trying to call back the image. His hand reached out for the surface of the mirror. Behind the three students, Sybil Trelawney was cleaning her glasses with a corner of one scarf. She looked incredibly satisfied at the vision. Even if it wasn't quite what she'd expected, the students had seen it, too.

As she straightened the glasses back onto her face, her sight leaped back into focus. She spotted Harry's hand millimeters from the glass. Her cry of 'No', quickly echoed by Hermione. However, it was already too late, startled by the noise. Harry's hand touched the mirror's surface. An instant chill raced up his arm freezing it instantly in place.

Lurching forward, Harry came back to himself. Or rather herself, as she straightened up, her lurch explained by the sudden massive bust that had pulled her forward. "Shit." the words further undermining her confidence in their tone. The fog before her eyes, more real, and deeper she was truly in its' midst.
12533122222222222222222122111.title: Hermione comes back to herself, and hugging Harry.

choices: You find new memories surfacing, the... encounter coming back to you.
Copying Hermione, you release your wand, and punch him on the nose.
"Jealous?", you shoot back, "Or do I still intimidate you?"
125331222222222222222221221112.content:
The grey clouds retreated, revealing the stone walls of Hogwarts. Experimentally reaching a hand out Harry touched the wall, ensuring it was real. The same hand snapped back from the chill stone, impacting her softer, but just as real bust.

It felt real, more real than the Pensieve or Tom Riddle's diary. But he wasn't ready to surrender his gender quite yet.

However, that didn't stop her spinning around to see if Sybil's class was behind her. Only the length of the corridor behind, and no trace of her former reality. She shuddered, hugging herself, a feeling of being lost bone deep and terrifying. Even the hug served as a reminder of her own rather large bust, and her questing hands reluctantly confirming she was fully female.

"Learnt your lesson yet." The familiar voice sneered from behind her.

Harry whirled around to face Draco, ready to draw her wand and defend herself. But her defiance was under cut, but the delayed shimmying shift of her bust catching up. The movement drawing Draco's eyes from Harry's face.

"So you caught, the blast intended for that mudblood, Granger."

Harry's grip tightened on his wand but it remained sheathed. She hoped that Draco would reveal more, and she didn't have to show her ignorance.

"Who knew the 'Big Jugs Jinx', would affect a wizard too..." his tone now amused. His leering gaze now open, his eyes searching for her nipples through her top. Harry, just, resisted the urge to slap her arms across her chest.

"Perhaps you did... Harriet... perhaps you wanted this..."

Draco's hand drifted, gesturing from head to camel toe, the familiar sneer back on his ferret face.
125331222222222222222221221112.title: The mist faded away, and reality formed around ...

choices: An awkward breakfast for Draco.
A late night girly chat. Harry opens up to Hermione.
Harry hears a Hermione voice from the empty air, while Hermione slept.
1253312222222222222222212211122.content:
Feeling Draco's nose crumple beneath his small fist filled Harry with great pleasure. Oddly, doing it as a witch added to her pleasure. Draco however felt no pleasure, as he stumbled backwards. A trail of blood already spilling from his nose, his windmilling arms crashing against a suit of armour in an alcove before falling to the stone floor.

The steady pace Harry took towards him, had him scrambling backwards. But, belatedly realising his lost dignity, he leaped to his feet. A snarl on his face, he turned and raced away down the corridor. Already Harry imagined him making up stories and excuses for another bloody nose. But she was more satisfied with the spell he'd cast at the retreating figure.

A little time on the distaff side might help him understand things. A spell he'd invented on the spur of the moment. She wasn't sure it would work, but the time delay on the spell would stop Harry from being blamed. As his sense of triumph faded, his potential success felt muted. A trip to the library would pass the time, and hopefully find Hermione, and maybe, just maybe an explanation about that damned mirror, and a way for him to return to normal.

Her arrival in the library confirmed one thing, Hermione was very reliable and very, very studious. The meeting was a little awkward, as guilt flashed across Hermione's face as she once more took in Harry's body. A hiss of sympathy coming from her as she looked over the generous bust she'd caught on her behalf. Harry couldn't bring herself to bring up that she was a stranger here.

- - - - -

Harry found the girls side of the dormitory a little disconcerting the familiar jokes of Seamus, Dean, Neville and of course Ron. Not having lived through the times that ended up with her in this reality, she struggled. Every moment expecting a scream of outrage and an outstretched figure pointing her out as a pervert.

Her dreams did nothing to calm her, as Ron appeared in her dreams. The dream Ron comforting Ginny grieving the loss of her boyfriend. Harry woke with a start as Ron promised to fill in for his sister. Her sheets were soaked with sweat, as she sat bolt upright in bed. The cold she felt nothing to do with the draughty castle or her thin nightgown.

- - - - -

Had she known she'd have drawn some comfort as her spell went to work on Draco. The snide Slytherin was being transformed by the Big Jugs Jinx. Although it would only become obvious to her on waking.

Draco's scream filled every inch of the Slytherin dorms as she woke to the horror of being a witch. Her bust far too generous to bind down or hide. Hell even her arm snaking to hide it only made it look bigger.

https://78.media.tumblr.com/7bbb9051dbfe...

The magic also ensuring her clothes shifted to match her new gender. Her breath heaving in deep rasping gasps. That only lasted until she saw her dorm mates enjoying the sight of her heaving bosom. Crabbe's eyes fixed leering on every movement. Goyle hiding a smile, as she saw the horror in Draco's eyes. Or at least the assumed Draco, after all she was in his bed. Draco, as a Slytherin, would be expected to deal with this herself.

She didn't need proof, Draco knew... just knew that Harry.... that Bitch Harry was behind this latest humiliation.
1253312222222222222222212211122.title: Copying Hermione, you release your wand, and pu...

choices: He sends the transformed former wizards to Beauxbatons to help them adjust.
Around the hall a variety of wands flare, before crackling and breaking apart.
Harry realises to avert disaster he needs to return through that mirror fog.
12533122222222222222222122111221.content:
There were whoops and cheers, as Draco entered the Grand Hall. Her shoulders hunched and her head low, but it was impossible for her to hide her transformation. Especially as she was flanked by Crabbe and Goyle, both of the side-kicks stealing glances at their friend. Frankly, it was hard for them not to. Their eyes drawn to the constant movement of her bust.

The problem was Draco's offence at wearing a bra. Ripping it angrily off, as if it were what transformed her or marked her as a witch. It was only pride that stopped her returning to the Slytherin common room after she found out how mobile the girls were, how eye-catching as she went down stairs.

Draco's cheeks burning, she kept her head so low it was almost buried in her breakfast.

- - - - -

Alas, after that day a covert, civil war erupted between Gryffindor and Slytherin. With ambushes, and mass jinxings, the school coming to resemble more a busty Beauxbatons. Some bystanders were caught in the cross-fire. Though Cho doubted the jinx she caught was quite so accidental. Her relationship with Harry was too well known.

https://78.media.tumblr.com/b15d8c2db490...

She sobbed in her Ravenclaw dorm, as she looked over the damage the spell had inflicted on her body.

- - - - -

Lavender Brown was caught in the open, and stung twice by the spell. Her hair paled as she writhed under it's influence, a disturbing thought flashing briefly through her brain. Would this make Ron look at her again?

https://78.media.tumblr.com/111fc8ecd36a...

As she burst from her uniform, the barely there underwear stretched but held. Well, mostly. The surge of hormones flooding through her veins, making her cheeks flush, as her imagination fired, and her arousal spiked. She raced for some privacy to relieve her mounting tensions.

- - - - -

By the end of the week, tragedy struck. Oddly, annoying, paining Harry more than his own transformation. Luna Lovegood was jinxed as she tried to intervene brokering peace. The spell knocking her unconscious, the two sides split and left her to eventually be taken to the infirmary. She woke with her her usual airy disposition. But from a seat by her bedside, Harry saw pain in her eyes as she took in her new bust.

https://78.media.tumblr.com/847a9cbd6a2e...

Even within the two dorms, there were now divisions forming. The transfigured wondering how this had escalated so quickly, the former wizards reacting differently than those born witches. Fractures were clear, and painful as many relationships were destroyed.

- - - - -

As the week-end started a special assembly was called for the entire school. Dumbledore scowled down from his lectern. His gaze passing over all the students. Behind him, only two of the four cylinders had any points in them. No trace of Gryffindor red, nor the least trace of green showed for Slytherin.

Dumbledore's lips moved as he started chanting a spell. One by one, the other teachers joined in, spreading out wider and wider until the whole head table was repeating the same spell. Harry crossed her fingers hoping that this would end the fight, that this would restore everyone.

With a brilliant flash or palest blue, a sphere spread out from Dumbledore's position, spreading and disappearing through the walls. Until the entire castle felt it's effects. A few girls exchanged glances, as if they too were thinking the same as Harry. But nobody lost their new genders, or their unwanted 'enhancements'.

Stiffly, Dumbledore announced that the Big Jugs Jinx would no longer work anywhere on the castle grounds. There would be no further casualties to this spell. That this was no mere prank, and shouldn't be indulged as a joke. His eyes fiery, as he glared at those he suspected/knew to be guilty of casting the spell. There was a pain in his voice, an ache as he continued speaking. The cure for the Big Jugs Jinx hadn't been found or made, and that those affected should expect a long wait. A couple of girls fainted, and many sobbed, as the news sank in.
12533122222222222222222122111221.title: An awkward breakfast for Draco.

125331222222222222222222221212.content:
Harry was at the Three Broomsticks drinking the night away. Umbridge had driven him crazy with constant detentions, and he needed to get away.

However.

What was not known about the Three Broomsticks was that it was a Ministry-owned establishment used to “enforce” certain standards around Hogsmeade. And that the barmaid Rosmerta was actually just the next in a long line of Ministry controlled drones whose job was to spy on patrons to monitor wizarding folk. It didn’t matter if they were male or female before hand. The Ministry would use the protocol to make “troublesome” citizens vanish while perpetuating the identity of the vivacious barmaid Madam Rosmerta.

Sipping back another butterbeer, Harry asked for another one.

“Sure thing, dear,” was the practiced response. But, unknown to both, the protocol for a new Rosmerta was in effect. Slipping the potion into the mug with his drink, Rosmerta served him. “It’s special blend. On the house,” she said with a wink.

Harry simply returned the smile, and sipped the special blend.

Harry saw Millicent Bullstrode was walking this way, on her own.
Daphne Greengrass was on her own in a nearby potions' class.
125331222222222222222222222.content:
Harry was sitting across the library table from Ron and Hermione. He was still unsettled by his visions of the Dark Lord, and the distance that the Headmaster was keeping from him. It was a small act, but he ensured that Hermione and Ron sat together. It made Hermione happy, and perhaps Ron study a little more carefully.

A bolt of pink magical energy crackled and flew just beyond his shoulder. It hit Hermione, and rippled over her skin, lighting up her flesh. It roiled over her face, and her hands, sending out an angry spark, as Ron tried to get closer to her. As the sparkling, arcing magic focused, it moved towards Hermione's torso.

At the same moment, Harry's seat went flying crashing backwards from the table to crack loudly against the heavy oak shelves. He peered through the gap just above the books, where the spell had shot. But he only saw a retreating figure. He raced after it, reaching the main passageway, as the library door thudded shut.

Impotently, belatedly cries, of 'Don't Run.', echoed after the culprit. Harry still hadn't caught a clear look. But he charged through the door. Eyes flashing wildly from the doorway, he spotted a figure as she disappeared into an archway. The length of her dark hair, and the flapping of her skirt marking her as a witch. The beading on her jumper green and grey marking her as a Slytherin.

Harry raced after her. But the stairways were against him, grinding their stony dance at a painfully slow pace. When Harry reached the archway, there wasn't a trace of the witch. His wand glowed with angry power, in his tightening fist. But unable to do more, he returned to the library. He might be able to help Hermione, whatever that spell had done to her.

The library door was kicked open, almost smashing Harry's nose in it's violence. But his anger drained away, as he saw Ron half-lifting, half-hiding Hermione from the library. Tears of humiliation flowing freely, but silently, her cheeks were crimson with suppressed rage.

"Infirmary." Ron hissed impatiently.

Hermione merely added a sorrowful nod of agreement.

Her distress was obvious. Her blouse was ripped wide every button gone. Only her jumper was stretched to it's limit. Hermione was burdened with two of the largest breasts he'd ever seen. Trying not to stare, he took Hermione's right hand side, to protect her from casual glances as much as possible. Her chest was more mobile, too. Obviously her bra was as destroyed as the blouse. Her jumper clung tautly to her exaggerated figure, even outlining her full nipples, each as ripe and firm as strawberry, the hint of their succulent pinkness teasing through the stretched wool of her jumper.

It was the longest walk they'd ever taken to the infirmary. One of the few, when Harry wasn't the patient, too. Harry drew his usual degree of attention as they went. Glances quickly shifting from him to Hermione, then just as quickly away again, or in some cases staring in disbelief.

"Madame Pomphrey." Ron called out loudly, once they reached the infirmary.

Harry was fuming, he'd decided not to bring the Marauder's Map, or his invisibility cloak. It was only meant to be a simply library visit. But both would have been immensely helpful. For identifying the attacker, and for getting Hermione here, more discretely and with more of her dignity intact.

He was frustrated once again, by the fact it clearly wasn't Draco behind this.

Without waiting to be invited, Hermione slipped into a bed. Not even bothering to undress, the thick blankets helped disguise the extent of her harm. Finally masking her proud nips from accidental expose.

Poppy gasped, as she saw Hermione, quickly shooing, the boys away, she pulled the curtains around the bed.

Harry snorted as Professor McGonagall hurried into the infirmary. Obviously sent by the Headmaster, who probably also knew what had happened, and who'd done it. Harry found himself grinding his teeth, and with an effort relaxed his jaw.

There was a hurried, whispered exchange beyond the curtain. Before Professor McGonagall came out, to question the two of them.

"Will she be alright?" Ron snapped impatiently.

Minerva's left eyebrow shot up in surprise. But she mastered herself, recognising the pain that caused his concern.

1253321.content:
Harry flicked through the potion book. After finding out who the 'Half Blood Prince' was, he felt more confident in using the book. It would be a waste not to use it, especially given the proficiency of the man who added all the notes in the margins.

There was even a list of ingredients given at the end of the chapter on transformation. A chapter that Harry had studied rather closely after the experiment with polyjuice and their time as Crabbe and Goyle....

This one was simpler to prepare, and promised to work it's magic on the subject's clothes as well as their body.

Harry had just read 'The Lord of the Rings', after his misadventures during the incident with the Goblet, and partly as a reference to that, he'd asked her for a lock of her hair as a memento.

He hadn't planned anything beyond that, and intended is simply as a joke.

But his fingers trembled as he held a single hair above the test-tube.

Even as he was deciding whether or not to go ahead, it slipped from his fingers, and dropped into the waiting potion. A small puff of white told Harry the ingredients had already mixed.

Harry wondered if the clothes would be based on what she'd been wearing when she'd cut the lock from her head, or maybe what she was wearing when he drank the potion. In either case, it was certain his clothes would transform, as she certainly wouldn't be wearing the boys Gryffindor uniform.

Before he worried himself into not taking the potion. He raised it to his lips, and quickly swallowed the contents. It tasted of freshly picked mint, and as Harry felt it go to work, he told himself that one way or another it would be over in an hour, and he'd be back to himself.

His muscles started to twich and spasm as his body changed. Falling backwards, he drummed his feet on the toilet floor, as Myrtle floated above him, circling slowly, watching curiously.

The world faded to black, as the potion went to work.

When Harry woke, he... she punched the air with joy. Her arm much skinnier and covered in the pale powder blue of Beauxbatons. As she sat up, she felt an unusual weight on her head. Instinctively her hand went up and discovered the loose tendrils poking out from beneath her cap.

http://img4.wikia.nocookie.net/__cb20131...

"Harry wants to be a girl..." Myrtle circled above the smiling student. She started to sing her words, as she giggled at the flustered Harry.

He tried to hush her, he didn't want this spreading around the school. The thought of Malfoy hearing send a shudder down her spine. But if he could keep her occupied for an hour, then it would just be another bizarre Harry Potter story... and hopefully dismissed.

Moaning Myrtle isn't the most fun person to talk too. But she eventually settled down, when Harry didn't rise to the bait. She hover close, as Harry scribbled away his deductions.

"Did you know Fleur?" Harry asked the ghost, a whisp of a French accent coming through.

Harry didn't wait for an answer. Instead shrugging away his own question. This was a bathroom, so there were plenty of mirrors. It was odd, to look at your reflection and have a stranger look back. His body is incredibly attractive, and Harry starts to pose in front of the mirror. Making pouty, sexual faces..., glancing backwards over one shoulder at her reflection. She takes in her ass, and gives an impersonal nod of approval.

Myrtle is back to her curious, quizzical expression. When she feels the weight of Harry's gaze she asks a question.

"So ... This is a more advanced polyjuice. Er... It did transform your clothes too, didn't it?"

"Oui, c'est vrai."

One eyebrow quirked upwards on Myrtle's face, but she continued.

"She's very pretty."

Harry blushed, "Oui, Je suis vraiment magnifique."

Harry turned back to the mirrors, and peered closer. She couldn't spot a single flaw in the copy. Her face was perfect. An idle thought wandered through. It was a shame there were no cosmetics to enhance her beauty.

At the same time, Harry found himself becoming aroused, looking so intently at a beautiful woman.

Harry was unfamiliar with the body, but she felt restless, her hands eager to be busy. Her body was becoming aroused too.

The reflection winked back encouragingly at Harry. Had he done that... He wasn't sure.... He must have... mustn't he?

125332111.content:
Harry was frightened by the mirror winking back at him. Surely it had to been him, to even consider the alternatives would leave him in a very bad position. It didn't end with a wink. Blown kisses, modelling poses, and a reveal of cleavage. Harry realized to late the mirror was goading him, appealing to Fleur's Veela side."Je ne serai pas en reste par ma réflexion!" Harry/Fleur said in a huff, trying to outdo the mirror in sex appeal. Myrtle considered leaving to find help for Harry, but no, she was enjoying the show. The boy had messed around and now was suffering the consequences.

Eventually the show stopped, and Harry/Fleur looked about, dazed and confused. "Are you alright Harry?" Myrtle asked, only to be given a utterly confused look by Fleur. "Harry? Je ne suis pas Harry, je suis Fleur Delacour, fierté de Beauxbatons! Comment pourriez-vous penser peut-être que je suis Harry?" Fleur said, in rapid fire perfect French. The ghost grew worried now, Harry was adamant he was Fleur. "You naughty boy Harry. I suppose Hermione will have to clean up this mess for you. You understand English well enough, just stay here."

Hermione knew about Harry's experiments with polyjuice, after all it was a great tool if you were smart with it. Barty Crouch Jr passed himself off as Alastor Moody for almost a year, with only Dumbledore having suspicions. What she couldn't expect, or rather what she should have guessed knowing how much of an idiot Harry was, was that he would experiment with non humans. Fleur's Veela heritage could cause serious side effects, and according to Moaning Myrtle, he honestly thought he was Fleur. Best keep this secret from Ron right now.

When she came to the bathroom, she found Harry/Fleur had in fact not stayed behind. She had returned now, but was carefully applying a coat of pink lipstick. "Myrtle I hate to do this, but can you make a distraction? Madame Pomfrey and Dumbledore need to know, and I can't risk anyone else finding out. If not for me, do it for Harry. I'll... I'll get him to invite you on a date when he's back to normal."

Myrtle considered her options. She wasn't a fan of Harry's new look, and the idea of a date with Harry was enticing. Nodding with a smirk, she began screaming as loud as she could, causing issues for Hogwart's plumbing yet again. Covertly taking Harry/Fleur to the infirmary, she explained the basics to Madame Pomfrey, who tutted at Harry, much to the blonde's annoyance, and explained to Dumbledore when he arrived.

"I am afraid there isn't much we can do. Much research on polyjuice tends to be underground, hidden knowledge used for nefarious purposes. Our best option is to have Professor Snape look through Knockturn Alley, and hope Harry comes to his senses, or the transformation ends."
