1253321112.content:
An hour since Harry changed went by, and he showed no signs of turning back to normal. In fact as Fleur he was furious that everyone thought he was Harry. SHE was Fleur Delacour, and she wasn't going to sit idly by when people seemed to insult her by comparing her to Harry Potter. Sure the boy was a hero, but he was scrawny, nosy, and not to attractive in her mind. In Madame Pomfrey's office, there was a discussion going on about what to do with Harry/Fleur. Snape, Dumbledore, Madame Pomfrey, Hermione, and an uninvited Myrtle discussed what the best option was.

"The boy foolishly rushed ahead with his potion. I should have an antidote ready by next month, Mr Potter is very lucky indeed I had the ingredients on hand, let us shock him into reality, hide him away for some time then use the antidote." Snape said, unhappy to be here and have to yet again deal with a mess caused by James Potter's son. "If he doesn't come to his senses then hide him away still, somewhere secure and wait for the antidote."

Madame Pomfrey very much disagreed. She along with many staff did not have a high opinion of Severus Snape, and she dismissed his idea right away. "Absolutely not Professor Snape. We can wait for the antidote, but trying to convince Mr Potter of who he is could have dire consequences on his mind. Furthermore this year is absolutely crucial to any student of any magical school!"

Hermione wasn't eager to speak, especially in front of many teachers, but she had been invited by Dumbledore, so she said her piece. "I agree with Madam Pomfrey. Harry can't miss this year, across the world 6th year and equivalents are very important. Many useful spells for everyday life are complex, and reserved to the final two years. I'm no Doctor, I can't say what will happen if Harry wakes up and realizes he's stuck in Fleur's body, but I doubt it could be any good. I... would be willing to research Polyjuice potion issues if someone can issue me the proper authorization."

"I don't care how you do it, but I'm getting that date with Harry eventually. Until then, I'll be waiting." Myrtle said gruffly, very upset by the issue at hand. She had wanted Harry to suffer something to show him what he was doing was foolish, but this was going to far. She wasn't into women at all!"

"I don't see any way around this. I'm afraid you are outvoted Severus, 3 to 1. 4 if you count Myrtle among us. Harry can absolutely not miss his school year, even if he is trapped in the body of Fleur Delacour. Please continue with the antidote Severus, and hopefully we can bring Harry back to normal. Every day we leave him in that body runs the risk of the change being permanent. I must inform Madame Maxine of the situation and discuss the situation. I am loath to do this, but I need unbreakable vows from all of you, even you Myrtle. As far as anyone is concerned that is not not us or Madame Maxine, that is Fleur Delacour in the bed."

Everyone held out their hands, even Myrtle, who was curious what breaking the vow would so to someone already dead. But the stern eyes of Dumbledore made it clear she would be punished somehow if she gossiped, which made her forget about gossiping. They all made the vow, and went their separate ways.

Meanwhile Harry/Fleur was resting in bed, wondering what was going on. Professor Dumbledore then came to her, and addressed her. He cleared his throat, and much to Fleur's pleasure addressed her in French. "Je m'excuse pour la gêne occasionnée Miss Delacour. Il y a eu une sorte de confusion. J'espère que vous pourrez nous pardonner, et peut-être passer l'année ici à Poudlard, en tant qu'étudiant d'échange?"

"Je serais ravi Professeur Dumbledore, pouvons-nous nous arrêter avec cette absurdité de Harry Potter maintenant?" Fleur replied. Dumbledore nodded and solemnly went about his duty. He had Madame Maxine to contact, perhaps even the real Fleur. He had to sort out the official paperwork, then organize Fleur to be part of the sorting ceremony. As usual he looked at his hand, shriveled and black, and wondered how long it would be until the curse took him completely.

12533211121.content:
Dumbledore had a massive headache by the time he was finished with Madame Maxine, the real Fleur, and all the paperwork. Briefly he was judgemental on Harry and the massive issue he had created, until sadly recalling that his own time as a teenager was far worse then anything Harry could do. Sighing he finished placing his signature on every piece of official paperwork, one hand aching, the other feeling nothing at all. Perhaps he was to old to be dealing with this sort of thing. What he could not have known however was that Severus had forgotten to place a silencing charm over Madam Pomfrey's office. With everything out of the way as far as he was concerned, he made preparations for this years sorting ceremony.

Fleur was enraged by the conversation she had heard in the office, but kept her cool when spoken to by Dumbledore. Why did they think she was Harry Potter?! When would they stop with this nonsense and let her continue on with her life? Still,she supposed an excursion at Hogwarts and Britain for a year was worth dealing with it. She could come back to France and Beauxbatons next year, or the year after, having learned the best of both schools. One thing she knew needed to happen was ruining this 'antidote', What would happen if she took it she didn't know, but Fleur wasn't going to find out. She would have to wait until it was almost finished though, to cause maximum damage.

"Students and Staff, I welcome you to another year at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry. As usual we will have the sorting ceremony and feast. However I have a few announcements. One is that all products from Weasley's Wizard Wheezes are banned items, and will be confiscated. I too have an admiration for Fred and George, but their products are not safe to be at Hogwarts. The second, far more important announcement is that we have a exchange student from Beauxbatons. Many of you will remember Miss Fleur Delacour, who represented her school proudly in the Tri-Wizard Cup. She has chosen to spend a year in Britain in order to further her magical studies. Please welcome her, and if Fleur would like to come up here and have her sorting done please, it would be grand. In exchange for Fleur, Harry is learning at Beauxbatons, and no doubt will be soon."

The Hat took it's sweet time deciding where Fleur should go. Strangely it thought it dected hints of Harry Potter in her. Must be some sort of mistake. Finally, the Hat screamed out words that caused Dumbledore's heart to sink. "SLYTHERIN!" Fleur eagerly took her place at Slytherin table, where she was greeted by many of her new housemates. One who attracted Fleur's attention the most however was Draco Malfoy. He seemed a pompous arrogant ass last she was here, but now he was more melancholy and sad. She decided to sit next to him as her robes changed into the green and silver of her new house.

"Bonjour ma belle Fleur, comment vas-tu?" Draco asked, surprising Fleur with his French, which sent her heart racing. Many boys lusted after her, but very rarely did she reciprocate any feelings, yet just being called a beautiful flower, a masterful play on words with her name, she was left blushing and speechless. "Voulez-vous que je sois votre guide en Grande-Bretagne et à Poudlard?" Draco continued to ask, smiling softly at Fleur. This was not the pompous ass she had seen before, Mr Malfoy had seemingly grown up quite a lot and she was surely tempted to say yes. Did she dare?

Fleur was entranced by Draco, and she immediately said yes to him! Her heart raced, she could barely believe it, not even here a day yet she had been asked out on what was practically a date. "Oui! Oui!" She squeaked excitedly. Nearby Pansy Parkinson looked in disgust. Draco had broken up with her at the end of last year, and here he was flirting with some French floozy. Well she would have her revenge, but for now she simmered her anger quietly.

Draco had ulterior motives for his tour with Fleur. He wanted to make sure he got to her before anyone else, and help bring her to the Dark. He knew she was a powerful witch, and would be a great asset to the Dark Lord. Of course, the woman was stunningly beautiful, long flowing blonde hair, Deep distracting blue eyes, and the Slytherin uniform she wore made her all the more sexy, and he was quite open about it to. "Cette robe te fait ressembler à une déesse ma chérie." Draco said, staring into Fleur's eyes as she blushed crimson red. "Merci ... Draco. Si je suis une déesse, vous devez être mon dieu. Tu es tellement plus beau, mature depuis notre dernière rencontre." She said.

After the sorting ceremony had finished, most people dug into their meals, but many at the Slytherin table stared at Fleur and Draco, where Draco was feeding Fleur the best parts of a chocolate pudding. They had only just really met 30 minutes ago, yet everyone was sure there was a new power couple at Hogwarts. After their dinner, Draco led off the students due to his status as a prefect, which only made Fleur respect him even more. She followed close by, not knowing where to go. Once the rest of the new students were sorted, Fleur began to speak to Draco in struggled English. "Monsieur Draco... I would.. appreciate if you.. kissed me." She said, taking pauses to think of the words. However Draco simply smiled and placed a finger to her lips. "Not tonight my sweet flower. I want to know you better before I do anything like that."

That night Fleur fell asleep to thoughts of Draco, kissing him, dating him, even marrying him. She knew some of the other girls would be jealous, so she wondered whether to avoid them, or develop her own clique of girls.

1253321112112.content:
(I realized I described Dumbledore's hand earlier in the story, which somewhat interferes with my ideas. So for this, take it as Dumbledore putting on the ring during OotP not HBP, and Umbridge comes a bit later in the story.)

Daphne was somewhat jealous of Fleur managing to grab Draco, but he couldn't blame her. When it came to boys, first come first served unless you could find a way to break them up. She wasn't about to go angering one of the Tri-Wizard Champions, a well known name in Pureblood circles across Europe. Her own family, the Greengrass family were rich, powerful, but nothing compared to the Delacour family. So instead, she decided it would be better to befriend the French girl, and she had just the idea. At breakfast, she made sure to sit next to the French girl, and tapped her on the shoulders. "Hello Fleur, I'm Daphne Greengrass. Congratulations on nabbing Draco, but surely you understand you've made plenty of girls, in all houses, jealous correct?"

Fleur was about to say something to Greengrass, to show she would not tolerate threats, before the girl quickly finished what she was saying. "I'm not here to interfere, in fact, I have a proposition. Me and some other girls would be more then willing to follow you, be your clique. All you have to do is teach us French, and some of the magic you learned at Beauxbaton's." Fleur pondered for a moment, then nodded. "Oui, Je peux vous parler du français et de la magie française caractéristique. Retrouvez-moi dans la Grande Salle, tous les week-ends à midi." Fleur said. At Daphne's baffled look, she apologized, and began to speak in simple English. "Yes, Weekend, noon." She then went about to organize such an event with the staff, which was approved quickly.

It was headache after headache for Dumbledore. Harry as Fleur was now in Slytherin, being courted by Draco Malfoy, and making her own friends. Even if the antidote worked, which there was no guarantee, the damage was being done. The repair of his friendship with Madame Maxine and Beauxbatons would take years, if it ever was repaired. The damage to Hogwarts would take as much time to repair. How could he take Fleur out of the mix suddenly and have Harry turn back up with no questions asked, no suspicions? It didn't help the Ministry was sending one of their own to Hogwarts as DATDA Professor after he failed to find one. His hand, Harry/Fleur, the Ministry, was nothing going right? Had his luck finally run out?

Things then got even worse for Dumbledore. During breakfast, was when Umbridge finally showed up, and made her speech. It was long, boring, and meant that the Ministry was openly disputing that Voldemort had returned, and was trying to take control of Hogwarts. Many in the school did not approve of Umbridge, but neither were they fans of Dumbledore, and the mysterious vanishing of Harry Potter. Slytherin especially were supporters of the Ministry and Umbridge, including Fleur. Breakfast ended, and now class was set to begin for the day.

12533211121122.content:
Defense Against the Dark Arts was.... interesting to say the least of it. Umbridge clearly had no intention of teaching anything but the most basic of spells, because in her mind, there was no threat to defend against. No one liked her, she even got on the nerves of many in Slytherin. But still many in Hogwarts were behind the Ministry. Draco wrote to his father of the situation, asking him to do something, but he was told in response that there was a plan behind it all, and to trust in Umbridge for now, and so he relayed the information to Slytherin. Daphne however had an idea to get around the poor class. "She never said we couldn't practice in our own time, let's use Fleur's French Club as an excuse!"

During class, they kept quiet, and kept to the plan, while Gryffindor frequently got into trouble for asking questions. Some, including Hermione Granger were asked to come back tonight for detention, where a 'special punishment' would be awaiting for them for 'spreading such vile lies and rumors'. Umbridge was very angry, and Fleur wondered if she had Ministry backing for this punishment of hers.

Fleur set the plan for the 'French Club' in motion. She would of course teach French, but also practice their magic during it. And so Slytherin began to ensure they would pass their tests with flying colors. Meanwhile Gryffindor had a similar idea, with Fred and George taking control of what was to be called Dumbledore's Army. Some however did not join, as many questioned where Harry Potter had gone, why he had suddenly left. If not for the reputation and respect of the Weasley Twins, many might not have joined the group at all.

Fleur on the weekend, waited nervously in Hogsmeade for Draco, having dressed up in the best clothes she could possibly find, all related to Slytherin and snakes. Her hair long loose and straight, all her jewellery involved Slytherin, a necklace, earrings, and her heels were custom made Slytherin design. Where was the boy? Suddenly an arm pulled her tight against someone's body. "Je suis juste derrière toi belle" Draco said, turning Fleur to face him, which caused her heart to beat faster. "J'espère que je ne t'ai pas fait peur?" He asked. "Non, je pense juste à nous. A propos de ce baiser que tu as promis." She replied. "Plus tard ma douce fleur."

The spent the whole day, talking, eyes only for each other. Draco regaled her with a tale about how he had escaped from a dragon, and Fleur was entranced. She hung on his every word, even though the story was probably bullshit. But she didn't care, or want to know, she would take him at his word. He told her more stories, about his time at Hogwarts, and in general. When it was time to leave, he walked her back up to the castle, only to suddenly take her in a passionate kiss, pulling her close. Fleur moaned into his lips, leaving her mouth wide open. How fitting that he would French kiss a French woman! When he was done, Draco knew he had a hold on her for life.

In the following weeks and months, things got interesting. During each of their classes, Umbridge was there, taking notes, asking questions, and just generally being insulting to staff, who could not do a thing to stop her. Students who went to her detention often came back in pain, covering their hands, some of them wearing heavy bandaging over their hands. Fleur took note of this, and gathered evidence. She had a feeling of what Draco's father had in store, and she wanted to prove her worth to Slytherin by participating in it. What they did not know was that she had destroyed a certain potion Professor Snape was making, the so called antidote. When discovered, Snape and Hermione gave up on Harry, Myrtle having drifted off long ago somewhere else, as she had grown tired of the scenery at Hogwarts, tired of waiting.

"It has been months now, and Harry shows no signs of changing back. I fear either his potion was permanent, or something along the way interacted with the experiment and made it permanent. He had rebuffed any attempts at friendship not approved by Slytherin, and it seems he, or rather she has begun dating Mr Malfoy. I will inform the real Fleur, though I cannot predict what she will do." In reality, Dumbledore had a plan, one to cause the least amount of trouble and damage.

When he saw the real Fleur again, she was furious at the headmaster and demanded to confront her clone. Instead Dumbledore altered her memory, made her into someone new, and sent her off. To many questions would be raised, to many things would come to light if Fleur had been allowed to co exist with her clone

One fateful day, there was a commotion in the courtyard, as Professor Trelawney, the teacher for Divination had just been fired by Umbridge. Trelawney was comforted by Professor McGonnogal, while Dumbledore had a heated conversation with Umbridge. It was clear one of them had to go. And that was exactly what happened. Slytherin had gained permission for their club, while Gryffindor hadn't, so when she caught wind it of, she tasked Slytherin with finding it. "Draco, Fleur dears. I have heard rumors of a secret club of students. I would appreciate it if you could investigate the claims, and help me crack down on them. I assure you both will have a place by my side when it is taken care of.

"Oui Professeur, nous allons sévir contre ces rebelles avec toute l'étendue des règles." Fleur said, walking out with her boyfriend to find the secret group. It wasn't hard, they simply took suspected students, and asked them questions using Veritasereum. It didn't take long before they found someone, one Marietta Edgecombe, who confessed the hideout, and the members. Quickly, Umbridge gathered a select number of students to bring the group into custody. The plan was an outright success, and dragging them into the headmasters office, they confronted Dumbledore. Unfortunately, he got away, using his Phoenix to vanish in a mass of flames after taking out an auror.

"As your new Headmistress, and Ministry High Inquisitor, I would like to announce the creation of a new group of school student authorities, the Inquisitorial Squad. Students Fifth year and above are free to join,and will gain extra credit for doing so."

(Sorry if this seems rushed and jumpy. It is. I've been dealing with some writers block, and I didn't want to waste time, so I kinda jumbled most of OOTP together to get it out of the way)
12533211121122.title: DATDA with Gryffindor

choices: The Squad coups Umbridge
The students rebel and overthrow Umbridge
125332111211221.content:
(With Harry gone with no notice,many potential allies would have been lost, so expect people who may have originally sided with Harry to be against Dumbledore, and possibly be influenced to the Dark Side)

It wasn't long before in a mass ceremony, the Inquisitorial Squad was picked out. Fleur, Draco, Blaise Zabini, Theo Nott, Susan Bones, Hannah Abbott, Zacharias Smith, Penelope Clearwater, Roger Davies were the volunteers to join the squad, who were given power over prefects, but not over the Head Boy and Head Girl, not that it mattered since Clearwater and Davies took the positions. Umbridge was willing to give out punishments for the slightest infractions, which the Squad were supposed to give out. Instead unless it was someone who had been a serious defender of Dumbledore, they let them get away. The orders from Lucius were to build allies for when Umbridge inevitably went to far.

Hermione was growing extremely worried. Harry hadn't changed back, even with half the year gone. Snape tried to brew another antidote, but he simply didn't have the ingredients, nor could he gather them without suspicion. Myrtle didn't care anymore, and had left, and Madame Pomfrey had to spend time healing the wounds of each student who was given detention. She had used the blood quill willingly to provide proof when the time came, having guessed what it was the second she saw it. She sighed, everything seemed to go wrong. Edgecombe had ratted out the DA, Dumbledore had vanished, and surprise member of the DA Pansy Parkinson had been stripped of her rank as Prefect, the title going to Harry/Fleur. She sighed and set to work building a movement against Umbridge. Her letters to Sirius had been depressing. He hadn't been surprised Dumbledore kept what happened to Harry secret, and that he would find ways to disable the vow.

Sirius had been doing his own sleuthing at Hogsmeade on the Weekends, all in his Animagus form. No one knew where Harry was, and Dumbledore the bloody geezer made sure he wouldn't know. He sighed, and turned into Padfoot, walking away from Hogsmeade before he gathered to much attention. He would doubtless have to leave the country one of these days, but for now he'd stick around, look for Harry, maybe spend some time with Remus. He had vowed many things in his life, that he would find a cure for Lycanthropy, that he would protect James until the bitter end, and more recently that he'd find a way to get past unbreakable vows.

Draco had pulled Fleur aside on patrol one night, in order to spend some private time with her. "Mes parents ont demandé à te rencontrer mon amour." He said to her, holding her hands with a smile. Fleur blushed bright red, but quickly agreed. She knew that if they wanted to meet her, they were seriously considering her to marry Draco. "J'adorerais les rencontrer ma chérie, je suis tellement excitée!" She said. It wasn't long before their patrol was forgotten, and Fleur was snogging Draco, who had pressed her against a wall. They lost track of time, and were only interrupted when they heard a croaky meow that every student dreaded. "Oh you fucking cat. Of course you show up when I'm kissing a beautiful woman!" Draco cursed. The scrawny dust coloured cat was Mrs Norris, and wherever Mrs Norris was, Argus Filch, the ugly and cruel caretaker of Hogwarts wasn't far behind. Resisting the urge to kick it was smart, because seconds later Filch was there.

"It's past curfew, even for the Squad. Get to bed Malfoy, French girl." He said before walking off. He wasn't going to punish the son of a school Governor right away, he might not know magic, but he valued his job here. Instead of heading to the common room right away, they went up to the Astronomy Tower, stargazing, her head in his lap. They spent that time in silence, simply enjoying the view before heading to bed.
125332111211221.title: The Inqusitorial Squad

choices: Susan Bones and Hannah Abbott join
Roger Davies and Penople Clearwater join
1253321112112211.content:
Umbridge's offenses began to get worse and worse. Students were on the verge of a riot. Quidditch had been cancelled, for all houses, even though only Gryffindor had been actively working against. Each other house only had isolated cases of members working against her. It turned many against the Gryffindor's, but also against Umbridge. Each day she punished students for some outrageous offense, each day more kids turned up with scars. The final straw was when the Weasley twins stole back their brooms, which caused a disaster. The two let off a show of outrageous fireworks, stuck a swamp in the middle of an essential hallway, and even Peeves, the old Poltergeist who obeyed save the Bloody Barron and Professor Dumbledore, saluted the two when they asked him to cause trouble for them, and quickly set the work.

"I have had it with these troublesome students!" Umbridge said later that day, in her office with two students to chairs. Some Ravenclaw called Lovegood, and the Weaslette, Ginny. "Perhaps the Cruciatus curse will open your tongues and let me know who among your comrades is causing trouble!" She said, a sadistic smile on her face as she seemed to have snapped. When she cast it, she was quickly apprehended by the Squad. "That's illegal Professor. My aunt and the Minister will be hearing of this." Susan Bones said. She had been hanging out with the Slytherin's more often, and had, with some persuasion involved, joined in their plan. "As will my father. I'm afraid your time as headmistress is over." Draco said.

"NO! YOU CAN'T DO THIS TO ME!" Umbridge squealed, as she was dragged off to the Ministry by Draco and Susan, who all agreed had the most sway in convincing the Ministry of her crimes. Meanwhile the others went out to address the restless students before they exploded, and started a riot. "Doleres Umbridge has been taken into Ministry custody. From here on, the Heads of House will rotate as Headmaster/Headmistress until the Board can decide on a new candidate." Said Blaise, making his voice magically louder.

Despite a bit of reputation as lackeys, their leniency towards students and their coup of Umbridge lead them all the become school favorites, and as Fleur continued her French classes, she spotted more students turning up. Of course, seeing the potential, she began leaking bits of Pureblood propaganda into her works. Very few Gryffindor's wanted anything to do with the squad, spurning potential allies in favor of demonizing them. Oh well, if they wished to dig their own grave so eagerly, Fleur would not stop them.

Exams came and went, and with Umbridge gone all students managed to score well. Umbridge quickly lost her position in the Ministry and Hogwarts. Fudge resigned as Minister for Magic in disgrace, replaced by Rufus Scrimgeour, former head Auror, who was openly pro Dumbledore. However it didn't change the fact that Harry was still missing, and for safety, all those involved agreed to remove the memory from their minds. No one could find out this information, especially Voldemort. Dumbledore remained missing as well, with some claiming his death, others claiming they had seen him living remotely as a hermit. Whatever it was, he did not return, and eventually the Board voted Lucius Malfoy as Headmaster, arriving at the end of year feast.

"Welcome everyone to the end of another year at this fine establishment. Originally the Board wished to reinstate the previous Headmaster, Professor Dumbledore. Unfortunately, he has not returned. Thus they have decided to place this fine school into my fine hands. Minerva McGonnogal will continue her post as deputy, and I can assure you there will be some changes, but everyone is still welcome at Hogwarts!" He said, not having much choice in the matter. He couldn't blow his cover as a Death Eater, nor would the Board or Ministry allow him to ban Mudbloods from the school. So, he would bide his time.

After the feast, the new Headmaster went to visit each of the houses. Gryffindor was the most hostile, followed by Hufflepuff, though noticeably Susan Bones and Hannah Abbott did their best to drum up support for him. Ravenclaw was split on the matter, while Lucius received a hero's welcome in Slytherin. Upon his entrance, there was mass applause from the students. Lucius knew he could let his guard down here. "Thank you Slytherin, thank you. Forgive me for the dribble I said at dinner. The time is not yet right for the world to accept the truth of our supremacy, but rest assured, when the time is right you will know.

"Certain students will be invited to Malfoy Manor over the holiday break. This is not something to be ignored, to be forgotten about. This is an invitation from the Dark Lord himself. We will suffer no ambiguity. If you do not attend, you will be assumed to have joined the Ministry's side, and you will be treated as such. I have two invitations personally to hand out, one to my son Draco, and one to the lovely Miss Delacour." Lucius said, taking her hand and kissing it. "I will see you two at the manor soon, the Dark Lord wishes to speak with you personally. Until then, I must be about my duties. Keep watch for those letters students, do not lose them, do not ignore them, do not refuse our offer. And do keep your mind open, you are not the only house we will be recruiting from."

After school had ended, Fleur and Draco boarded the express, talking to their friends. "Who do you reckon will be getting the letters? I don't Crabbe or Goyle receiving one, nor any Gryffindor's." Blaise Zabini said, well out of earshot of the two brutes doing guard duty at the ends of the carriage. "Most of us already have a parent in one of them, or at least a parent sympathetic to the Dark Lord." The rest, Theo Nott, Fleur, Draco and Daphne Greengrass considered. "Everyone of us, will get letters." Fleur said. Her English was improving, but still she struggled and kept her speech short and simple. Nott spoke up next. "Inquisitorial Squad members." He said, short and simple to the point.

"Are you sure about that Theo? Smith is an asshole who alienated everyone, Bones' aunt is the top Law Enforcement official in the nation, and I don't see another Barty Crouch Jr happening, Abbott panics whenever things go wrong, she'll go wherever Bones' goes,Clearwater used to date the really pompous Weasel, don't recall his name, and Davies, well he's good at Quidditch, but never really stands out anywhere else." Daphne said, having gone over each of them. "I don't think we'll be able to even guess until we see them. If they're smart, they'll have kept their heads down and not attracted any undue attention. Now that I say that maybe Davies is one of them."
1253321112112211.title: The Squad coups Umbridge

choices: Straight to Hogwarts
Draco and Fleur get rings fitted
Blank Option, whatever you want
12533211121122111.content:
(First image is how Voldemort looks for this series of chapters, second is the dress given to Fleur/Harry by Narcissa/What she wears to the ball, third is Draco's ball outfit. For plot purposes/forgetfulness the 1996 Azkaban Breakout happens in this chapter)

https://bifa.imgix.net/web/2018/10/394fu...

https://i.pinimg.com/originals/3a/9b/c2/...

https://i.pinimg.com/originals/68/51/be/...)

Fleur and Draco took a portkey directly from Platform 9 3/4 to Malfoy Manor, where they were quickly greeted by Narcissa Malfoy. "Welcome home Draco, et bienvenue à vous aussi Mademoiselle Delacour. Il semble que notre fils soit très amoureux de toi et toi de lui." She said, quickly switching over to French for the benefit of Fleur, which brought a smile to the French woman's face. "C'est merveilleux de vous rencontrer enfin Madame Malfoy. Draco m'a tellement parlé de toi." Fleur replied, walking with the older woman while Draco went to visit his father. She was given a tour of Malfoy Manor, from it's grand libraries, to a outdoor Quidditch pitch, many bedrooms, bathrooms and various other rooms, all for one purpose or another.

"You're a good Pureblooded girl Fleur, some Veela heritage but then half of Wizarding France is part Veela, doubtless you know what being invited here means for you. Out of many potential suitors, me and my husband have decided to betroth you to our son, not that Draco would accept anyone else. Since you are an adult, you do not need your parents permission, but we decided to talk to your parents anyway. They accept, and you are free to live here. You need not worry about 'separate' bedrooms. I know how randy I was with Lucius when I went to Hogwarts. I would talk to you more in French, but I'm afraid I do not know the language well" She said with a hearty laugh. Fleur shook her head, excusing Narcissa. "It is quite... alright Mrs Malfoy. I can... make do in anglaise." She said.

Draco talked to his father while Fleur was off with his mother. "The Dark Lord is currently away on some important business. He will be back very soon, and he will speak with you then. You'll be happy to know Fleur is officially your betrothed. We will be buying the best rings for the both of you very soon." Lucius told his son. Draco was ecstatic that he was officially betrothed to Fleur, unable to think of what to say other then a quick "Thank you father!".

Fleur was soon brought back downstairs, wearing a gorgeous green dress that was a gift from Narcissa. At that moment Draco forgot about his parents, and had eyes only for Fleur. "Tu es la femme la plus magnifique du monde en ce moment." He stated, pulling her towards him, while she locked her arms behind his neck. "Attends juste notre bal de fiançailles, tu ne voudras plus jamais me laisser quitter la chambre." Fleur replied with a flirty wink. It was then a new arrival cleared his throat, and the two of them look embarrassed.

"Such young love is good to see in these days." The man said. He was clearly in his older years, balding dark brown hair, standing taller then both Lucius and Draco, a light beard crossing his face, wearing a fancy black suit. Then there were his eyes, yellowish red, pupils like a Snake. Both of them had the feeling this was a glamour, that the Dark Lord hid his real appearance for the sake of not scaring people off. All four knelt before him,before he beckoned them up. "Let us sit down and discuss our plans." He said. His voice was smooth, and yet there was something about it as well. How much did he modify himself?

They all sat down at the dining room table, where Voldemort began to speak. "Thank you Lucius for allowing me to use your home as my headquarters. The betrothal of the lovely Mr Malfoy and Miss Delacour works nicely in my favor. Your engagement ball will serve as a reason to gather various students. You and they will form the new blood of the Death Eaters, and there will be some surprises waiting in store for the candidates." With that, the Dark Lord stood up and left, no doubt planning something. Something about him made Fleur feel uneasy, but she pushed down the feelings.

A few weeks passed by and the day of the ball had finally arrived. During that time there had been a mass breakout from Azkaban. Fleur was led out to the ballroom floor by none other then the Dark Lord himself, while Draco was led out by his aunt Bellatrix. No amount of makeup and hair potion could change the fact the woman looked utterly unhinged. She retained her soul from the Dementors, but not her sanity. As the ball began, each person danced with the person who they came in with, and both Fleur and Draco were uncomfortable, but did their duty.

The most surprising part of the ball were the guests. The Slytherin's were of course of no surprise, but there was Hannah Abbott and Susan Bones among a few other students, who Fleur couldn't put name to face. When the dancing took a break, she walked over to them. "Mademoiselle Bones, Mademoiselle Abbott, I am... surprised to see you." Fleur said. Her English was getting better, but she would always retain a heavy French accent. "We were both surprised to get a letter in the mail. Those French classes Fleur, your lessons... awoke something in me. Those talks about Pureblood society, how we're better then Mudbloods. I started doing my own research, and the Dark Lord... the words wrote down about him are enchanting." Susan said.

"But what about your aunt, the Madame Bones. Is she not... a very big deal?" Fleur pressed. "Aunt Amelia has grown more cold and distant. She'd thrown herself into her work, and has zero time for me, or my questions. Maybe I'd be walking down a different path, but Headmaster Malfoy was happy to talk to me and Hannah. And with Harry Potter and Dumbledore still missing, I don't see how the Dark Lord can be defeated." She said, a grim look on her face. Susan knew it was far to late to turn back now.

"What about you Mademoiselle Abbott?" Fleur asked, turning her attention from the redhead to the fellow blonde. "I go where Susan goes. I had those same questions, but my parents refused to even consider talking about such things. Plus, my boyfriend, Neville, he broke up with me when I joined the squad. He wouldn't listen to a word I had to say in my defense, instead he said he was ashamed of me, and he didn't want to speak again. Lastly, like Susan said, Potter is missing, Dumbledore is missing, who is left to oppose the Dark Lord?" She said, much more unsure about this then Susan.

"Welcome, welcome all to the lovely Malfoy Manor. Lucius and Narcissa have been eternally helpful in letting me use their home for the Death Eater's headquarters. First, I have some guests to introduce. My loyal followers have been suffering in Azkaban, but with the defection of the Dementors, they are free once more! However, all of my dear followers will be honored today, so when I call your name, my dear followers, you will stand before me." He said, and he started to name his followers.

"Alecto Carrow! Amos Avery! Amycus Carrow! Antonin Dolohov! Argus Lee! Augustus Rookwood! My faithful left hand Bellatrix LeStrange! Cadmus Snyde! Corban Yaxley! Fenrir Greyback! Gellert Gibbon! Gregory Goyle Sr! Griselda Lee! Helena Snyde! John Jugson! My faithful right hand Lucius Malfoy! Michael Mulciber! Peter Pettigrew! Rabastan LeStrange! Rodolphus LeStrange! Severus Snape! Sturgis Selwyn! Theodore Nott Senior! Thorfinn Rowle! Torquil Travers! Vincent Crabbe Senior! Walden Macnair!"

All of his servants lined up before him, and the Dark Lord left them in awkward silence for some time before speaking. "There needs to be some trimming of the fat. Crabbe, Greyback, Goyle, Jugson, Travers the Lee's, the Snyde's. Escort all these names from the premises, and deal with them as you wish. Everyone else, consider this a warning. I will not tolerate failure in any of you! Now, for the new blood.

"Blaise Zabini, Daphne Greengrass, Draco Malfoy, Hannah Abbott, Fleur Delacour, Susan Bones, Theodore Nott Junior. You have the honor of being the newest generation of Death Eaters. Come forth, and receive your marks." Voldemort commanded, and one by one they obeyed, stepping forward, and painfully having the marks burned onto their arms. Afterwards the ball continued as normal as it possibly could. At the end, the newly minted Death Eaters were given a task. "You are to find a way for Death Eaters to infiltrate Hogwarts. We will firmly take over the school completely. You can expect no help from Lucius, this you must all do yourselves."
12533211121122111.title: Susan Bones and Hannah Abbott join

choices: Slughorn is eager to have them both in the club
Slughorn tries sending Draco away
(Blank choice, whatever you wish)
125332111211221112.content:
(Not sure why the images aren't working, so let's try this again. Send me a message if they aren't working. I'd appreciate being told what I'm doing wrong with them as well. Short chapter but I just couldn't think of anything else just yet, and I didn't want to restart it again tomorrow.)

Voldermort's apperance: https://bifa.imgix.net/web/2018/10/394fu...

Fleur's Ball outfit: https://i.pinimg.com/originals/3a/9b/c2/...

Draco's Ball outfit: https://i.pinimg.com/originals/0c/5c/91/...

The ring: https://images-na.ssl-images-amazon.com/... )

The day after the ball, Draco and Fleur were taken to Diagon Alley, just in time for the shops to open. "Surely we could have slept in a bit longer?" Draco said embarrassed. He and Fleur were about to get frisky when his father had barged in. "Nous n'avons rien fait d'inapproprié Monsieur Malfoy, je le jure! Draco is a perfect gentleman!" Fleur declared, and even now she was still blushing, though looking at Draco like he was the only thing in the world. "Oh don't you worry Miss Delacour, I was the same with Narcissa at Draco's age, even now I can't keep my hands off her when she wears..." "Please stop there Father, let's just get this business done with."

Lucius simply smiled at his son and led them inside. The store was quite a fancy one, full of jewellery, magical and non magical. "Ah. Mister Malfoy, we have your items ready to go, pending payment and except the rings of course. 7000 Galleons for those, final total will be calculated once you have your rings picked out." The wizard at the counter said. "7000 GALLEONS!?" Draco yelled, before being shushed by his father.

"Yes, 7000 Galleons. We can afford it easily, and this isn't some plastic thing, these are specially crafted enchanted items, which you will both receive at your wedding. Now, no more outbursts, let's get rings fitted for you. Please show us the way Ignatius." Lucius said. The three were led out of the shop itself, and into the Gringott's Bank. "Si la banque a ces anneaux, ils doivent être incroyables!" Fleur said excitedly. She loved the idea of expensive jewellery just for her and Draco.

"Vault 1021 please." Ignatius the store owner said, showing his key and wand to the goblin sitting at the front desk. "Everything is in order, come along then, hands and feet inside the cart at all times." The goblin said, leading them into a mine cart. The journey took some time, underground it was almost impossible to tell the time, and eventually they were led to a high security vault. With the key, and the goblin placing his full hand upon the door, it opened up, and revealed carefully lined displays of jewellery. .

They were led to the ring section, where Fleur and Draco were fitted with multiple rings, of various jewels, sizes and enchantments. Eventually a silver and emerald, with a floral pattern was chosen. Igantius nodded, and talked to the goblin, who took the rings, and delivered them to a different vault, 845, on the way back.

Now it was finally time for the two to return to Hogwarts. Lucius and Narcissa came to see them off, and soon they were seated with Daphne Greengrass and Blaise Zabini. "It's great to see you two lovebirds again. Ready for the big task?" Zabini said, first jokingly, then settling into the grim reality. "As ready as I can be. We need to get this done quickly, no screwing around, every inch of free time goes into finding a way past you know what." Draco said, just as grim as Blaise.

Fleur kept silent, merely watching the two, when a messenger arrived. "We have a new Potion's professor, Snape got Defense! His name is Slughorn, and he wants to meet Miss Delacour and Mister Malfoy." Said a third year Slytherin, and soon the two set off for their meeting with the new Professor.

125332112.title: In a panic, Harry scrabbles through his notes.

choices: "Just an hour, relax... It's just for an hour." Myrtle says calmingly.
"Non, je suis Fleur Delacour." she proclaims defiantly.
She concentrates, her forehead crinkles up. Before she says, "I'm Harry."
1253321121.content:
Harry dives towards her books and parchments scattered untidily on the floor. Her dress billows out around her, as she sinks down. She starts to breathe heavily as she flicks the pages forward and back.

"Pourquoi sont-ils en Anglais?" she asks her voice rising in panic.

Myrtle floats down, "You do remember that you are Harry Potter?" she asks intently.

The veela looks gorgeous, but more than that she looks confused.

"Plus lentement... parle plus lentement, s'il te plaît."

"They. are. in. English. because. you. are. English." she points a spectral hand directly towards Harry.

Harry bites her lower lip, it trembles, and her eyes are welling up, on the verge of tears.
1253321121.title: In a panic, Harry scrabbles through his notes.

choices: Hermione isn't in the library.
She summons Hermione to Harry's side.
As she tells Hermione, the conversation is overheard.
12533211212.content:
"Non, je suis Fleur Delacour." she proclaims defiantly.

"No. You. are. Harry Potter." Myrtle repeated back frustratedly.

"No. Harry Potter. C'est le nom d'un garçon. Ai-je le corps d'un garçon?"

She looked a little insulted at the spectre's suggestion. She gestured to her reflection, with a French flourish, and look of disbelief.

A pout formed on Fleur's face, as she tried to work out the ghost's problem. Was she stupid.... or playing a trick....

There were gaps in her memory that she didn't like to consider. This was clearly not Beauxbatons, and if she was right, the view through the window, she was in Hogwarts. She loved the Scottish countryside. She had to concentrate, and not get side tracked.

Myrtle was also feeling like she was banging her head against a brick wall, and for a ghost that was even more irritating.

"Wait. Here." she said.

Even frowning, she looked cute.

"Wait...ez ici." She pointed to the floor.

Clearly this was beyond her. Harry probably wouldn't appreciate this, but she had to go and get Hermione and Ron. Well, she had to at least get Hermione.

She took the direct route to the library, passing out through the wall. Just before she left, she looked backwards. She saw Fleur sitting on the floor, her lips moved slowly as she silently pronounced the words to herself. Clearly English was becoming more difficult for her... him.

She snorted and kept going.

125332112121.content:
Myrtle muttered to herself as she floated through the walls and passageways of the castle. She didn't like to involve herself too much in the affairs of the living. But she liked the three friends... her three friends. Besides, it was clear, (to Myrtle at least) that Hermione belonged in Ravenclaw.

She popped her head down through the ceiling of the library, and took a bird's eye view of the study tables. Hermione wasn't there, so she headed off to the Grand Hall. The great clock chimed the hours, and she felt a longing for food. As a ghost, her days weren't split up with meals.

With a kick off the air, she sped off towards the Hall. As she got closer, she heard the sounds of cutlery and plates, and boisterous conversations. As she surfaced, her head popped up through the table. Or more accurately, through the roast turkey, resting on a platter on the table.

Ron gave a short shrill cry, that wouldn't have sounded out of place coming from Ginny's mouth.

"Myrtle." he spluttered trying regain his composure, "You startled me."

She beckoned Hermione closer. Her transparent hand, becoming more diffuse. Ron automatically leaned closer.

"Do you know what Harry was trying to do?" she hissed archly.

Hermione and Ron exchanged glances. Not wanting to discuss that so openly. Harry's experiments with polyjuice were meant to aid the Order. An attempt to make a more potent version, longer lasting, or capable of withstanding greater scrutiny.

"And if we do?" Ron asked.

Hermione interrupted before Myrtle could say anything, "You said 'was', you said 'was trying to do'. Has he succeeded?"

The ghost's face must have revealed more than she wanted.

As Hermione sighed, "Okay, what's he done?" she asked with a certain resigned tone.

She liked Ron's enthusiasm, and how much he relied on her help. But Harry was just as 'enthusiastic', (reckless, she told herself), but he was much more capable of getting himself into bother.

"Would it be easier just to show us?"

Myrtle smirked, a rare and oddly unsettling sight, "Oui, Madame." she added. Floating off towards the door. Ron tucked an apple inside his cloak as he left the table.

By the time Hermione and Ron arrived at the girl's bathroom Fleur/Harry was gone. A few blonde hairs, an empty goblet, a blank piece of parchment and a book on potions was all that remained, and no sign of their friend was there. By Myrtle's French, it didn't take long for Hermione to figure out who Harry had transformed into. Suddenly she pounced on the book and began flipping page after page.

"Not good, not good at all." She muttered to himself. "Harry's transformed himself into Fleur, but she's partially non human, with her Veela heritage. I can't find a thing on what might happen. Polyjuice is supposed to last an hour, but my own experiences lasted longer then that." Hermione said, snapping the book shut and putting it down. It was a different situation though. Hermione had accidentally taken animal hair. Veela's were humanoid, far more sophisticated and sentient then a cat.

"Ron, Myrtle, could you please see Professor Dumbledore and Professor McGonnogal? Explain the situation. I''ll try and find Harry, and then a cure." She sighed. Myrtle had a smirk on her face, finding it amusing. But she did like Harry, and wanted to help him. Ron was all to eager to take the task, unsure of what Fleur's Veela heritage would do to him.

As Ron sped off with the ghost, Hermione looked back and saw the paper once more. If it was what she thought it was, finding Harry would be a lot easier.

"Ron, Hermione." his voice... her voice was still soft and feminine with a hint of a French lilt. She tried to sit up, only for Hermione to stop her with one hand. A stray lock of blond hair fell across her eyes.

Poppy misunderstood.

12533211212132211.content:
"Sorry Poppy, I should have told you." Dumbledore's quiet but firm voice confirmed from the door of the infirmary. His arrival as ever was silent, and his presence unnoticed until he wanted to speak.

He cast his eyes slowly over the three students. His eyes brimmed with Wisdom, pausing only briefly on the student in the bed.

"Harry is away at Beauxbatons... and might be there for some time." He spoke clearly, informing Harry of his fate, even as he directed his words towards Madam Pomfrey.

"I'm sure he'll learn a lot in his time there, but I hope he learns caution most of all."

"Headmaster, if I'd known in advance, I could have topped up my Veela specific supplies."

Madam Pomfrey was one of the few who would argue her corner with Dumbledore. Verbally her hands were on her hips.

"Fleur is half-Veela. But you are right. I shall get Hagrid to search the Forbidden Forest for the various herbs that might be needed."

Ron couldn't meet Dumbledore's gaze, and Hermione's eyes kept drifting off his forgiving face. However, it was Fleur who met his look, even if she did find herself blushing as red as a Weasley.

"Hagrid knows which to collect, but perhaps he'll appreciate a few more hands and slender fingers."

He arched his eyebrow, and Hermione took the hint.

"I like Herbology." Hermione said with enthusiasm. She had to fight to not raise her arm before answering.

Fleur piped up, "I think the fresh air might help my recovery."

Madam Pomfrey gave a sceptical "Mmm hmmm.", but shared a look with Albus before giving the slightest of nods of acceptance.

"Of course, Fleur seems to have found friends with these two Gryffindors, so I think she should share with them."

He tapped his chin as he thought.

"I believe there's a free bed in your particular dorm, Hermione."

The three students were each wanting to protest for their own reasons. But their words died in their throats.

"No, Fleur!" Hermione stressing her name just a little heavily. "You'll be sharing a dormitory with me."

Leaning closer, she hissed in Harry's ear. "I know you can visit the boys' side, but I'd advise against it."

Fleur's frown was a picture of cute confusion, one that made Hermione groan internally. Was there anything she'd do that wasn't cutesy or adorable. She fought down her insecurity, and jealousy. Reminding herself who was behind those wide, deep, seductive eyes.

Nodding 'Fleur's attention towards a nearby mirror. Hissed for herr to look closely at it. The reflection was largely filled by the pair. But, the background was what Hermione wanted her to notice.

Fleur's mouth dropped open, just a smidgeon when she spotted it too. The rest of the Gryffindors were all staring at them. Or rather, at her. Some of the young wizards admiring her full, pert, perfect bum. Others trying to inhale her presence. The witches were not immune, some elbowing distracted neighbours to restore their attention. Others with fixed, thin-lipped smiles, as their eyes shot mute daggers at her back.

With a guiding arm around her waist, Hermione directed Fleur towards the girls stairs. Mounting them unsteadily at first, and with growing confidence. Harry/Fleur realised they weren't about to transform into a slide, and the school truly recognised her as a witch.

125332112121322112.content:
It was Hermione's trip to the library that provided most of the suggested herbs on their list. With 'Fleur' remaining ignorant of their uses and purposes. As a mature half-veela, but without the puberty that came with growing up in that body Fleur was an explosive mix of hormones and needs.

The questions Harry/Fleur had asked Hermione as she was assembling the list was greeted with a few too frank answers. Harry blushed to her roots, as Hermione described what would happen to her body, if she didn't get it under control. The responses she'd pull from the other students was just as horrifying to her.

She was virtually hanging off Hermione's arm, as they left the library. Ron was excluded from this little excursion, as Harry was afraid of what he might tempt his friend into. He certainly didn't want him to ask him on a date, or provoke some extreme lust-inspired act of devotion from him.

Hermione hid the worried look that passed over her face, as the pair descended the path to their big friends cottage. Taking in the sway of those hips as Harry led the way. The swing of her hips uninhibited, and unconscious. She doubted if Harry were aware of the change from his normal ground-swallowing stalking walk.

- - - - -

Hagrid, with his half-giant heritage, and natural immunity wasn't going to be suspectible to those surging seas of hormones Harry felt coursing through her blood. She hoped to have this under control as quickly as possible with these herbs.

The broad beaming smile on his face, as he opened the door to his visitors was as wide and genuine as ever. Sinking into a crouch, he welcomed Hermione with a warm embrace.

"And 'hoo might you be?" Hagrid asked, before taking her in. "Fleur Delacour, as I live and breathe."

"It's a school exchange program." Hermione added, "Harry's at Beaubatons."

"Ohh..." Hagrid sounded hurt, " 'e didn't say good-bye."

"It's me.." Harry/Fleur said in a rush, "Diving into Hagrid's arms... It's me... I'm 'arry." She gestured to her body with a shame faced smile, as if revealing a dark secret.

Hermione shrugged, with a passable, if high-pitched impression she added, "She shouldn't 'ave said that... She really shouldn't have..."

The three friends hug tightened as they shared the secret.

"So..." Hagrid said, with a deep breath, "You're half-veela." His nostrils flared, taking in the aroma. Although immune to it's influence he wasn't blind to its' presence.

"Yeah." Hermione added, shifting the parchment to present it to him. "We need these to help her keep control."

Rummaging through the various barrels and jars of dried herbs, and the ones still drying in posies on the wall, they found the majority of them.

Harry felt a difference in Hagrid's gaze, he was studying her. A professional appraisal of a 'Care of Magical Creatures' professor.

"It's still me, 'agrid." she insisted.

1253321121213222.content:
Hermione felt a wave of jealousy, as she looked over at the poor confused girl. Even now, she looked perfect, her make-up, her mannerisms, everything about the poly-formed Harry was a perfect imitation of Fleur Delacour.

But she managed to push down her frustration, that perfect skin, that natural grace.

"Harry." she said, half angrily, half disbelieving.

"Oui. C'est moi." she turned towards Hermione, and for a moment, just the briefest moment she saw Harry behind those perfect eyes. It was difficult to hate someone who needed you as desperately, as Harry did.

"I know what the problem is, what you failed to consider..."

Harry's expression turned sad, though even now, it was still pouting gorgeously. She spared a glance to Ron who was being quieter than usual.

With a sigh, she explained, "The polyjuice, it's being enhanced."

"Enhancé, mais comment..." her voice was seductive and desperate. Hermione felt an attraction that she was quite uncomfortable to feel. It wasn't towards Harry, it was towards this perfect specimen.

Blushing, she continued, "You forgot, Fleur is quarter Veela, and her magic is extending the duration of the polyjuice."

She smiled, "You might be like this for quite some time..." She tried to sound sad, or disappointed, or upset. But having this ravishing beauty around would be pleasant. Besides, she told herself firmly, Harry would never learn if he didn't have to face the consequences of his actions.

Then Hermione played her trump card.

"You need to remember, that at the moment, you are Fleur."

Harry/Fleur nodded adorably.

"That means at the moment, you are part-Veela. And I suspect you haven't quite got the same control of things, the same experience as the real Fleur."

"Quoi?"

Hermione lowered her shoulders, and nodded towards Ron. "Your best friend, is fighting to hide his .... excitement, at ... at even sharing this table with you." Her voice turning snappish and sharp.

Ron turned from squirming in his chair, to turning beetroot coloured.

"Go on." she added, "Ask him for a dance, I dare you."

Fleur's face twitched she wasn't aware girls shared that type of information.

"Désolée... errr, Sorry Ron."

Despite the accent, a hint of the true Harry Potter snuck through, and the spell broke. Ron gave a little smile, but quickly shifted his gaze from Fleur's face to the more comfortable one of his friend Hermione.

125332112121322213.content:
"No... Not Snape." The objection came out in a hurried French breathy voice. It's seductive tone, even in protest tugging at Ron's libido.

"You 'av too..." Hermione repeated, her voice taking an almost mocking French tone itself.

"Your magic, Fleur is helping keep you in that form."

She stopped, to make sure Harry was following her.

"Polyjuice helps you with your disguise, in your case, especially obvious with your accent."

Again Fleur pursed her lips, pouting gorgeously, before finally acknowledging, that her voice was enforced by the potion."

"You're struggling with English, the English that Harry grew up speaking, that's foreign to you Fleur."

Reluctantly, following Hermione's logic, Fleur again nodded. She hadn't even confessed that she was thinking in French.

Hermione sighed, "I read, of accidents with polyjuice. Where the person stayed polyjuiced for too long, and they ended up stuck in that body, transformed permanently into their enchanted form...."

Harry/Fleur, finally understood. But her mind still fought. "But... But, what about Mad-eye... when Barty Crouch Jr. was constantly downing polyjuice." Her voice trilled, hopefully, triumphantly.

Hermione continued, now on less certain ground, "I think, I don't know, but I think. He only drank it during the day. That while he slept it wore off, and in the morning he started afresh,... it was only for appearances. Multiple doses are safer than a longer, stronger dose."

She waved to the frightened Fleur opposite with a weak wave of her arm.

"I suspect that once your body burns through all the potion, then if Veela-magic is holding you in that form, then it won't return to your Harry form."

"Mais, vous... -merde-merde-merde- But, you don't know..." Harry's protest undermined by her own words.

Hermione exhausted from the argument, switched to her final hope, "Well, if you're happier like this, I'm sure you can find some of your own, old loose hairs on your pillow, and you can return to being Harry Potter. But... But, he doesn't have any inherent magic like the Veela to keep you in that form. You'd just be available for a brief visit, until you ran out of loose hairs."

That struck home. She could see the colour drain from the perfect Fleur face. As she nodded her agreement.

"So we go and see Snape." Hermione insisted, fixing Harry/Fleur with her most studious expression.

Her grip on Hermione's hand tightened, and she was in the rare situation of wanting to come across Professor Snape.

"I solemnly swear I am up to no good." she spoke the necessary words as she unfolded the parchment. They needed to find him as soon as possible.

1253322.content:
Harry was naturally suspicious of all Slytherin students. But what he'd discovered had to be confirmed. He couldn't just make wild accusations, without appearing mad or malicious.

There was only one way to gather more evidence, and that was to visit the Slytherin common room. He'd managed to get a stray hair from Pansy Parkinson. It would feel odd being inside a girl's skin, he thought. The upside would be he... she'd be able to ask Draco questions without raising suspicions.

At the moment she was at Hogsmeade, and should be away for the rest of the day. So Harry felt now was the time to act.

That was why he was secretly brewing polyjuice potion. He hid it from Ron and Hermione, because he knew the reaction... well the reactions they'd both have if he told them what he'd overheard.

His previous visit had confirmed the idea that neither Crabbe nor Goyle were given to questions or curiosity. Besides he was more likely to open up to Pansy. Although he hid his feelings, Draco craved Pansy's attention. That was something Harry could understand.

She wasn't demure or as smart as Hermione, but she did possess a gruff self-confidence, and judging from her marks on the notice board she was intelligent.

He dropped the hair into the polyjuice potion, and with a small -whuff- and pale cloud, the ingredients mixed.

Harry hid in a cubicle and slid the bolt across. It was cramped, but he didn't want to risk being discovered with a Slytherin uniform... a girls' Slytherin uniform....

Carefully he undressed and put his Gryffindor clothes to the side. Before he could change his mind, he raised it to his lips and quickly swallowed the contents. They tasted bitter, but not unpleasant. Like a strong herb... Harry was trying to work out which one, as a strand of hair fell across his eyes.

The magic continued to work, and soon he felt his face shifting and contorting. Touching his lips, they felt massive and the hair felt like an unwieldy cascade. He tried not to think of the other changes...

But as the weight built on his chest, his hands came forward instinctively and cupped the swelling breasts. He had to restrain himself from reaching lower, and with a gasp he felt his ass expand to a more feminine swell. It was a high pitched gasp, and she stepped away from the chill marble of the partition... the cause of her gasp.

As she felt the magic fade, she quickly got dressed, and ready to face the world.

Still tugging at the unfamiliar underwear, Harry left the cubicle. It pinched and pulled at her shoulders, but Harry went to the mirror.

http://img2.wikia.nocookie.net/__cb20110...

The effect was impressive, and Harry tugged at her uniform, she only had a limited time until the polyjuice wore off.
1253322.title: Pansy Parkinson

choices: Thankfully, Pansy... the real Pansy has skipped her detention.

"Hi, Harry.", she passes Luna along the way to the greenhouse.
12533223.content:
'Pansy' headed down through the castle, heading for the Dungeon and the Slytherin common room as quickly as she could. In her bag, an extra couple of samples of polyjuice chinked against each other. The vials ready to extend her time as Pansy, until she could learn the truth.

She only got as far as the ground floor before she felt a heavy hand land on her shoulder. Whirling around in fright, she fought to keep her gaze from focusing on the immense bosom of Pomona Sprout. As a girl it filled her eye line, and made her aware of her own bust. Her heart slowed back to it's regular pace. The fear that she'd been discovered by Pansy herself was Harry's greatest fear.

Despite her planning she hadn't been able to immobilise her, or even be sure where she was. It was a big castle, and Harry just hoped to not run into her... Not his best plan, but he felt rushed into this one, the consequences of not acting feeling like the bigger risk.

"I hope you haven't forgotten about your detention, young miss." Professor Sprout's voice filled with scathing contempt. Keeping her hand firmly on Pansy's shoulder, the pair marched to the greenhouses. A cold lump of dread building in Harry's gut, as she walked through the castle. The fear that at her destination, she'd be face to face with the real Pansy, a very real, very worrying possibility.

13.content:
Hogwarts was still mourning the loss of Dumbledore, and everyone was telling their favourite stories of the Headmaster. Sometimes it was a small act of kindness, a sweet offered at a timely moment. For others it was sage word of advice that helped them make a choice with a difficult decision. The students swapped their own stories, even as they heard of the younger days of the most powerful wizard of the age.

Harry was staying away from large crowds, there were too many painful... too many fresh memories... Too many people asking him for his favourite memory of Dumbledore. He even made the effort to escape the comforting words of Ron and Hermione. They meant well, and obviously they too were feeling the loss. But he needed some time to be alone.

That was how Harry came to be walking through a less explored part of the castle. Seeing the size of some of the cobwebs he was sure that Ron's eyes would have been darting around nervously. His steps faltered, as he walked passed a door. It didn't belong there, it just didn't fit with the shape of the corridor, and even in his distracted mood, he was sure it wasn't there a few moments ago.

It had to be the Room of Requirement, he wasn't sure quite what thoughts or desires had summoned it up. But Harry wasn't about to ignore it. He was mildly disappointed when the door didn't creak as he pushed it open. The heavy oak swinging aside smoothly if slowly.

Taking one last look to either side; nobody there. Harry entered and closed the door behind him.
13.title: Harry needs some peace and quiet.

choices: Harry watches as further changes happen to his reflection.
He decides it is best to get his friends as witnesses.
A spectral whisper emanates from the surface of the mirror.
132.content:
As Harry got closer, he thought it looked slightly different. Something about the stand... It was hard to tell with the cloth covering it.

He just wanted a quick look at his family. Telling himself, he had kept his promise to Dumbledore... He hadn't sought it out... But when it was near enough to touch, Harry sighed... it was a different mirror.

Pulling the dust sheet away, it slid down the glass and slithered onto the floor, forming a loose pile around Harry's feet.

There was writing around the frame of the mirror, and Harry ran his fingers over them just enjoying the texture. When he looked at his reflection he was disappointed. It was simply himself, and the rather unkempt room behind him.

Twisting this way and that, he tried to see any magical qualities in the glass. It was only when he leaned in close, to examine his face that he saw anything. The scar was still there... the glasses were still there... but... his in the mirror his hair was tidier.

He stood back upright as he tried to work out the purpose of the mirror. Was it meant to flatter the owner... or to inspire them to better present themselves... or was it instructing him...

Perhaps Hermione would be better able to work out it's purpose, or Ron would see something different. In either case, Harry wanted to consult with his friends, if he found this room again... and if he didn't no harm done.

Harry almost stumbled into the mirror with his feet caught in the dustcloth at his feet. But found his balance before the worst happened. He shook his head at his reflection and carefully stepped out of the tangled cloth.

Something about the reflection was different... again.

If he hadn't shaken his head he might not have noticed. His reflection had longer hair, it was just short of his shoulders, and even as he looked it took on a tidier aspect.

Shifting from a chestnut mess, reminding him vaguely of a lion's mane. It shifted into a tidier curls... similar to Hermione's but shorter.

Running a hand over his head, he confirmed that the reflection wasn't real. It looked realistic, but Harry's hair simply wasn't that long.

Frankly it was starting to freak Harry out. The changes ... and not knowing the nature of the mirror ... well ... both brought their concerns. The mirror could be a Slytherin artifact... But Harry didn't want to just flee because he was afraid. That wasn't a Gryffindor characteristic. Courage was feeling the fear and still doing the right thing... He'd heard that somewhere.
132.title: Harry spots the mirror of 'Erised', and...

choices: As Harry watches, the mirror image starts mouthing words to him.

"Harry! You in here?" a familiar voice cried out.
1321.content:
As he looked deeper in the mirror, he saw that his forehead looked different. The scar that had marked him, that had defined him as 'The Chosen One', it was ... it was fading. Harry ran his pinky over it. In the real world it was still there, only his reflection in the mirror world lacked that curse.

Pulling his focus out wider, he spotted that his whole face looked cuter now. His nose just a little smaller, a little more refined. He was feeling worried in his distracted state, his reflection now had hair that fell past his shoulders. The styling was more elegant, and clearly belonged on a girl... on a woman. It would have looked incredible framing Ginny's face, either in her natural copper colouring or in this rich chestnut.

As he mused about what was happening, Harry saw his reflection with fresh eyes. It looked more androgynous now. Even as that thought entered Harry's mind, his mirror lips plumped up, and took on extra colour. It changed his whole look, it brought out how high and defined his cheekbones were. With a snort Harry recalled an overheard conversation, when he'd been wearing his invisibility cloak. Apparently, high, well-defined cheekbones were incredibly desirable.

With the evidence of his own eyes, Harry couldn't help but agree. In the mirror there wasn't a cute girl looking back, it was a beautiful woman. Harry caressed his cheek with the back of his hand the entrancing image looked so much more attractive, as she mirrored his actions.

Gasping Harry stumbled backwards a step, he took in not just the face, but the whole of the mirror. Pouting back at him from the mirror was a divine beauty. The Gryffindor lion on her jumper hinting at the gentle swell of her breasts. Harry's right hand went to cup one breast, only to find nothing there.

His left hand slid lower, towards his trousers... her mirror skirt.

Everything was still intact. He gave a huff, as he released the breath he didn't know he'd been holding.

She continued to mouth words, and Harry grew ever more attached. He had to hear the words. But nothing he knew about the Mirror could explain this. Could the mirror host another world? Part of him needed to touch the mirror. Another needed to run, and run far
13211.title: As Harry watches, the mirror image starts mouth...

choices: Join
Stay
132111.content:
Harry touched the mirror, and the girl who was him grabbed his hands, guiding him through the mirror. "Hello Harry." She said with a gorgeous smile. She looked much like Lily, except for small features of his father. But they only enhanced the beauty. "I know you've been oh so sad lately, with the death of Dumbledore. We can merge though, and I can take you to my world. Not everything is the same though. This is the start of your year, before Dumbledore died." She said.

Harry was entranced by her, and by the news. He had a second chance. But part of Hermione had rubbed off on him, and he knew he should ask questions.He wanted a second chance. He wanted to save Dumbledore. "Tell me more about your world."

"In this world, you are me. Born a girl, Lillian Rose Potter. You'll gain all my memories, but at the cost of your own." Girl Harry said, "By coming through the mirror, though, you can't get back out the same way." She said.

Harry knew his only options were to spend his days, seeking another way, or merging with Lillian. The first wasn't an option. He had zero idea of how this place worked. But part of him still wanted answers. When he was about to ask the questions, he was stopped by Lillian.

"I can't spend much longer here Harry, join or stay."
132111.title: Touch the mirror

choices: Lillian is the hero
Lillian betrayed Harry, in her time she's a discple of Voldemort
1321111.content:
Harry weighed his options against each other. He felt horrible for even thinking of leaving behind his friends, his godfather and mentors, and fleeing from Dumbledore's death. But the weight of his greatest mentor weighed on him. Lillian was right, he was miserable. Sirius was still on the run, he had to face another years of Dursley's, and the Ministry was incompetent as ever. All he did was weigh down his friends, weigh down the true hero's, He had a right to be selfish damn it, hadn't he done enough? Four encounters with Voldemort, the death of Cedric, a year with Umbridge.

Harry took Lillian's hand, and she smiled softly at him. "Take me to your world, please, let me live your life. Maybe I can fix things, maybe you aren't the hero, and I won't have to lift a finger, I can just live my life." Harry said. He wished to know all about this world he was entering, but Lillian claimed there wasn't much time, and he trusted her word. Lillian pulled him forward, the mirror acting as a portal between worlds. She kissed him to have their bodies merge, and Harry had t admit his final kiss as a man was brilliant.

Lillian awoke with a start, blood pumping, she was nervous, scared, why? She looked at the mirror and remembered. Something strange had happened, a portal had opened up. She had almost been pulled in by a mysterious force, surely trapping her inside. With a shiver she ran to the nearest girls bathroom to look at herself in the mirror
1321111.title: Join

choices: Pursue Draco
Focus on someone else
13211112.content:
Hazel eyes, long auburn hair, no glasses needed, no scar, Lillian appreciated herself in the mirror. Of course she had no knowledge of any glasses or scar on her. In her life Voldemort had not tried to kill her, nor her parents. James Potter had married a Pureblood, lost contact with his old Hogwart's friends as he took on the role of the head of a noble house. She had been raised in Pureblood society, and recently had met the Dark Lord himself. She wore long sleeved clothes to hide the Dark Mark on her forearm. Her robes were black, emerald and silver, the colors of Slytherin of course.

Lillian made her way to class as something strange happened. Memories recalled to her of meeting a boy like her, but with black hair and green eyes. She recalled being trapped in the mirror, the only release to convince some poor fool to merge with her and let her out. So that was why she had been so scared, why her heart was racing when she left the mirror room. She giggled to herself as she recalled the bullshit she peddled to convince him. Save Dumbledore, what a load of shit. Her task along with Draco Malfoy's was to take the headmaster out of commission, by any means necessary. Thinking about Malfoy delighted her, the boy was handsome, smart, and his confidence made her quite like the boy. If only that tart Pansy Parkinson would get out of the way. The tart had a on again off again relationship with Draco, and Lillian was considering taking Malfoy for herself.

She thought to herself, was Draco worth the trouble? Should they remain friends and seek out other relationships? Or should she wrap the boy around her finger and make herself Queen of Slytherin house? (Holy shit the people here are really generous with gift points)
13211112.title: Lillian betrayed Harry, in her time she's a...

choices: Pansy is caught with Crabbe and Goyle
Pansy is caught with Nott and Zabini
132111121.content:
Lillian decided she wasn't going to sit back and let Parkinson win. She was raised to be ambitious, ruthless, and above all to find herself a proper marriage with a good Pureblood boy. Surely Father would approve of her gaining Draco Malfoy, and she would reap the benefits as well. She coped by imagining herself in Pansy's place, being the one Draco was kissing, surely even fucking. She just needed a plan. First of all, she spent weeks getting closer with Draco, and letting herself fall in love and enjoying the moments. She told him when he was being an idiot, and laughed when he was actually funny.

That was all well and good, but it seemed like Draco still saw her as a friend and partner. She worked on the vanishing cabinet with him, communicating any setbacks and progress to the Dark Lord's Lieutenants. A lot was riding on the vanishing cabinet, as Voldemort's right and left hands were respectively, Draco's father, and Draco's maternal aunt. What she didn't know was that Draco was waiting for the right time to break up with Pansy. His father had recommended he seek out a better positioned wife, and who better then his fellow new Death Eater, Lillian Potter?

"I'll admit her father and I didn't get along in school, but we've been in touch recently, and he's open to the idea. Miss Parkinson is nice, but her options after school are limited, her family isn't in the greatest of positions, and the fact is Pansy is only there for the power and prestige. When you arrive at school, take note of how Pansy treats you, and how Lillian treats you."

With Lillian knowing zero of this, she set out to ruin Pansy, and to kill three birds with one stone, rid Draco of Crabbe and Goyle, or was it Grabbe and Coyle? She never quite got it right. The two were idiots, to stupid to become Death Eaters. Draco had two friends already to fill the void, Theodore Nott and Blaise Zabini. The silent pale Nott and fiery olive Zabini were polar opposites, yet somehow best friends, and good friends to Draco. Both were intelligent, and had been made into Death Eaters like her and Draco.

Then again, Pureblood society was extremely cutthroat. Might it be better to eliminate two potential rivals and leave Thing #1 and Thing #2 in place?
132111121.title: Pursue Draco

choices: Lillian remembers
Lillian forgets
1321111211.content:
It was the easiest choice she'd made in years. It would utterly humiliate Pansy to be caught with those two, and she had confidence that Draco could easily deal Nott and Zabani should they turn against him. Lillian began making the preparations right away, easily convincing Crabbe and Goyle to follow her. "Grabbe, Coyle, Draco wants to see you, now!" The two weren't intelligent enough to realise she'd yet again got the G and C mixed up and followed her. A few simple memory charms would have them hitting on Pansy. Now getting to Pansy was the harder part. It was no secret the two didn't get along. So that's where two love potions came in, with a friend of Pansy's "convinced" to put them in her drink.

The after effects were hilarious, as soon as Goyle and Crabbe began hitting on her, she smiled and fluttered her eyebrows at both of them. It didn't take much convincing to have them sneak off for a private area. Later that day after skipping a few classes, the three were caught in an empty classroom with no clothes. It quickly spread around the school. Draco was said to be furious with his lackeys, and with his now ex girlfriend.
