He didn't even send them off in person, nor was he there when all three were suspended.

Giggling, Lillian set off, happily skipping her way to the Room of Requirement, to wait for Draco. She had almost made it to the vanishing cabinet when she heard a slow clap, turning around to see Draco was already there, a smirk on his handsome face. "Well played Lillian, you show every day why you're Slytherin's Queen." He said, walking over to her. "Why Draco, nice to see you! I have no idea what you're talking about. Shame about Pansy, and Grabbe, and Coyle.." She replied. "Crabbe and Goyle, and you don't need to act so coy. You've made your point quite clear. Me and you, Hogsmeade on the Weekend?" Draco said, pulling Lillian close to him. She could smell his aftershave, which made her smile up at him, intoxicated by his smell. "Oh Merlin Yes!" The two connected their lips for a long kiss, and that night they worked not as a team, but as a couple.

Later on, Lillian suffered nightmares all night. Something felt wrong, memories entered in her head, memories she knew were not her own. A red haired boy, a bushy haired girl and a black haired boy, with green eyes haunted her thoughts. When she looked in the mirror in her dream she swore she saw him, as her own reflection. This shouldn't be possible. Hadn't she tricked him to free herself from that blasted mirror? Why were his memories entering her own. "Did you think I would settle for your tricks? I'm going to get my body back you traitor."

Suddenly Lillian awoke, gasping for air in her room. She had just been asleep, it was morning already. What was she dreaming about?
1321111211.title: Pansy is caught with Crabbe and Goyle

choices: Pansy interferes alone
The Gryffindor's recruit Pansy for their scheme
13211112112.content:
(Lillian Potter: https://i.pinimg.com/originals/c0/44/26/...)

Lillian tried to remember what she was dreaming about, but the memory simply wouldn't come to her. It had been a nightmare though, she was sure of that. But it was no use dreading over a dream, especially one she couldn't remember at all. Last night had been perfect. Not only had she humiliated Pansy, but Draco had found out, was impressed, and invited her on a date before kissing her. Oh Merlin she was giddy after all that! Her timetable today had her start off in Potions with Gryffindor, how fun to ruin those dorks, especially when Professor Snape favored Slytherin's. She knew he had issues with her Father at school, but she had impressed early on, and shown she was not like James Potter.

Draco walked to class with Theo and Blaise by his side instead of Crabbe and Goyle. Those two had grown useless long before Lillian humiliated them, and it only served as an excuse to get rid of them. The two sat by themselves in classes now, getting by as hired muscle, though they didn't target any Slytherin's, they were made well aware they would regret it. Pansy was simply an outcast now, no House wanting a thing to do with her.

"Trust you to hook up with one of the hottest Slytherin's Draco!" Blaise said with a laugh. "Daphne has that angelic beauty, and big in the right places, but your new girl? Lillian isn't as big, nor beautiful. She's more pretty, that's not a downgrade for me, that's two different categories. She just has that 'girl next door' vibe you just can't beat!" Theo as usual put up with Blaise's antics, and spoke softly and not much. "Congratulations Draco." Draco rolled his eyes at Blaise. "Better not let Daphne or Lillian hear you say that, you'll end up small in all the wrong places." Draco said. Blaise simply laughed it off as the three entered Potions.

Lillian entered Potions not long afterwards, a seat having been saved for her next to Draco. "Your seat Madam!" Blaise said with a over the top bow and whirl, which got a giggle out of her. Neither Draco or Theo were keen to repeat Blaise's earlier remarks in fear of what the witch could do. She looked over at the Gryffindors, the rag tag mess they were. Bookworm Mudblood Hermione Granger, idiotic and annoying Ron Weasley, Clumsy yet cute Neville Longbottom were the main trio. They were usually flanked by the Irishman Finnigan, Mudblood Thomas, one half of the Patil Twins, and lastly Lyall Lupin Jr. They were a rag tag bunch, yet strangely she didn't despise them today. A small voice in the back of her head told her they were better people then herself and the Slytherins, but she shook it off.

Snape called the roll for the class, not enjoying the sight of Draco and Lillian sitting together. "Mr Malfoy, I suggest you do not become distracted by your new love interest. Any passed notes, or other lovey dovey things will not be tolerated. Understood?" "Yes sir, we understand." Draco replied. Class went as usual, with them sabotaging Gryffindor at every chance. Oddly though, Lillian felt bad for doing it, which had never happened. She shook off the feelings again and resumed focus on class. Afterwards she excused herself from the group and headed to a bathroom, washing her face with cold water. What in blazes was going on? The rest of the week passed with little fanfare. Sometimes Lillian dreamed that same nightmare, but would forget afterwards, angry at herself for not recalling.

By the end of the week, Lillian was to excited for her date with Draco to care about some forgotten dream. She and him would have the day to themselves. She spotted Pansy Parkinson but paid her no mind. Inside the three broomsticks, her face lit up when she saw Draco. The two embraced and kissed, Lillian yet again intoxicated by the smell of Draco's aftershave. "If you stop using that stuff by Merlin I'll kill you." She said. Draco wasn't sure if she was joking or not. "We'll take two butterbeers thanks!" He said to a waitress, and soon after they were enjoying themselves, promising to talk later about the cabinet.

However, trouble was brewing, and some people were considering interfering. Walking in the pub was Pansy, and in another corner were the Gryffindor's in an intense discussion over if they should get revenge.
13211112112.title: Lillian forgets

choices: Gryffindor Civil War
One side manages to convince the other
132111121122.content:
One Gryffindor in particular, Weasley beckoned Pansy over to the table, she was an outcast from all houses, and had an axe to grind against Slytherin especially. The enemy of my enemy is my friend, and neither Pansy nor Gryffindor had no love lost for Lillian Potter. Sitting down, she was soon included in their heated debate. "It's to risky, and if Madame Rosmerta sees a thing we might get Gryffindor banned from the Three Broomsticks, let alone how many points we would lose!" Hermione said, trying to be the voice of reason. "Who says we have to do it here? Who says it has to be today? We just invited a Slytherin to our table, why not think like one? Going in brave won't do a thing, we'll only get in trouble!" Said Neville, surprising many of them. "We hide in the shadows, and take out our targets one by one. We isolate them, follow them in secret somewhere private and hit them!"

Lillian and Draco were seated alone, Blaise and Theo standing nearby on guard duty. "So there we were, me, Blaise and Theo, staring down a dragon!" Draco said, suddenly interrupted, "A dragon?! If you're going to brag Draco at least be realistic" Lillian said with a giggle. She didn't mind Draco bragging though, it was part of his charm. "Okay maybe it wasn't a full grown dragon, but we faced it down, our fathers providing no help as we had to prove our manhood to them! Suddenly it came at us, barely missing us as we shot spell after spell at it, but nothing worked. But I bravely ducked underneath, risking my own life to save my friends, and hit the Dragon right in the..." This time Theo interrupted, rolling his eyes at Draco. "It was a juvenile Welsh Green, and we got nowhere near it." Draco rolled his eyes right back. "Excuse you Theo, I'm trying to impress my gorgeous new girlfriend here!"

Lillian simply laughed. "You don't need to impress me Draco, you already got the girl! Though flattery I certainly don't mind. If we're going to be telling stories, let me tell you about this time my father and I went to America, and I managed to talk with a Horned Serpent! We were out hiking in the wilderness, and had gotten lost, and for some strange reason our wands just wouldn't point the right way. Father thought we were done for when a Horned Serpent rose from the water, we must have been in it's territory. To calm it down, I used you know what, and managed to get directions back to the town."

Gryffindor began to split, their argument having reached it's climax. "No! I will have no part in this! You fools will only get in trouble, and we'll all be blamed for it! If we weren't friends, I would be talking to Professor McGonnogal and Headmaster Dumbledore right away!" Hermione said. Dean, Lyall, and Pavarti all backed her up, While Ron and Seamus sided with Neville and Pansy. "You four are the fools here! Slytherin need to be taken down a peg! And the only way we can do it is by using their own tactics against them!"

"My mother and father would never approve of such tactics, and neither would your Grandmother Neville! Stop this silly scheme of yours before she sends you a howler!" Lyall said. "I doubt Mrs Weasley would approve either Ronald! Is this what you want, shame on your entire family!? Is this the example you want to set for your sister?!" said Pavarti "Don't you dare bring family into this! No one needs to know, and no one will know!" Ron said, his face redder then his hair. "Ron's right, no one needs to know! Unless you go and tattle no one will know!" Said Seamus. "Maybe we should go and tell someone. It might be the only way to stop this mad plan of yours!" Dean replied. No one saw who drew first, or who shot first, but soon they had a extremely angry Madame Rosmerta on their hands. "OUT! ALL OF YOU OUT! SHAME ON GRYFFINDOR! MINERVA AND ALBUS WILL HEAR ABOUT THIS!" She screamed, using a spell to throw each and every one of them out. The two factions raced towards Hogwarts to convince others to join their side.

"What the bloody hell was that about?" Lillian asked, blinking. "You know Gryffindors, short tempers, red hair, freckles, great kissers." Blaise said, then realizing what he had just said. "Oh my, had someone been seeing the Weasley girl Zabini? I knew you liked feisty girls but I didn't know you liked them that feisty!" Draco teased, all four of them soon forgetting about the incident.
132111121122.title: The Gryffindor's recruit Pansy for their sc...

choices: Put the book back
Read further
1321111211221.content:
"Launching spells at one another is bad enough, but to do it in The Three Broomsticks?! Have you any idea the amount of damage you could have caused, the harm you could have caused!? You should all be ashamed of yourselves. And for refusing to explain yourselves, detention for the rest of the year! Each one with a different Professor! Be you are among the school best and brightest, or all of you would have been expelled! We were in the lead for the House Cup, now we will be coming dead last for the rest of the year! 5000 points from Gryffindor! Professor Snape has authorized me to take away 625 points from Slytherin because of you Miss Parkinson. Now, get out of my sight and so help me if any of you have to come back here, you'll be scrubbing cauldrons for Professor Slughorn for the rest of your time at Hogwarts! Dismissed!" Minerva McGonnogal was extremely angry at her students right now. She'd been ropeable when Madame Rosmerta came to her, extremely angry and explained the disturbance at her Pub. She rubbed her head, sighing. She meant her threats, and as much as she was loath to take away house points, she needed to drive the point home. Maybe a bottle of Ogden's Firewhiskey would do her good at such a time.

"I am incredibly ashamed at each and every one of you. You should all have known far better then this!" Professor Dumbledore said. Somehow his disappointment was worse then McGonnogal's anger, "Should any of you find yourselves in trouble ever again, there will be a hearing to discuss whether you are suited to stay at Hogwarts or not. Should you be allowed to stay, as Professor McGonnogal said, you will help Professor Slughorn clean his cauldrons, and I can assure you these cauldrons will be quite filthy. Now all of you, back to your common rooms. I expect your parents will want to talk to you. Please keep any howlers private.

Gryffindor had split into two factions, neither of whom were willing to back down. Of the students who had not been at The Three Broomsticks. Euan Ambercrombie, , Ritchie Coote, Jack Sloper, Andrew Kirke, Romilda Vane and Cormac McLaggen all joined Neville's faction, while Katie Bell, Colin and Dennis Creevey, Jimmy Peakes, Demelza Robins, and surprisingly Ginny Weasley joined Hermione's. "Gin why the hell are you in with them! I'm your brother! Get over here now!" Ron complained. "Because Ronald, you're acting like an idiot once again. I'm 15, not a child, I can make my own choices, and Hermione is right! Stop bossing me around or I'll Bat Boogey you!"

While Gryffindor was coming to blows, Lillian and Draco worked on the vanishing cabinet. "Why won't this bloody thing work! We've spent months on it and I feel like we're no closer. How do these things work anyway?" Draco simply shrugged, and Lillian rolled her eyes. "Some help you are. Cmon, let's get to bed. We'll get it working eventually." She said. Of late, she had been distracted. Sometimes when she looked in a mirror, she swore she saw Harry somewhere behind her. She mustn't have been getting enough sleep.

Neville just missed the two leaving the Room of Requirement, seeking it for some private time. Inside as he walked around, many things caught his attention. A cage containing a dead magical creature, various books and scrolls, and even a tiara. But something stood out for some reason. It was an old copy of a Potion's book. Slughorn must have noticed it, and put it here. Inside, there was a signature. "Property of The Half Blood Prince. Wonder who that was?"

Everyone stared at the other, in silence
1321111211221.title: Gryffindor Civil War

choices: Harry makes Lillian forget about him, but not Ginny
Harry makes Lillian forget about Ginny, but not himself
13211112112212.content:
(It only just popped into my head that without Harry, Neville is the Chosen One in this world. I explained a bit of this before, but in case you missed it: James Potter marries a fellow Pureblood instead of Lilly Evans. Because of this, Lillian is born instead of Harry, at a different date, so the prophecy doesn't apply to her, James is never killed, and the Longbottoms are killed instead. James becomes prominent in Pureblood society and the wizarding business world. I'm also changing some years around because I don't like, nor think it makes much sense how late in life Fleamont supposedly had James)

Neville was intrigued by this Potion's book. He thought the handwriting was familiar, but he couldn't place it. Flipping through the pages, notes written in the margin, crossed out lines, and even spells caught his attention. Whoever had owned this was confident in their abilities, and their knowledge to correct the book on potion work. He thought about putting it back, but decided not to. This would come in handy for him. He made sure to memorize the spells, especially Sectumsempra. The description said for enemies, and he had an idea which enemy he wished to use it on.

Lillian and Draco again were working on the vanishing cabinet, and finally seemed to be getting somewhere. It would take until the end of the year by her estimations, but then they could set their plan into motion, and let Death Eaters infiltrate Hogwarts. Ironic considering these things largely sold on the backs of escaping from Death Eaters. "So, who are you taking to Slughorn's party?" Draco asked. After an incident where the Dark Lord had revealed himself, and Lucius Malfoy had been outed as a Death Eater, Draco had lost a potential invite to the 'Slug Club'. Just saying the name made her feel dirty.

"I'm taking you, idiot." She said as if it were the most obvious thing in the world. "I don't have an invitation." He replied. He knew he'd be going, he simply wanted to see what scheme Lillian came up with to get him in. "You forget, my father owns quite a few companies. He recently bought back Grandfather's old Sleakeazy Hair Products, a company I know for a fact supplies Slughorn anti balding products. With some generous discounts on that and potion supplies, if he shops with us, I think old Slughorn will be happy to welcome you to his party." Lillian explained. "And this is why I love you, you're the perfect scheming Slytherin." Draco said pulling her close, and kissing her.

Things started to get heavy, and their kissing get more intense, only for Blaise to enter from guard duty, clearing his throat. "Bloody hell Malfoy, you work fast. Curfew is coming fast. I suggest you two do your prefect rounds and go to bed. Me and Theo can't stand here all night." He said. With a sigh, Draco and Lillian stopped work for the day, doing their rounds and going to bed. Classes were relatively uninteresting, mostly theory about complicated spells they had to learn. Something did catch her eye though. Longbottom was suddenly a Potion's whiz. Previously he was a bumbling idiot, who was only in this class because Slughorn had lower standards. Yet now he was excelling, and he had even won a bottle of Liquid Luck she had been hoping to use.

Potions had been their last class that day, and the party was coming up soon, so Lillian set her plan into action. "Professor, I've been meaning to speak to you. You see I wish to bring my boyfriend to your party. Only issue is he doesn't have an invitation. Would you be so kind to give him one so I can bring him?" She asked. "Certainly, I'm afraid I don't pay much attention to gossip, who is he?" Slughorn asked, looking at her with a smile ready to write his name down. "Draco. Draco Malfoy." She answered, and as expected, the smile dropped from his face. "No. I will not invite the son of a Death Eater to my party, no matter what!"

"I thought you might say that Professor. By the way, is that Sleakeazy Hair Loss Prevention I smell? My grandfather came up with the original recipe you know. Of course, he sold the company when he retired, but recently my Father bought it back. If you were to.. reconsider my request, I can talk with my father, and you will find you personally will have a discount on all items sold by Potter owned brands." Lillian said, with a smirk on her face. Suddenly he chuckled, and wrote down an invitation. "Your family has been in Gryffindor for years. I taught your father, your grandfather Fleamont too, and went to school with your Great Grandfather Henry. All as Gryffindor as they come. But you my dear, I see the hat chose well putting you in Slytherin." He said, handing her the invite. "Thank you Professor. I'll send an owl to my father right away."

Lillian did good on her word, sending an owl to her father right away. When leaving, she ran into Longbottom. "Oi, Longbottom. What's with the sudden skill in Potions?" She questioned, suspicious of his sudden success, and jealous. "None of your business Potter." He replied, walking off suddenly. To bad she couldn't get anywhere close to him. Or could she? She recalled the young Weasley girl, and wondered if there was a way in there. But something in her mind seemed to want to stop her, trying to divert her thoughts elsewhere to other topics. She had no idea why, and she struggled to get her mind back on the task. That's when she connected the dots in he mind. The dreams, the images in the mirror. Harry still existed in her mind, he was trying to stop her. Suddenly he tried to wipe her memory of remembering him at all.
13211112112212.title: Read further

choices: Qudditch Starts
Mass Azkaban Breakout
Free Space, your pick here
132111121122121.content:
Harry wasn't quite sure who he was, or even what he was. He didn't know how to describe himself, and he had almost no memories, just vague senses that he had to protect certain people like Ginny, and so he tried to steer Lillian away from the red head. He quickly scrambled however when Lillian noticed he was there. He didn't even know how, but he erased the memory of him being in her mind, but couldn't do it again for the girl's plan with Ginny.

In real time, Lillian spaced out for a moment. What had she just been thinking about? Oh yes, her plans with Ginny. She set about finding the girl, and set a few of the Slytherin girls to be on the lookout for her, and to isolate her. One afternoon after class, she finally got her hands on the girl.

"Weasley. Ginny. I'd like to talk with you a moment." Lillian said, crossing her arms "Whatever Ron and Neville did, I swear I had no part in it!" Ginny squealed in a panic. Lillian only looked at her with confusion. "They haven't done a thing as far as I know. I was wondering if you'd like to spend a girls day out in Hogsmeade next weekend. With Pansy kicked out we need another member for our group, and I think you'd be a perfect fit."

Ginny was shocked to say the least. Lillian Potter was inviting her to join her clique? She really should say no, but she thought about how isolated in Gryffindor right now. Lavender had been undecided at first, but had joined Ron, while Pavarti was busy a lot, hanging out with her twin Padma. Hermione was nice, but isolated herself as well, and Luna was nice, but sometimes she got really fucking weird. She thought about how her brother and Neville had their own Slytherin, and so it was decided. "You know what? Sure, I'll hang out with you." Ginny said with a smile.

Lillian celebrated in private. She now had a chance to pry Weasley away from Gryffindor, maybe even pull in that weird Lovegood kid while she was at it. She wondered what Ginny's brother and Longbottom were planning, but dismissed it. They weren't important right now. She made sure all the other Slytherin girls knew that Ginny was coming that Saturday, so it was no surprise when Ginny showed up, unsure of herself but still taking a seat.

Ginny was having second thoughts, but it was a bit late now as she stood in front of her potential new friends. Lillian was there, as well as Daphne Greengrass, Daphne's little sister Astoria and Tracy Davis. "Welcome Weasley, hope you enjoy your time her, I'll be happy to help you get adjusted." Daphne said smiling. "Obviously you know Lillian and I. Astoria is my little sister, and Tracy is a friend of ours."

Ginny greeted them all, and settled in. As the conversation went on, she actually found herself enjoying it. The girls were easy to talk to, and they seemed to have quite a few things in common. They talked boys, fashion, makeup. "I hate to ask, but I have to, what's Malfoy like as a boyfriend?" Ginny asked. It was a common talking point among Gryffindor, she just hoped she didn't cross a line. It must have showed on her face because Astoria giggled. "Oh don't worry Weasley. Every girl wants to know that question!"

Lillian sighed, but she still smiled, she relished a chance to describe Draco. "He's a good kisser, incredibly sweet when he wants to be, and an absolute braggart. He started telling this story about fighting a dragon to prove his manhood, and it was the biggest load of shit I've ever heard. Theo pulled his head in and said it was a just a regular old Welsh Green, and they got nowhere near it! He's an immature brat sometimes, but he's my mature brat. Merlin knows he needs someone to pull his head in. If he wasn't a Slytherin I swear he and those twin brothers of yours Ginny would have gotten along like a house on fire." She said with a laugh.

Ginny laughed as well. "If we're telling tales I have one. We once had a gnome infestation in our garden. My mum asked Fred and George to get rid of them one day, and they haggled with her and got some pocket money for it. So then they go into the garden, and instead of getting rid of them, they haggle with the Gnomes to mess with Mum! So then they go back and forth until Mum and Dad just got Bill and Charlie to do it. Only instead of doing it regularly, they made a whole competition to see who could throw one the farthest!" Ginny's tale had the whole group in stitches.

"You think that's funny, Daphne learned how to apparate last summer, Mother and Father were proud, not as proud as Daphne though." Astoria said, Daphne blushing as the tale began. "So she begins apparating all over the place. And you know how you have that loud crack every time? It scared everyone. She wanted to ask Father something, so instead of just walking up the stairs, she appartes there, and scares the wits out of Father, ink everywhere. She did it at dinner time, and and House Elves kept dropping plates because of the loud cracks every time. Every time someone would go to bed, she'd wake up the whole house because she'd apparte into each room. Father got so sick of it he put anti apparation jinx on the house!" Once again the group was in hysterics except Daphne, who was red with embarrassment.

Tracy began her own tale. "One time Mr Nott, that's Nott Sr, not our Nott, came to our home to discuss business. I had just learned those silly jinxes like leg and tongue locks. I kept hitting poor Mr Nott with them without being seen. He'd be about to get up for something and fall over, or saying something when his tongue locked. Eventually Mother found me and had me confess. He wasn't even mad though, he thought it was impressive, and on the spot arranged a betrothal between me and Theo."

"Well, let's talk about the time Astoria tried flirting with each male Hogwarts teacher. In her first year!" Daphne said. "Oh come on Daph, I'm sorry, can we not talk about this?" Astroia begged, but Daphne smirked. "Oh it's far to late for that sister. Her first class was Defense, and all the gossip was in the common room was about how Astoria tried flirting with Lupin to try and get out of seeing the Bogart! He tried to be a sport about it and shut her down gently. But oh god I wish I was there to see Snape's face when she tried it in Potions! She tried it with Hagrid of all people, and I don't doubt she would have done it to Kettleburn as well if he were there. Professor Flitwick sent her to Dumbledore, where she tried the EXACT same thing!"

Now it was Astoria's face that was red. "I hope you ladies aren't laughing about us." A voice said behind them. Draco, Theo and Blaise had shown up, but not in time to hear a word of what they were saying. "No, but next time we totally will." Lillian said to another round of laughs. "How about we escort you girls to Honeydukes? I'd especially like to take Weasley, if the cute red head doesn't mind?" Blaise said, flirting with Ginny, who quickly responded and took his arm. Daphne left with some Ravenclaw she found cute, Astoria with a Slytherin boy in her year, and Tracy with Theo.

That left only Draco and Lillian. "Enjoy yourself Potter?" He asked, letting her ride on his back. "I did, and I may have made fun of you a little bit." Lillian said. She jumped down, and to her excitement Draco gently pushed her against a wall for some leverage. "And what did you say about me?" He asked, leaning in for a kiss. Lillian kissed him, and spoke in between. "Well, I said you were sweet." "Sweeter the Honeydukes chocolate." Draco replied. Another kiss. "I said you were a huge braggart." Lillian said, Draco laughing. "You're not wrong. And about my kissing ability?" He replied, kissing her again. "Not decided just yet. I'm going to need more and longer examples to find out." She said, wrapping her arms around his neck as they kissed yet again, but for much longer.

Neville was disgusted at the sight of Potter and Malfoy kissing. He knew in his heart they were both Death Eaters, and were planning something, but he couldn't do a thing to them in public. He walked into Honeydukes, sickened to see Ginny hanging out with Zabini. "What are you playing at?" He asked her storming up to her. "Oi oi oi! Out you! Rosmerta warned me about you, I'm not having you cause trouble in my shop. Leave them alone or McGonnogal will hear of it!" The owner declared. Neville angrily bought a few items and left for a planned meeting at the Hogs Head.

Ginny would have to deal with Neville later on, but right now she was loving to spend time with Blaise. "I've never been able to afford this many sweets my whole life!" Ginny said, amazed at what Blaise was willing to buy for her. After this kiss, Draco and Lillian walked in, with Draco noticeably having a few lipstick stains on his face and neck. He decided to get revenge for his looks, and had Potter close her eyes. "Trust me, you'll love this sweet." He promised, having her bite down on it.

"Oh Merlin! Cockroach Cluster!? You wait Malfoy, you'll get yours!" Lillian said angrily to Draco, who was cracking up with laughter. "Oh god you actually fell for the Cockroach Cluster trick! I can't believe it!" Blaise and Theo shook their heads. They were to protect Draco from external threats, not his own girlfriend. Before they left, Lillian made a special order. "I'd like the biggest packet of Bertie Bott's Beans you have. But if you can, would you make sure every single one of them is an awful flavor?" The owner agreed, and she walked out with her purse a good bit lighter, but with the perfect revenge on Draco in mind. She knew the boy was stubborn enough to assume there must be a good flavor deep with it.

Sure enough after Lunch, Draco snacked on sweets, and made a face with each bean he had. "Surely there's a good flavor in here somewhere." He said, not connecting the dots between Lillian's gift to him, and the flavors. Lillian wondered how the rest of the week would go.
132111121122121.title: Lillian forgets again, but not about Ginny

choices: Azkaban Breakout
More Quidditch
Neville uses the spell
1321111211221211.content:
(Short chapter, but it advances the story somewhat, and I just need time to get my groove back)

Lillian and Draco were excited to see the House Qudditch cup starting once again. They were almost always the dominant team, with few able to stand in their way. Only expert seekers seemed to be their bane, and this year there were none as far as they knew. Charlie Weasley had graduated before their time, leaving Gryffindor struggling ever since. Hufflepuff with Cedric Diggory had been a competitive team, but he had graduated and moved onto the English Quidditch League.

Lillian had been playing Chaser as a backup for two years, before finally being moved onto the main squad in her fourth year. Draco of course had bought has way onto the team with Malfoy money, but he had dedicated himself and become an expert Seeker, and had been named Captain. The rest of the team was filled with various Slytherins. Roger Urquhart and Blaise Zabini were her fellow Chasers, with Crabbe and Goyle as beaters, who while technically outcast, outmatched anyone else in Slytherin for hitting power. 'Mean' Miles Bletchley in his final year played Keeper.

Their first match was against Hufflepuff, and almost everyone predicted a Slytherin blowout victory. A minor setback occured with the announcement of the commentator. "What!? How!? Lee Jordan graduated last year, what do you mean he's still commentator!?" Lillian raged at a first year delivering a message. Apparently the Board had hired him, as he drew in quite the crowd with his antics. Fortunately Lillian got Luna Lovegood, a good friend to Ginny, on commentary as well. She would be sure to invite the strange blonde girl next time she was out, something about the girls eyes told Lillian that Luna was smarter then people gave her credit for.

"Welcome everyone to another Quidditch Cup! To all my haters who thought that me graduating would be the end, too bad! Especially the beautiful Professor McGonnogal! I'm Lee Jordan, and the lovely blonde next to me is Luna Lovegood. Luna, a few words?" "I'd like to warn players about an infestation of Nargles in the dressing rooms and urge them to be careful." "I have no idea what my commentary partner just said, but let's start it off. For Slimy Slytherin, we have Urquhart, Zabini, Potter, Bletchey, Crabbe, Goyle, and the Captain, that git Malfoy!" "And for the Hufflepuff team, we have Summerby, Fleet, Bones, Abbott, Preece, Cadwaller, and with a large number of Nargles around him, having an argument with Cadwaller, Captain Smith!"

The game wasn't close. Hufflepuff only started to perform moderately well after Susan Bones hit a bludger right at their captain, after he spent most of the game arguing with and berating them. Smith was taken out on a stretcher, and replaced by another Hufflepuff. They may have lost, but Hufflepuff didn't care, it was clear Smith was no longer captain.
1321111211221211.title: Qudditch Starts

choices: Neville fires and hits Lillian
Neville firea and hits Draco
Neville walks away
13211112112212111.content:
(In this World Peter Pettigrew never became a Death Eater. With the Potters not opposed to Voldemort, there was no reason to recruit him)

One particular evening at breakfast, everyone was crowded around newspapers, each student awaiting their own delivery. The unthinkable had happened, and everyone was unsure of what to do. Teachers tried to keep the calm, while discussing the news amongst themselves. Today's Daily Prophet front page headline had the entire Wizarding world in shock.

DEMENTORS DEFECT! DEATH EATERS FREE FROM AZKABAN! MINISTRY IN CRISIS!


Draco and Lillian had their own papers, and could hardly believe the news. "Holy fucking shit. I can't believe it." Lillian said, hazel eyes showing her utter shock. "They actually did it." She felt like celebrating, but Draco, while composed, kept his fear hidden. The people being released were insane when they went in, the worst of the Death Eaters. He only remembered bits and pieces of his Aunt Bellatrix and Uncle Rodolphus, only that Bellatrix was even more sadistic then the Dark Lord, and Rodolphus was a violent alcoholic, capable of destroying entire communities in his drunken rages. The two were tailor made for each other. Was he truly doing the right thing, bringing these people into Hogwarts.

At the Gryffindor table, Neville's hands twitched, a silent fury building up inside him. Bellatrix Black. Rodolphus and Rabastan Lestrange. Bartemius Crouch Junior. They were the ones who had tortured his parents into insanity, before cruelly killing them and dumping the bodies where the world could see. Prized Aurors Frank and Alice Longbottom, just more fatalities of the war. He wanted to rage, to scream, to curse every single Slytherin, many of whom were no doubt celebrating.

All classes that day were tense, with Professors desperately trying to get on with their job, and students desperatly looking for any new information on the breakouts. Finally Professor McGonnogal lost her temper. "The next student to talk about the breakout in my class will have detention for the entire year. You are here to LEARN. Not to talk, now pay attention, especially you Mr Weasley!"

Even Hagrid was surprisingly tight lipped about the whole situation. "I ain't saying a thing, Dumbledore's orders ya hear!" He yelled out to students gathered around his hut. Eventually a staff meeting was called, and all students dismissed to the dorms. "I can't take much more of it Albus. I wish I could tell the kids anything, but we've got nothing!" Filius said, looking sad. Many of them had been personal friends to Frank and Alice.

"Someone needs to keep an eye on Longbottom! It's only a matter of time before he attacks one of my students!" Snape demanded. "Well perhaps if so many of them weren't the Death Eaters Youth League you wouldn't have such a problem Severus!" Pomona snapped back. "As I recall you were good friends with Rabastan and Rodolphus while you were all students!"

"Severus has changed! And someone does need to keep an eye on Longbottom!" Aurora Sinastra said, side eyeing Minerva. "If you have words Aurora speak your mind, or else keep your mouth shut and your head in the stars!" Minerva replied. Quickly the teachers had developed their entire feud, and kept arguing until Dumbledore rose. "Enough. Enough! I SAID ENOUGH!" Quickly all heads turned, to a extremely irate Dumbledore. "Children, all of you arguing like children! Fighting amongst ourselves will do nothing! All of you, return to your quarters, now!"

Dumbledore sat alone, trying to calm himself. He had never lost his temper in such a way before. He sent off multiple patronuses, all with a message. "The Order of the Phoenix is to meet at Number 12 Grimauld Place, this is an emergency meeting. All members and former members still living are to attend."

Remus sat in his cottage in the woods. He lived with simple means, far away from civilization. If he could he would have sent the love of his life away, and never had a child, but Lyall Jr was now attending Hogwarts, and Lilly Lupin nee Evans would never abandon her husband. They were shocked when a Phoenix Patronus appeared outside their door, calling on them to attend a meeting of the Order of the Phoenix.

James Potter weighed up his options. He had parted on bad terms with many of the members, and he was sure many of them probably considered him an ally to the Dark. He was at least going to attend the meeting, along with his wife, Alexia Greengrass, and see what Dumbledore had to say.

Augusta Longbottom leaped at the chance to attend. What they had done to her darling son Frank, and lovely Alice, she would never forget.

"Could you at least have asked me before Albus?" Said Sirius Black, living in Grimauld Place with yet another on again off again girlfriend, and his friend Peter Pettigrew. He wasn't angry though, and had a wry smile on his face. He wondered just who would turn up.

All across Britain, wizards and witches received messages, invitations. Some had never been members, and were now being invited. Others had been members, and were being called back into the fold.

Back at Hogwarts, Draco Malfoy received a letter, from the Dark Lord himself. He was upset with the delay, and in graphic detail he listed what he would do if the task failed to meet it's deadline. He ran, to where he didn't know, but he ran, somewhere private, somewhere he could just process all his thoughts, all that was happening. He failed to attend the rest of his classes that day, and Lillian began to get worried.

"Spread out and find him!" She commanded, and so Slytherin obeyed, it's students spreading out across the castle the search for Draco. It was Lillian who found him, alone in a deserted bathroom, crying, ground flooded with water as he had tried to use the noise of the taps to drown out his thoughts.

"Draco?" She asked softly, inching closer. "Draco what's wrong? I've been worried about you." She said. He looked at her, with bloodshot eyes. "I can't Lil. I can't do it anymore. I don't want to be a killer. If I fail... You die. Mother and Father die. He'll do it, he'll kill everyone I care about to punish me. You should leave. If we break up, if I'm distant, maybe he'll spare you" He said, falling to his hands and knees. Lillian picked him up, and shook him.

"We don't have to complete the task. If the Dark Lord comes after us, we'll kill him, because I will let NOTHING, stand between you and me! I will NEVER leave you! I love you! I always have, ever since we met in Madame Malkin's. I'll never forget that scrawny blonde boy, trying on robes, and how he was the perfect gentleman to a frightened girl that day, scared of something as foolish as leaving home to go to school! I'll never forget his stupid brags, and how he shared his own ice cream when the girl dropped hers."

Draco stared into Lillian eyes, and honestly felt like nothing could stop him. With her behind him, he felt invincible, like he could take on the world. Lillian believed in him, loved him, and that was all that mattered. "I love you to Lillian. I know we're betrothed already, but I need to do this, to say it to you personally. Even if we weren't betrothed, I'd damn the consequences and say this." Draco said, getting on a knee in the flooded bathroom.

It wasn't exactly the peak of romance, but Lillian didn't care. She knew exactly what Draco was going to say. "Lillian, will you marry me?" He asked. Quickly he was tackled to the floor and kissed. "Merlin you're a sentimental idiot." She said with a giggle. "Yes, I'll marry you."

The happy moment was broken up when an irate Neville Longbottom walked passed. He had heard shouts coming from this direction, and now he saw these two Death Eaters, kissing, no doubt celebrating the release of the prisoners. His hand twitched and reached for his wand. "What are you doing here Longbottom, move on before we clear you off. I'm trying to spend time with my fiance." Draco said, leering at him, getting up off the floor.

"Come on darling he's not worth the time, let's just leave him." She said, intending to pull Draco away from the tense situation.
13211112112212111.title: Azkaban Breakout

choices: Lillian and Draco are given a new task
Draco is punished for his failure
Lillian and Draco defect
132111121122121111.content:
Seeing them try to walk away just made Neville angrier. He had a right to be angry, and he wasn't going to let these two Death Eaters just walk away without tasting his sting. "Suppose you two are celebrating the release of your friends and family from Azkaban huh? Throwing a big old party for them at your big manor? Should've known two Slytherin's like you were Death Eaters."

"We aren't Death Eaters Longbottom. Now please, let us leave. We weren't celebrating a thing except the relationship between me and Draco. We have no quarrel with you." Lillian said, trying to defuse the situation. "Shut your mouth Potter, you have no idea what I've been through, you have every quarrel with me! If you aren't a Death Eater show me your arm!" Neville demanded. "No! Fuck off with your delusions! I don't have to prove anything to you Longbottom, you aren't an Auror, you're a student."

Draco's worries and fear had turned to anger when seeing Longbottom's harassment. "Piss of Longbottom and leave us alone. Don't you have something better to do?" He said, rashly pointing his wand at Longbottom. Neville pulled out his own wand and the two stared at each other. "Draco, put that away!" Lillian said, taking Draco's hand and trying to force it down.

Unfortunately Neville chose to launch a spell just a moment before. "SECTUMSEMPRA!" He cried out in anger, before his eyes widened in the instant Lillian got in the way. Lillian didn't even make a sound, just fell to the ground, cuts spreading across her body. Blood stained the water as Lillian grew paler by the second. "LILLIAN!" Draco screamed, fallng to his knees, trying to figure out a way to stop the bleeding.

Neville looked on in horror. He had no idea what the spell had done. He assumed it would have hurt someone, but not kill them. It looked like people had heard the shouting that had gone on between them, as Professor's Snape and McGonnogal marched down the hallway angrily arguing. Draco ran out of the bathroom, hands soaked in Lillian's blood shouting. "Help! Please! Longbottom! Curse! Lillian!" He said, unable to get more then a single word out. The seriousness of his words and face conveyed the situation though, as the two rushed into the bathroom.

"Vulnera Sanentur! Vulnera Sanentur! Vulnera Sanentur!" Snape, serious faced, performed a healing spell, that seemed to reverse the damage done to Lillian. McGonagall angrily rounded on Longbottom. "Straight to the Headmaster boy! Consider yourself lucky if you are merely expelled and not arrested!" She said, marching him along. Snape continued to perform the spell, before picking up the girl in her arms. "Draco, inform Mr Potter of his daughters injuries via owl. Lillian must see Madam Pomfrey now." He said, marching off to the infirmary.

Draco did as asked, rushing to the owlery, sending a hastily written note to James Potter. He then rushed down to the infirmary, where he saw the staff off to the side, while Madam Pomfrey gave Lillian various potions, though the girl had still not awoken. Even those who had implied Lillian was a Death Eater looked ashamed, wondering if perhaps they had goaded Longbottom down this path.

"Whatever you've done Severus saved her life, there's no doubt about it. No spell I know of could have ever healed wounds like that. That is a serious dark spell." She said worriedly. "I've done what I can, St. Mungo's will have to take over from here." She said, sighing. She turned around and say Draco standing there. "What exactly is your relationship with Ms Potter, Mr Malfoy." She asked, though she could already guess. "L-Lillian is m-my betrothed." He said. He felt scared, cold.

"You'd better go with her then, you will need medical attention of your own I suspect." Madam Pomfrey finished, before sitting down, exhausted. "Best get ready, James Potter's on the war path." Argus Filch said, bursting in, shortly followed by Potter himself. "What the hell is going on!" He demanded, rounding on everyone in the vicinity. His eyes turned wide when he saw his daughter laying there, cold, pale.

"She's alive, and she'll live. If Severus hadn't come at the right time, she would have bled out on the floor." Pomfrey said, intervening before James lost his head. He turned, and looked Severus dead in the eyes. "I... Uh... Oh to hell with it. Thank you, Severus. From the bottom of my heart, thank you. I'm in your debt." He said, hugging the man. Snape was shocked and had no idea what to do. His hate for the man had subsided over the years, and he was clearly different from the bully he once was. Plus, neither had won the affections of Lily Evans in the end. She had chosen the werewolf Lupin.

"There is no need for affection Potter. Lillian is a fine student, and I would not have her die in such a way." Snape replied, with James sheepishly releasing him. "At least come around to Potter Manor for dinner sometime. It's the least I can do. I'll also organize something with my potion companies." He said, before turning on Albus. "I want him punished. If he wasn't Frank and Alice's son I'd demand he be arrested and expelled. Just punish him, and ask St Mungo's to contact me when my daughter wakes."

Finally, James turned to Draco. "Come with me Malfoy, I want to talk with you about my daughter." James said, leading Draco outside. "Is Mr Longbottom in my office? Good, I will see him privately." Dumbledore said, walking off. Severus and Minerva took their leave when the healers arrived, and once Draco came back into the room, they were taken to St Mungo's

"Neville, you will be serving detention each and every night with me, for the rest of your time at Hogwarts. Consider these private lessons, for you are the Chosen One, there is no doubt, it must be you who deals the killing blow to Voldemort." Dumbledore said. Voldemort had vanished from public life after a failed attack on Neville himself, though the world was still aware he was alive. After that the Death Eaters had collapsed, and fallen over one another to pretend they were on the side of good.

"You will be taught various spells and abilities, and things such as temperance, patience, and who your real enemies are. I had wished to ease you into this my boy, but you have forced my hand. There will be no more vigilante attacks on students, no more of this civil war within Gryffindor. Argus will be told personally that you are under my tutelage, and will be out past curfew. I expect you here, tonight, at 6 sharp. Am I understood?" "Yes, Headmaster sir." Neville said, somewhat ashamed at his actions.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ----------------------

Every moment seemed like a bad dream to Lillian. She remembered falling to the ground, cuts spreading around her body, and slowly bleeding out. She tossed and turn in her sleep, full of nightmares of death, of hers, her fathers, Draco's. She awoke with a start in a hospital bed, wearing a long sleeved gown. "Where am I?" She said fearfully. "I'm here, and so is Draco sweetheart." James Potter said, looking down at his daughter with concern. "You're in St Mungo's. She got hit with a nasty curse. But you're alive, that's all that matters."

"D-Draco?" She said weakly, smiling when he stepped from the shadows. Knowing both of them were there comforted her, and soon she drifted back to sleep. At that moment, a rather large, older man stepped into the room. He was clean shaven, with his graying blonde hair slicked back "The Dark Lord sends his regards to young Miss Potter. He would like to meet her as soon as possible." The man said.

"I know you. Corban Yaxley, you're a Death Eater." James said, before the mans words processed in his mind. "Wait what? What does Voldemort want with my daughter Yaxley!?" He asked, though he fearfully knew the answer. Yaxley simply turned and walked away. James carefully lifted his daughter's sleeve, and gasped. She had the Dark Mark on her forearm. "Oh fuck." He said, before turning around. Draco saw no reason to hide his own mark, and showed James.

"I want to know everything Malfoy. Now!" He said angrily. "We joined last year. The Death Eaters were looking for young members, and we both believe in the cause. We were given a task to complete by years end. We were supposed to let Death Eaters into Hogwarts. No one other the Dumbledore was supposed to die if the plan went right."

James had just been put in a very bad position. His daughter was a Death Eater. His future son in law was a Death Eater. He couldn't tell Dumbledore, not when it was his own daughter. "I.. I want you to tell Voldemort to meet me. I want it an explanation from his mouth. Surely I am owed that?"

"I'll try for you sir, but I can't predict what he will do to us or you. Me and Lillian have effectively failed our task."
132111121122121111.title: Neville fires and hits Lillian

choices: 'Joining' The Order
Draco gets sent back to Hogwarts alone
Other
1321111211221211111.content:
Walking into his own home felt like a death sentence. His father was on thin ice already after a number of setbacks, and his failure surely meant to death of his family, of Lillian. Instead he found the Dark Lord welcoming him. "You need not worry about my threats Draco. I would not hurt your family so soon after the tragedy with your betrothed. The situation at Hogwarts has grown more dire then I thought, other students will be assigned your task." The Dark Lord said, having read Draco's mind without the boy realizing it.

Draco was keenly aware of it however, after Voldemort revealed information Draco hadn't said. "So, Mr Potter wishes to meet with me? Very well, tell him I accept. It shall be a rather interesting meeting. You may go now Draco, you will receive new orders soon. Be aware that failing this one will result in the aforementioned punishment." Draco hurried out, not wanting to even think about the consequences he could face.

Later that day, the Dark Lord arrived at Potter Manor, knocking on the door. James' face was tight, he knew exactly who was at the door. "Come in, please." He said, guiding the man who should be his mortal enemy inside his own home. "I'd offer you a cup of tea, but I'd rather get right to business." He said, sitting down in his favorite chair. "As to be expected from one of the leading corporate executives in Wizarding Britain. I'm sure we can cut a deal Mr Potter."

"Alright. I have some questions. First, just how involved is my daughter as a Death Eater?" James asked. "She has worked her way through the ranks quickly. Some of the old guard are a bit... jealous of the favor I show the younger generation." Voldemort responded. "Alright, Second, is she in danger if this task isn't completed?" "I do not abide by failure Mr Potter. I would give her many chances for coming from such a high ranking Pureblood family, but I can't have failure rewarded."

"I can give some other reassurances I figure you may have. I am not as much as a zealot in my old age then I was fourteen years years ago. Ever since the attack on the Longbottoms ruined my reputation, I have had time to think, and while I still believe Purebloods are superior, I can accept certain, how to say in a politically correct term, 'First Generation Wizards' into the world, so long as they cut off contact with the muggle world."

James was surprised, and scared at how reasonable the man sounded. "How do I know you aren't leading me along?" James asked. "You don't. But you very well know Dumbledore isn't as reasonable as you think. He already knew of the allegiance of Draco and Lillian, and was in fact letting their plan go ahead. Ask the man yourself, you'll have to poke and prod him though." Voldemort said with a chuckle.

James sighed, and racked his brain for more questions. "No doubt you are wondering Mr Potter, what is there in this to benefit you, in a more financial form. I have money of my own to give you, free of charge. But you will spend it not on yourself, but on other Wizarding families, ones that have lost their money, either years ago, or only just now. Bring them back into prominence, and they will thank you. More then that, you can bring Wizarding Britain into the present. We still live in the 1500's, while it's 1997. Fourteen years in exile, till I revealed myself shortly after the Tri-Wizard Cup. Those fourteen years changed me. We are superior to Muggles, yet we have allowed them to surpass us in every way in terms of technology! Their cities span hundreds of kilometers. Our biggest settlements barely qualify as villages! Follow my instructions Mr Potter, and you will reward yourself, by dragging the Wizarding world into the 20th, and soon 21st century." Voldemort said, a smile on his face.

"One last thing. What is my daughters task?" James asked. Voldemort chuckled at what he knew would be the last one. "I want her to infiltrate the Order of the Phoenix. Severus had been a good spy, but neither Order nor Death Eaters have been sure of his allegiance. I can hide their marks, all they need is you to vouch for them, to get them inside." He said. James sat there, thinking, thinking for who knows how long. "Fine, you have a deal. Now, please leave before I regret this and do something stupid." James said. He had just made a deal with the devil, he supposed he'd better tell Lillian.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ----------------------

Lillian had gotten plenty of rest, and what she really wanted was to get out of bed. But the healers expressly forbid it, and so she either sat there, or laid down, bored out of her mind. At least Draco was there, constantly by her side. "Stop blaming yourself Draco. If not for you Snape might have never known I was there." She said, weakly holding his hand. "Promise me one thing." She said, smiling at Draco. "Anything, anything in the world."

Lillian laughed when Draco said anything, and made her wish be known. "I want Longbottom's head on a silver fucking platter. I'm going to kill the bastard." She said seriously. Now it was Draco's turn to laugh. "It shall me done my Queen." He said mockingly. Her father made sure to let the moment pass before knocking and clearing his throat. "I uhh. I spoke to... him. Your master. I made a deal with the devil. If anyone asks, he is your 'Uncle'." James said, before walking away, while an older man, wearing layers of glamour walked in.

"My Lord. I would kneel, but Longbottom's curse did quite the damage to me." Lillian said, while Draco knelt before him. "Rise Draco. I understand Lillian. Your previous task can no longer be completed by you. Instead, I have something else in mind." He said, pulling up each of their sleeves, seemingly removing the Marks. "I need spies within the Order of the Phoenix, your marks will only get in the way there. When possible, pass along information you have learned to me. Severus is not to be trusted."
1321111211221211111.title: Lillian and Draco are given a new task

choices: Lillain invites Ginny and Hermione on a girls night out
Lillain spends some quality time with Draco
Lillian takes her revenge on Sirius
13211112112212111111.content:
Lillian and Draco were surprised their marks could be removed so easily, they had thought it was a brand for life. The Dark Lord only chuckled at their surprise. "Not all my spies can wear long clothes all the time. Your marks are still there, but hidden. It is a gift, one that can be removed should you disappoint, or disobey." He said, giving an example by having their marks show up again. He vanished them once more, then made his leave. Draco let out a relieved sigh when he left.

By the time Lillian was discharged, it was the Holidays, and so they set their plan into action, with the reluctant help of James Potter. "I would have noticed if my daughter was a Death Eater Albus. You can check their arms, they have no marks, and you know they can't be removed." James said, keeping his mind and thoughts a maze. He knew Albus would try Legilimency, and he was prepared.

Dumbledore was suspicious, but the rest of the Order intervened, allowing them in. 12 Grimauld Place was quite the large house, almost a manor or mansion. Things were weird around here, what with the portrait of Walburga Black, Sirius' mother screaming every obscenity under the sun, at her son, Kreacher, an irritable old house elf with a creepy fascination with pureblood girls, the various one night stands Sirius brought to his home every night, Tonks, Sirius' cousin who could morph her features into anything and anyone, the rat like Peter Pettigrew who no doubt wished he could change his looks, and so much more.

The only consolation was that Ginny was here, and that meant Lillian could work on making friends, and driving a wedge between her and the rest of the Order. "Lillian! It's good to see you! I'm so sorry for what happened, if I'd known what Neville was going to do..." Lillian interrupted Ginny with a hug. "You don't need to apologize, I'm just glad to have another girl my age here."

The two spent some time together, talking, gossiping, and just having fun. "So Gin, any man you're interested in?" Lillian prodded. "Well, before all this I was into Neville, but I don't think I could ever look at him the same way again. I've talked with your friend Blaise quite a bit. He's uhh... unique with his phrasing and mannerisms." Ginny said blushing. Lillian giggled at the sight of Ginny blushing. "We need to get out of this place for a while. Why don't I contact Daphne and the others and organize a girls day in Diagon Alley?" Lillian offered, which Ginny accepted right away. "Merlin yes. This place is fucking creepy, especially Kreacher."

Draco was having no such luck. He was treated with suspicion and disdain, and sometimes outright hostility. Sirius in particular seemed to have it out for him, constantly calling him a Death Eater, and pulling pranks on him. It got bad enough that James had to step in. "Enough Sirius! I'll not have you treat my daughters fiancee like this! Stop acting like a child and grow up for once!" He said, standing in between Draco and Sirius.

"You mean to tell me you're allowing your daughter to marry this Death Eater trash? Have you really sunken so low Prongs?" Sirius asked angrily. "Surely Lillian could do much better then him! Have you no regard for your daughters wishes? What's she think of the whole situation?" James grew angrier with his former best friend. "She made the match, I only gave her my blessing. She's head over heels with the boy. And don't call me Prongs! We aren't kids anymore Sirius. Now go away, and grow up."

The two men walked away leaving Draco alone, but the fight continued later, right when a meeting was about to start. "You need to start some discipline with your child James, a Slytherin, and she's fucking the Malfoy boy. Have you no shame? How far have you fallen? Are you a Death Eater as we-" Sirius was cut off with a right hook from James. "You will NOT speak of my daughter in such a foul manner! Nor will you speak to me like that!"

A crowd gathered around to try and seperate the two. "Just sit down Sirius, please." Peter said, trying to stop his two friends from tearing each other apart. "Just shut your fucking mouth Sirius, for once in your life!" Tonks said, Remus and Professor Snape held James back. "Ignore him Potter, Black is simply trying to bully his way through life as usual." "Calm down James, and Sirius for goodness sake shut up before you make things worse!" Eventually Hagrid intervened, picking up both men with ease. "What's the bloody hell's wrong with you Sirius! Speaking to a lady like that, and accusing James Potter of being a bloody Death Eater!" He said, firmly taking James' side, as did most people.

"Do I not get to speak a word in my own defense? You accuse me of being a Death Eater, yet clearly I have no mark, and neither does Draco. I get attacked, sent to the hospital, and now I am showered with scorn and insults! If you believe I am a Death Eater, then I'll take my leave!" Lillian said, standing up to leave. "Please Ms Potter, sit down. We have no wish of you to leave." Dumbledore said. Severus had told him the girl was a prime target for recruitment, and was likely a Death Eater already. He wished to keep an eye on her for the time being.

"As for Sirius, either calm yourself or lay down. We have a meeting to hold." He said, looking at Sirius with a very serious look. The black haired man grumbled and sat himself down. Everyone introduced themselves, and eventually the topic came to Voldemort's plans. Lillian took deep notice of what they knew, and what they didn't. Her first chance she would report this information back to the Dark Lord.
13211112112212111111.title: 'Joining' The Order

choices: The meeting is crucial
The meeting is boring
Something else
132111121122121111111.content:
The meeting was boring. Very little was discussed, and what she did learn of their plans was of little interest. She needed a way to establish trust, to remove doubt in their mind that she wasn't on their side. Perhaps the Dark Lord had a few expendable Death Eaters she could kill or capture. For now, all she could do was work to integrate herself further with the girls here. Most of the Order were older women, mothers, veterans of the first fight against Voldemort. There were a few close in age to her. Given her aunt's position in the Ministry, Susan Bones had been brought in. Ginny was here with her family, and Luna and Hermione there with Ginny. Last was Nymphadora Tonks, though Merlin forbid you use her first name.

She had the perfect plan to bind them to her, which was helped that she was already friendly with Ginny. And so, she had the four other woman gathered in her room. "I've got to admit, that was boring, and I'm getting pretty sick of dealing with this place. I'm proposing a girls night out in Diagon Alley, or Muggle London. All five of us get dressed up, go out for some drinks, and for the single girls, maybe even try to find a few guys." Lillian said, trying to get them all on board. If she could set up the girls with Slytherin's, she'd be able to keep closer tabs on them and isolate them.

"Fuck it, I'm down. I never pass up a reason to to go drinking, especially girls nights out." Tonks said, pretending to raise a glass. "Well, it would be nice to get out of the house, though Tonks will have to order the drinks for us." Ginny said. Luna, Hermione and Susan were not so big on the idea. "I don't think Aunt Amelia would like me going out drinking." Amelia said. "Alcohol consumption makes you a prime target for Wrackspurts" Luna said. Last of all was Hermione. "Me? Going out? But why me? I'm not pretty, I don't drink, I'd rather just read a book."

Lillian sighed, and came up with an idea. "Alright, I'll cut a deal with you Hermione. Let me give you a makeover, then if I can make you beautiful you're coming on the night out." She said. "If I lose, take me to the bookstore and I'll buy you the biggest, densest tome you can find." Luckily that got Hermione to agree, and got the other two intrigued about what she could do with Hermione. She always kept Sleakeazy hair products around in case of a hair emergency, and so she got right to work, working on Hermione's hair, just like it had been at the Yule Ball two years ago.

It was hard work, and it took quite a few spells to tame Granger's curls, but finally step one was done. She did Hermione's makeup, a light glossy pink lipstick, a light black eyeshadow, and a little bit of mascara. When she was done, Hermione looked stunning, and she intended to prove it. If she could give the girl some confidence, she might pry her away from the Weasley's. She was to useful to give up on, and while the girl would never become a Death Eater, she didn't have to ever know Lillian was one.

Hermione stared at herself in the mirror. The last time she had looked like this was at the Yule Ball, one of the few times anyone had paid attention to her. She had reveled in it for some time, being the partner of Victor Krum, but the scorn she had gotten from Ron, and from so many female fans and supposed friends caused her to revert back into the bookworm. Maybe she shouldn't be so hasty to decline Lillian's offer. "Alright, you win, I'll go with you Lillian." She said with a sigh, worried that people would treat her harshly.

With Hermione on board, she managed to get Susan and Luna convinced. Susan, by promising that anything with her aunt would be smoothed over by the Potter's and Malfoy's, Luna by proxy of Hermione and Ginny going. When they were all ready, they set out, trusting Tonks to lead them to wherever the best place was. Luckily they avoided any awkward situations leaving Grimmauld Place, and soon enough Tonks apparated them near the Leaky Cauldron. There they entered Diagon Alley, as Tonks led them to a bar she swore was one of the best.

What Lillian left out was simple, she had arranged to meet up with some other Slytherin girls, and even a few of the boys, and after some time in the bar, they just so happened to run into each other. "Daphne, Tracy, Astoria, it's great to see you! Hello to the boys as well." She said. Only Blaise and Theo had been invited. She wondered if she could hook up Theo with someone, though she doubted it. He was too broody and silent for most girls. However his betrothal with Tracy hadn't worked out. Rumors was that they were to closely related. Blaise she knew had been meeting up with Ginny on the side.

"Weasley, looking as sexy as ever." Blaise said, quickly pairing off with the redhead in a corner somewhere. One down, three to go. Luna and Theo were quickly ruled out, and she doubted she could get Hermione with the man, so that left Susan. "Why don't you talk to Theo, Susan. Sure, he's silent a lot, but he's a big thinker, you know, the philosopher type." She said, trying to hype him up.

"You don't need to be my wing woman Lillian. Would you care to talk Miss Bones?" Theo said, quick and short to the point. Oh well, as long as they hooked up. She talked to Luna about setting her up with someone, but it was revealed, through intense blushing, Luna only had eyes for one man, a Hufflepuff, Rolf Scamander. Well, that was part of her plan shot down, but she would recoup, and reconsider another day. "He's the only one who's ever taken me seriously." Luna admitted.

The most surprising revelation was that Granger wasn't into men, and neither was Daphne. Lillian was sure she was dating one of the Weasley's, or at least a Gryffindor man, but no, Granger was single, blushing and stammering when Daphne started to flirt with her. "You're looking gorgeous Hermione." Daphne said with a wink. "Oh! I... thank you Daphne." Hermione said, burying her face in her hands. Daphne only giggled, flirting further. "You're so cute when you're embarrassed, why don't we talk in private, hmm?" Daphne took Hermione's hand, leading her to a secluded corner.

For the most part it worked out in the end. Tonks teased her somewhat about it. "Playing matchmaker are we? Ah don't worry about it. I did the same when I was your age, trying to set all my friends up with other friends. You've had more success then I ever had with romance." She admitted. "And here I thought being able to change your looks would be a bonus to men." Lillian said, to which Tonks shook her head. "Once they see the real me, they seem to think I'm plain average."

Tonks before had a bubblegum pink Mohawk, and a tanned appearance. Now however, she was a bit shorter, with long mousy brown hair, pale skin, and a heart shaped face. She reminded Lillian of someone, but she couldn't place it. She didn't pry though, Tonks wasn't useful to her plans. After some time, they headed back for Grimmauld Place, all with a bit of alcohol in them, and promises to see each other at Hogwarts when the Holidays ended.

The next day another meeting was scheduled. Lillian attended again, hoping to find something useful. "Where were you last night?" Draco asked with a smile, already knowing the answer. "Sorry babe, girls night, you know how it is. Learnt some real interesting things though. Ginny's into your mate Blaise, and Granger's into Daphne, and Daphne's all to happy to return the feelings" Draco nodded, leaving that stuff to her. "Found out some good info. Apparently Black is nearly broke. He got drunk last night and started rambling. He's surviving solely on loans from Dumbledore."

The two were interrupted by the more people coming into the room. Hopefully this meeting would actually give them something substantial to report back.
132111121122121111111.title: Girls Night Out

choices: Ron bursts in and sweeps Harriet into an embrace.
Harriet discovers she is not the celebrity that he used to be.
They head off to the infirmary to deal with the cuts and scratches.
13213.content:
There's a heavy low creak from behind you, "Harry! You in here?", it's Hermione.

"Yes... Over here."

Unfortunately, Harry's feet had become entangled in the cloth. He lost his balance and crashed towards the mirror. He yelped as he felt his ass hit the mirror. Everything tilted wildly as the wooden frame creaked. With a wooden -crack- the hinge broke, and Harry and the mirror crashed ass backwards onto the floor.

For Harry it happened in slow motion. Like a dizzying out of body experience. As he fell, the world stopped moving, and time reversed. Before his eyes, he saw himself walking backwards out of the room. His sense of balance told him he was still falling. But his view unwound further into the past, to the first meal in the Grand Hall. That flickered away in an instant, and Harry saw himself at the Train station trying desperately to figure out how to get to Platform 9 3/4.

The scene flickered again, and he was in a dark, dank room. Spotting a friendly face, he wanted to weep for joy. But no words came out his lips. He was simply a mute passenger on his journey into the past. Unable to express himself, as Hagrid said, "You're a Wizard, Harry."

A faded outline of the real world intruded into Harry vision, as the scene shifted again. This time, to one of Dudley's birthdays, there were five candles on the cake, and as Harry caught his reflection, the misery was already as clear on his face, as his youth.

There was a flurry of owls, as the scene shifted. Harry was wrapped up, warm and cosy. He looked up into the massive face of Albus Dumbledore, and Professor McGonagall. Behind them, towering over them, Hagrid his face wet with tears...

"Dead...", he sobbed, blowing his nose loudly on his handkerchief. It looked big enough to be a towel, or even a blanket at his own diminutive size.

He held out one tiny hand, to try to hold onto the headmaster, but he just scraped his pudgy digits over Dumbledore's nose.

As the scene shifted again, Harry knew there wasn't long to go. Either he'd simply vanish, or be forced to face his own nightmare memories. Even as the realisation struck, he found himself sitting in a crib.

"Take them, Lily... Take them and run..." he felt his throat tighten at the sound of his father's voice.

Outside the door, there was sickening green flash, and a scream. A death scream. Harry knew his father was dead.

Lily entered, her hair was wild, and her eyes glittering green and fired with passion.

But something was off, something was different. His crib was in the wrong place, when he looked over to where it was meant to be, he saw it. There was another crib there. An identical infant sat in his pale blue romper suit, he was crying at the noise. He was still struggling to work out what was going on.

She bent into the crib, and picking him up she hugged him to her chest. "He'll never harm you.... he'll never harm either of you." she swore with passion.

But Voldemort was already behind her, his wand pointing steadily into her back.

"Avada Kadavra."

Sickly green lightning shot forth, and rippled and roared around their hugging bodies. With a shriek, Lily and Harry Potter died in each other's embrace.

As Voldemort tried to pull his wand back, it resisted. The vile green lightning leapt from their bodies, and wrapped itself around the Dark Lord. It was his turn to shriek in pain and denial, as the magic backfired and killed him utterly.

From behind the crib bars, Harry watched still unsure what was happening, still not sure if this was a dream, a nightmare or the actual events... and why they were now different.

He was still in shock, as Hagrid crashed into the room. Literally shattering the frame, as he entered the room. His face fell, as he saw the bodies on the floor. His foot crashed down into the corpse of Voldemort, turning his broken spine to dust. Hagrid's anger fled, and his tenderness returned, as he examined Lily and Harry. But it was already too late. Their lives were extinguished.

A wail ripped itself from Harry's lung, he felt his powerlessness vanish, and a cry rip from his soul.

Hagrid heard it, and a smile split his face.

"I'll keep you safe.... At least, I can keep you safe, little Harriet."

Harry felt himself being lifted in powerful, but tender hands, safe and secure. He was carried to Hagrid's bike. With a kick, the engine started, and they were airborne.

Time flickered, and the scene shifted. He was back on the Dursley's doorstep, still wrapped in the blanket, and his romper suit. Hagrid was sobbing, McGonagall's eyes were wet with tears, and the Headmaster was struggling.

"He was already dead." his voice was distant and cold, "Voldemort was already dead."

"I ... I couldn't save them... I tried..." Hagrid's voice quavered and broke.

He took time to gather his thoughts. His mouth opened and closed several times, but no words came out.

Professor Dumbledore put a steadying hand on his shoulder.

"You saved Harriet... You saved her..."

"Her?" Harry thought. He wasn't able to check. Time flickered, and the scene shifted.

It was Dudley's ninth birthday, and Harriet was passing him a simple present in a white box with a pink ribbon. "Oh Shit." was the thought racing through her head, as Dudley snatched it from her open hands.

Time flickered and flashed, Harry barely noticed.

Until with a crash, Harry returned to the here and now. Sitting up, she saw Hermione leaning in to offer her hand.

"Come on Harriet, just make sure you don't cut yourself."

Extending his hand, Harry saw that once again Hermione was right. His hand was her hand. Slender and feminine, it disappeared into the frilly cuffs of her blouse.

Getting to her feet, she gave a shimmy to shake loose the shards of mirror she felt sticking to the back of her uniform.
13213.title: "Harry! You in here?" a familiar voice ...

choices: Ron crashes into the room, and plants a passionate kiss on Harriet.
She gave Hermione's hand a reassuring squeeze, and they left the room.
Harry confides the truth to Hermione, to judge her reaction...
132133.content:
Hermione's face was compassionate, and caring, as she rubbed Harriet's arm.

"I know today is hard for you. The day you ... the day ... you lost your brother."

"But the Dark Lord was destroyed." Harriet answered somewhat sullenly. "A celebration..."

Outside the window, the flash of fireworks lit the room.

But the memories... the emotions were still raw for her. But her fragmented memories were coming back together. She still remembered.... everything before crashing into the mirror, and she knew that that was real. That this wasn't ....

But it still felt totally real to her. She recognised the sincerity of Hermione's interest and humanity. There was a growing memory of them sharing notes in class... of -she blushed- her noticing Ron Weasley... as a boy.

"We should visit Madam Pomphrey." Hermione suggested, she took Harriet's arm, intending it to be more than a suggestion.

Harriet planted her feet, resisting.

"We don't need her... You don't need her."

She offered her arm up. Hermione's lips barely moved as she muttered the healing spell under her breath. Her fingers took on a pale glow, and she passed her hand up and down Harriet's, watching as the scrapes and scratches faded to nothing. She'd been working on the spell too, enhancing it. As Harriet watched, the small tears and rents in the fabric pulled together and repaired themselves at the same time.

Harriet took a quick mental observation of herself.

The corner of her lips quirked up, 'herself', that felt odd... no, not odd, just different. She felt comfortable in her clothes. She felt the weight on her shoulders as the bra pulled there. She felt the hem of her skirt brushing over her legs, and the myriad subtly different things that told her she was completely feminine. She knew she should feel more ... well ... wrong ... But she didn't.

She was damned if she was going to feel wrong. With gritty self-determination, she gave a curt nod to herself. She caught the resolution showing on her own face. A face that was familiar, but different. She restrained a gasp, there was no scar on her forehead.

It felt incredibly liberating. Freed from a burden, she'd never realised she'd been carrying.

Harriet determined that she'd at least enjoy today, even if she did decide to return to her real world. She could spend some time here as a normal witch. Not 'the Chosen One'.
132133.title: They head off to the infirmary to deal with the...

choices: They finally arrive at the infirmary.
Harry confides in Hermione about his -real- past.
Harry wants to explore this Voldemort free world for a while at least.
1321331.content:
Ron crashed into the room. The door bouncing back from the frame, and swept Harriet into a crushing embrace.

Harriet was still in shock, as Ron planted a kiss firmly on her. It just felt weird and wrong, her own body betraying her. She felt Ron's flat chest crushing her breasts as she was held powerless in his arms. She tried to wriggle free, but that was fruitless, and just made her nipples graze against the firmness of Ron's chest.

Surrendering to the inevitable, she felt a growing sense of rightness, even more so when Hermione joined in wrapping her arms around both Ron and Harriet.
