They held the embrace until, Hermione gave them each a friendly pat on the back. Ron broke away with his cheeks flushing crimson. He hadn't intended to show his needs... his emotions so blatantly. Harriet was blushing too, her gaze firmly on the floor, as she tried to sort her jangling emotions.

It was just the right interruption, as Hermione had now lost her bossiness momentum.

"I'm fine." Harriet assured her.

"I'm sorry we argued..." Hermione said out of the blue.

The confusion must have been clear on Harriet's face.

"Either of us will be an excellent Head Girl."

As Harriet heard those words, it felt like a flood-gate was opening, as she felt waves of new memories filling her mind. She recalled, her arrival at Hogwarts, and the Sorting Hat putting her in Gryffindor.... her sly happy smile when Ron ended up there too. She remembered her first time standing up to Hermione's bullies. The girls who were intimidated by her intelligence... and muggle origins. Harriet felt a bond with her, despite her wizarding parents, she had been raised as a muggle, and she likes the feeling as Pansy's nose had squished up on contact with her tightly balled fist. She remembered her first stuttering conversation with Ron.... the way he stared at her when she spoke... the way that made her feel.

She had to stop and lean against the wall, her breath heaving from her lungs. She still recalled her transformation... transfiguration... transportation... She wasn't sure what the term was... but it was still there... and she knew it .... knew it to be true. However, it lacked the emotional weight of her new memories.

"No arguments." Hermione snapped. She put one arm around Harriet, from the left, as Ron supported her from the other side. Together they carried her to the infirmary and Madame Pomfrey.

As the waves of new memories slowed, Harriet recalled that she was still the youngest seeker for Gryffindor, and taking after her father made her feel warm inside. She hoped she grew to be as compassionate and kind as her mother.

The three caused a commotion as they carried Harriet through the school. There weren't many students in the halls, the sound of parties breaking out from various closed door, as the Wizarding world celebrated the utter defeat of the Dark Lord. But whenever they did pass someone, they left a trail of goggling faces in their wake.

Even in her less than coherent state, Harriet still noticed that the boys reacted rather differently than the girls. She -damn it- He was used to furtive glances, and whispered comments everywhere he went. But this... this was different. She felt their eyes undressing her, judging her dimensions, comparing her to other girls.

The other girls looks just as intently, although their faces held different judgements.

She wasn't sure... it was all so strange... But she thought she liked it.
1321331.title: Ron crashes into the room, and plants a passion...

choices: The three friends check out their reflections.
The mirror only shows their normal reflections.
Hermione runs her fingers around the ornately engraved frame.
1322.content:
For a change it was Harry who raced through his dinner in the Great Hall. Although Ron wasn't much slower. Hermione however, ate at her usual pace, with a look of vague disappointment at the boys.

She was just as eager, with Harry's story of the mirror. Ron didn't seem especially curious, after all he'd seen the last magic mirror that the boys had discovered. She also wondered if these things were just less interesting to those born into magical families.

As she put down her glass, washing away the last of her dessert. The boys were already half out of their seats.

But soon they were following Harry as he tried to find that room, and that mirror.

She had to give a stern cough, to stop them racing ahead, their enthusiasm exceeding their manners.

However, when she saw the excitement on Harry's face, she knew that he was sure he'd found the room.

Like an entertainer, he bowed with a flourish, and opened the door, with a sweeping wave, he gestured them into the room.

The footprints in the dust, and the uncovered mirror identified it to Ron and Hermione.

With a furtive glance, Harry checked nobody was watching, and closed the door behind himself, joining his friends at the mirror.
1322.title: He decides it is best to get his friends as wit...

choices: A trilling giggle echoed through the room... "That tickled..."
As the ripples faded, the remaining image was different now.
With a gasp, Harry realised he was wearing a bra, and it fit!?!
Something else...
1323.content:
Harry felt the blood pounding in his ears. The room was eerily silent, as he strained a sibilant voice came from the glass. Or at least sounded like it did. The floorboard creaked as he stepped up to the mirror. Holding the frame, he peered behind, double-checking for any pranking Weasley brothers.

Feeling the ornate carving, he eased himself back to face the mirror. Hunting for some trace of its' purpose. Was it a lure, like the mirror of Erised, that would entice with empty futures, or tease with false pasts.

His fingers traced letters hidden amidst the decorative ivy carved around the frame. The leonine Harry appeared in the mirror, when he peered more closely at it.

He recoiled, as he heard the whisper once more. This time, it was distinct enough to make out.

"Come... Come explore..." The invitation was a siren call, insidiously tempting. When he was caught by the reflection, this time his face inches from the surface. He could make out more!

The shape of his face was different in the mirror. A hand reaching up to caress the different chins, only cemented the impression. Harry's hand in the glass was slender and feminine. He recoiled, as it hit him, that was a female version of himself! The eyes more expressive, and the hint of a bust making the mirror-jumper swell.

"Come... Explore... Experiment..." The words were seductive, enticing. Harry frowned wondering if they were parsel tongue. He'd never noticed when he spoke that rather than human speech. It was all subconscious.

Twisting his wrist this way and that, he was more fascinated with the mirror hand. It seemed to have a glossy sheen. Not coloured, not painted, but protected.

As his hand drifted, the mirror reflection making his gestures awkward and counter intuitive. It drifted closer to the surface. The chill touch startled Harry, and he snatched his hand back.

It was then the ripples spread out from the point of contact, spreading in circles out to the frame before vanishing.
1323.title: A spectral whisper emanates from its' surface.

It took a moment for the ripples to pass. The view looked slightly different, though Harry struggled to pin down the differences. Tilting his head this way, and that, he watched the mirror girl copy his gestures.

Then it hit him, the mirror image was still the same. As he turned his hand around, she copied his moves exactly. Then he snatched his hand up to examine it directly. It was the same as the mirror image. He now had that slender feminine arm, and those delicate fingers, the sheen of clear nail varnish.

The next thought tumbled over the last, as Harry's hands smashed against his chest. Discovering, and mashing a bust that was entirely alien, and every inch a part of his body. Uncomfortable at the sensations, he'd discovered, his hands dropped lower. They found the pleats of the skirt the mirror revealed. And beneath... Harry knew he was a she before her questing fingers confirmed it.

She could feel the leonine mane of hair, shake back and forth over her shoulders, as she shook her head in unsteady, disbelieving denial.

Stumbling backwards from the mirror, she bumped into a pillar. Turning around, she held onto hope for the briefest of moments, that she'd been hypnotised, under some mirror derived delusion. But, she felt everything...

Pain speared through Harry's head, and he raised a hand to his temple. This felt different, it wasn't the presence of Voldemort, or his dark thoughts that were intruding.

Her eyes screwed shut against the pain. Wobbling uncertainly, with her eyes closed, she struggled to stand upright.

Opening her eyes once again, she took in her reflection. Tilting her head, to the side. There was something off about her reflection. It just made the back of her mind itch.

But, then she caught it. She'd changed the colour of her nails, experimenting with a different shade. Pouting, she blew a kiss to her reflection. Shooting the glass a coquettish look, she knew she could reel in any boy with that smile.

Well, once she practised it a few more times, it all just came naturally. The faux-innocence, the accidental shift in her posture to emphasise her bust. The defiant shoulders back, chin high, look that mixed confidence with innocence. An sure-fire invitation for anyone who wanted to correct her.

Mussing her hair up, she refreshed her 'Just Fucked' look of casual naiveté. One that screamed out loud how aptly that style was named. She could fake the blush, if anyone wanted to name the style to her face.

It wouldn't work on the other witches, but she could virtually guarantee that her girly-girl look would have any young wizard wrapped around her finger.

Harriet had to dial back her sulty strut, as she left. If she were faking innocence the cock-hardening, eye-drawing tease of the sway of her arse had to be toned down.

133.content:
A large wicker hamper catches Harry's eye, the edge of a cloak hangs over the edge untidily.

Harry looks around, embarrassed by the thought entering his mind. He rummages through until he finds what he was looking for. He finds a cloak, and slides it into place. Stealing another glance, he's still alone, he spots a mirror and goes over to check out his reflection. The cloak is a little tight on his shoulders, but it's a base impulse.

He runs his fingers over the silver and green serpent embossed on the edge of cloak.

The questions he'd asked himself as he thought about the Sorting Hat. He knew he was in the right house, but that didn't satisfy him entirely. Perhaps as a Slytherin, he'd be more able to vanquish Voldemort.... or as a Ravenclaw, he'd have found a wiser solution to the return of the Dark Lord.

Startled at his absent minded gestures, Harry snatched his fingers away from the serpent. It's quite luxurious too, with a satin lining in forest green. Pulling it closed he found a name tag stitched inside.

'Hestia Carrow'

The name rang a bell. Oh, Yes. From the Slug Club. There was a Slytherin girl there with that name. This was probably her mother's cloak. She looked to be a bit of cold fish. But he'd thought the same of Hermione at first. Besides she was well spoken and held her own in any discussions at the club.

More came back to Harry as he thought about the club and Hestia. She'd had a twin sister... F..-something... Flora. That was it Flora. Wouldn't he still be himself, if he were in Slytherin. If he were friends with the Carrow's rather than the Weasley. Surely a house couldn't change a person. Harry didn't want to dismiss all Slytherin, just because Draco was a .... Draco was Draco. A pipsqueak upstart with delusions.

Realising he'd almost walked into the mirror. He drew himself up to his full height and struck a Slytherin-y pose. Putting a sneer on his face, and an arrogance in his stance.

1332.content:
The reflection isn't what Harry expected at all. There were hints of the Carrow sisters in the image, but it didn't look like them. He was entranced by the image, perhaps it was their mother when she was a student. But she had piercing green eyes, and the sisters were blue eyed. He recalled Hermione nudging him, telling him not to stare.

She was a beautiful girl, and as Harry absently brushes his hair aside, he spots his reflection has the same scar. The pensive look is familiar too. He wasn't sure what to think when he thought again about finding herself beautiful. But the reflection blushed, so Harry imagined he was too.

She mimicked his moves, like a true reflection. He ran his hands down his side and she did too. With a wicked thought, Harry cupped his imaginary bosom. Lifting and squeezing the girls. He felt nothing in his hands, although his reflection was again blushing. It was an odd pose for this, but he thought that the girl was rather well endowed.

He shook his head, and allowed his hands to fall limply away. Again the mirror mirrored.

Harry scanned the frame for writing. He remembered there was a clue carved in the frame of the mirror or Erised.

The palm of his hand brushed against the surface, Harry recoiled. It wasn't chilly like glass or metal. It was warm, the warmth of human flesh.

He was drawn to touch the glass again. Reaching out his hand, he stopped half an inch from it. Then his nerves on edge, he gently tapped on the glass, and snatched his hand back. It rippled like a pond. The ripples vanished at the frame. This time, it had had a texture, it wasn't just warm, it had felt coarse... like skin.

13321.content:
Harry took a deep breath and calmed himself. It was a magic mirror, he wasn't sure what it was showing him. Perhaps a world where he'd been born a girl, a world where he'd been slow to object when the Sorting Hat had suggested Slytherin as his... -er- her house.

Harry scowled at his reflection, maybe he was blaming the wrong thing. It might be the cloak that was enchanted, he might truly be female, and it's only his memories that tell him what he expects.

In any case, if it were dangerous then Dumbledore would have kept it's location more secure or destroyed the item.

He decided the quickest way to settle his nerves was like ripping off a plaster, or facing cold water. Slapping his hand flat onto the glass, the frame rocked with the force of the blow, giving him a glimpse of the ceiling as it almost fell over backwards. But it's weight pulled it back forwards, and gave his hand a slap.

He high-fived his reflection.

That was when things became weird. Harry felt his fingers become wrapped in those of his reflection. Instantly his gaze shot to his hand, semi-transparent fingers were woven around his own. They were more slender than his, and the nails were glazed in glossy crimson.

He tried to break free, twist his hand loose. There was a wrenching, and Harry was pulled forward. His hand vanished beneath the surface of the glass. His reflections face was twisted in victory. Her face a grinning mask. Her hand vanishing at the same point. But you still felt her fingers woven together with yours. A second chilly hand gripped your forearm, and Harry was wrenched forward again. This time up to his shoulder. He scrabbled to break free, flailing wildly afraid to grab the frame with his free hand.

Without a firm grip, Harry was powerless to resist, with a final pull. Harry stumbled across the threshold of the mirror.

A chill raced across his skin as he passed. But that was nothing compared to his other shock.

Looking back out the mirror, Harry saw his body blowing him a kiss before turning and walking away. Harry tried to follow, but the glass was simply a wall to him. Beating his fist on the glass had no effect. Harry watched as he spoke some words, at least his lips moved, but he heard nothing emerge.

Harry felt his chest shift as he battered again and again against the barrier. Looking down, Harry spotted that now he was female. Not overly endowed like the witches in Ron's Magazine.

Although Harry did fantasise about some of them in 'the Golden Snatch'.

Reaching slimmer hands downwards, Harry confirmed that she was fully female. She recoiled when she did confirm it. Her hands flying away like they'd received an electric shock.

She turned around to examine her mirror universe.

13332.content:
The cascade of blonde hair, rolling over her shoulders unsettled Harry more than he liked to admit. Just as the cold flinty hardness of seeing Hestia Carrow look back out of the mirror felt wrong.

His mirror image copied his gestures, moves and mannerisms perfectly, as he once more tried to somehow trick the mirror. Flipping his hand up to his shoulder, the girl in the glass tosses her hair back off her shoulder and out of the way. But Harry's hand met no obstruction, felt no tickling caress of long hair.

Glancing away from the mirror, he double-checked his own body. It was still the same, the same Gryffindor jumper beneath the Ravenclaw cloak. the same boys jumper.

Cautiously he took a step closer to the mirror. His eyes searching for something to explain the mirror's magic. Just to look more closely, to check for an engraving around the frame.

Dizzyingly, the surface of the mirror rippled, slowly like a deep lake. As the mirror resettled, Harry stood still eager to find any changes. However, it looked like the same girl looking back out. She looked to be in her third year... or thereabouts. Rather a pretty girl...

Taking another step forward, he was almost at the mirror. Again it rippled, and again Harry stopped, to see what changed. A gorgeous fifth? year girl was reflected back. Her face, refined, her lips full, her nose petite. Turning sideways, Harry admired the curves... Their size, nice without being too obvious... yet still firm and full.

Unbidden, the comparison sprang to mind. She was more beautiful than Cho Chang, her face, her body...

Shuffling sideways, the last half-step until he was almost pressing against the mirror. It rippled once more, the girl had to be in her final year. An elegance in how she wore the uniform, a confidence in her pose. She was as tall as Harry, her long hair now long straight and smooth. The little girl vanished, only the woman remained. Harry smiled in pleasure, she truly was a beauty. Naturally enough, she smiled back, those full perfect lips spreading to reveal perfect white teeth. Her high cheek bones shown to their best by simple act of smiling.

The voice was heard just a moment too late, as his fingers came into contact with the cold glass of the mirror. The chill replaced a moment later by the heat of the slim mirror fingers.

The mirror woman copying his tilting head, the curious look on his face. The surface rippled, and Harry felt the fingers entwine in his own. In seconds the grip tightened and heaved him through the glass. His cry cut off as he plunged forward. A harry-shaped outline rushing to the frame of the mirror. Tripping over the lower edge of the mirror, Harry face-planted. His view obscured by his mop of unruly hair.... Hair that felt slightly too long, too straight. He dismissed that as his imagination.

Still stunned and confused, he looked around for the mystery woman. But the room was empty, planting his hands on the wooden floor he levered himself more upright. Barely rising a few inches, before an awkwardness became apparent. His chest felt heavier, looking down, he spotted that he was now she. Abundantly so, the chest he'd admired mere instants ago, now felt too big, too heavy. Snapping his gaze from the canyon of cleavage, he spotted that the arms supporting him, were now girly themselves. His hands slim, the fingers slender, the nails coated with a clear gloss.

Easing himself to his knees, Harry confirms the last detail, head to toe, and everywhere inbetween Harry Potter... Harriet Potter is now the 'Girl Who Lived'. She hugged the cloak about her, a sense of vulnerabilty that he'd never thought about was now more pressing to her.

134.content:
Drawn to a plain box, Harry opened it to see a piece of ornate jewellery. With a large blue stone embedded in a web of silver. Initially he thought it was a necklace, big enough to draw admiring eyes, to a suitably displayed cleavage.

It was only when he spotted the Eagle's head atop the gem, that he then recognised the web, as a pair of wings, and knew it was a crown. As he stroked the soft fabric of the box, he saw it lacked the chain of a necklace, too.

Pain flared, from his scar, as his fingers brushed over the engraved writing of the crown... tiara?

Snatching his hand back, he suppressed the idea of slapping it to the floor. Memories of Katie Bell sprang back to mind, and he instead slapped the wooden box closed.

His fingers itched and tingled, as he wandered on, deeper into the room. Until he found a thickly padded wing-back chair. It reminded him of the ones in the common room, though a little the worse for wear. Settling down, Harry was glad for a little privacy, peace and quiet. Free from the different demands placed on him.

Pulling a book, from his back, he gave a small snort, as 'Advanced Potion Making' came out. The tattered cover looking as fragile as ever.

https://68.media.tumblr.com/8ea6c9a93423...

Carefully folding, the cover down he traced his finger over the inscription. The faint indentation of quill scratches, a reminder of the mundane nature of the book. However, powerful and knowledgeable the previous owner.

https://68.media.tumblr.com/ad204a560765...

He flicked ahead to the next section that Professor Slughorn had told them they'd be studying. He didn't want to lose his reputation in Potions, or the chance to impress him, any leverage that could help him retrieve that memory needed to be exploited. He wasn't sure he convinced himself, but it did no harm either. He knew the book wasn't imbued with any menace itself. A test he hated to admit he was reluctant to perform.

But, both Hermione and Ginny, were finally convinced. The pages crackled dryly as he turned them. His attention caught, by an arcane swirl on the previous page. He recognised it as a wand gesture, and beneath it. Pectore cresce, was the incantation, and beneath it, for enemies (witches).

He murmured to himself, once more Hermione's suggestion, that somehow, the Prince was female. Was this a spell, only a witch could cast, or was the target only to be a witch. Wandless his finger traced the path through the air, the spell unspoken as his lips moved silently. Perhaps, it was a weaker spell, like Sectumsempra, but somehow gentler.

Distracted, Harry glanced at his wrist. The old analogue watch, a replacement to his digital one. A cheap Casio, a present from the Dursleys one Christmas. The best present he'd ever received, until he saw it had come free with some breakfast cereal. Alas, digital watches and magic didn't mix, and he'd bought something more reliable from Diagon Alley. It's face aged yellow, and with two other smaller dials at 3 o'clock and 9 o'clock, with the phases of the moon, the other being a tiny orrery, with the position of the planets.

It was almost time for his next class, easing himself up from the chair with a groan and a sigh, Harry headed off.

135.content:
Harry woke... or thought he had... looking around he only saw a forest. It was dark all around.

Lumos.

The light cast a glow around the tip of his wand. The effect was the opposite of what he wanted to achieve. It made the shadows deeper, the darkness blacker.

"It's only a dream.... It's only a dream...." he kept muttering under his breath. His recollection was fuzzy, but the last thing he could think of was being in the castle. Whatever he was thinking.... wherever his mind was, physically he was in the castle. Harry tried to picture himself slumped on the floor... tried to imagine himself getting to his feet... tried to imagine waking up.

A noise startled him, and he whirled around. The shadows danced and flickered.

"Harrrry ... Pottter..." the voice hissed. It sent a shiver down his spine. Voldemort.

"Show yourself... show yourself you coward." he snarled, spat into the engulfing darkness.

His defiance fell into the abyss, and was swallowed.

"Harrry...." the voice was at his ear, just over his shoulder.

He whirled around, almost loosing his footing in the undergrowth.

There was nobody there.

"Harrrry...." he felt the warmth of his breath tickling his ear.

He turned more slowly this time, but again Voldemort wasn't there.

"You're alone, Harry.... All alone..."

Harry's lip curled up in contempt.

"I'm not alone." the voice continued with amusement.

Something made him look upwards... It was a chilling sight, there were dementors circling above... He felt his soul grow colder, grow harder. He felt the fear of losing his Godfather afresh... he remembered losing Sirius in the ministry. Despair grew, hopelessness threatened to overpower him.

The light flickered at the tip of his wand as his grip loosened.

"Surrender... Just surrender... and let the pain end... let it all end."

The words, the sentiment were still from the Dark Lord, but Harry felt more swayed by them.

He thought of Ron and Hermione... Perhaps without him they wouldn't be targeted. He felt hope swell, as he realised that even if they weren't beside him physically, his friends hadn't abandoned him.

Expecto Patronum!

There was a flurry of white sparks, but it faded into the dark. Harry refused to surrender.

EXPECTO PATRONUM!

This time, the light shot from the tip, and the stag formed proudly it pranced around Harry. His very presence illuminating the enclosed glade more than his previous spell.

He chanced another glance upwards, the dementors were still circling, but not descending.

There was a serpentine laughter, and Harry felt his head being wrenched. A shriek was ripped from his throat. Ahead of him, a cloaked figure was fighting his patronus. There was a flash of pale skin. The hands gripped the antlers, and Harry felt the pain rip across his temple. Not merely focused on his scar.

In a shower of sparks, the antlers detached. His knees gave out, and he fell to the mossy ground.

"Oh, dear... oh deer-y deer-y me."

The forest faded, and the voice faded, as Harry felt the cold smooth stone of the castle floor reassert itself.

14.content:
He'd just left Hermione with a new pile of books in the library, and made his excuses and gone. He wasn't sure that Hermione had even noticed. Harry enjoyed just wandering the castle. In his first year, he'd found the Mirror of Erised, and that had made him always want to find more of the castles secrets.

A place as old as this, with it's history, and it's predecessors made him take a deep breath as if he could inhale their memory their deeds. He wanted to find what had made his father an animagus. Professor McGonagall always insisted that it was a rare skill, but the fact that James, Sirius and even Peter Pettigrew... were all animagi. Well, it just seemed as if there was more at play.

He tried to quiz Hagrid for clues, without giving away too much himself. He'd never come out and asked directly, although asking the Head of house didn't seem especially suspicious. So he liked to probe Minerva as much as he could. As the only animagus in the school (or at least the only registered one), he tried to be as flattering as possible.

He liked to suggest that Dumbledore was an animagus, and ask what his form was, what his patronus was...

He was lost in thought as he walked. It wasn't a corridor that was immediately familiar, but sometimes that was the way of it. Rounding a corner, he spotted Ron standing outside a door. There was something very odd, furtive about his behaviour, that stopped him from calling out to his friend.

He slipped back around the corner, and pulled out his cloak. It slid into place, and with a shimmer he was invisible. Harry was free to observe Ron's odd manner.

He was a look out... Glancing worriedly up and down at the least noise, even an unexpected breeze was enough to make Ron start to sweat and look unsettled.

A number of unpleasant ideas surface, a Slytherin curse or some kind of controlling geas. But as George and Fred burst out of the door, the reason became clearer.

There was a prank afoot.

George and Fred high fived each other, and smiled.

"They'll never discover it... until it's too late."

Ron's eyes darted between his brothers, clearly he wasn't in on the plan, as he listened intently for snippets of information, clues as to the prank.

"The Slytherin's will have a fun practice." George said before giving a ludicrously camp wink to his brother.

choices: Draco accuses Pansy of causing this.

141.content:
As Harry padded down the corridor his senses were peaked. There was a muted boom, followed by a hiss and a scattering of shouts. He looked around anxiously, his invisibility cloak would be no protection if a horde of students came barrelling down the passageway. There was a huff of escaping gas, and Harry spotted a flare of emerald smoke jetting out from behind a portrait.

It was a rather flustered looking old man. He was posed in a chair, but he kept trying to look behind his own frame. Harry backed against the opposite wall. The frame of the picture juddered, more gas burst out, the ornate frame making it dance in billowing patterns.

The cries became louder... closer.

It fell into place. Harry recognised the picture.... recognised the location. This was an entrance to the Slytherin common room. With a crash the frame gave a crack, and the picture swung aside. Out from the green smoke strode the students. Some covering their mouths, others just coughing, promising vengeance between rasping breaths.

Harry wanted to help, this seemed a cruel prank. But he stopped, revealing himself like this would make him look like the prankster. He knew there wasn't much love for him in that particular house. Harry covered his mouth, as more students came out. They milled around, and Harry was careful to avoid getting too close, to risk bumping into anyone.

There was something else that was a little odd about it. All of the Slytherin students were female. He wondered if there was a meeting for the Slytherin girls, or whether this was the Weasley's target. Until he spotted a certain platinum blonde girl.

It wasn't until the holidays started that they could really kick things into gear. They alternated between talking to Hermione, asking her questions, and investigating records in both Muggle and Magical Britain. Certain things in Hermione's life didn't fit, such as memories of vague parents, that were not Mr and Mrs Granger, how the Granger's refused to ever discuss the topic, and how they had no pictures of her before she was 3. But it was nothing conclusive, she needed something bigger that couldn't be dismissed as perhaps Hermione remembering some imaginary tale she had made as a child.

"I found something!" Daphne Greengrass declared one day, holding a official document proudly in the air. She explained how she had been investigating muggle orphanages when she had stumbled across this, hidden deeply in a forgotten section of the building, one that according to the caretakers didn't exist. Things began to fall into place, and Calista discovered mental blocks in Hermione's mind, ones that had been placed there with care and skill.

It took some bribes and threats, but they had someone remove the block, and suddenly Hermione burst into tears. She didn't know why, but Calista felt the urge to comfort her. "It's okay Hermione, tell us what's wrong." She said. Oh Merlin she was developing feelings for Granger wasn't she? The urges to protect and comfort her were growing, and while she had planned to use Hermione as a way to release sexual tension, she found herself admiring small details about her.

Hermione didn't quite know where her relationship with Calista lied, but she was greatly comforted by Selwyn pulling her into a hug and stroking her hair. "I.. I remember now. There was this orphanage. My father placed me there, then he left. I was a baby, unable to speak. I.. I think he made sure I couldn't remember anything before he placed me there. But why couldn't I remember the orphanage? I should have spent around two years there. Who could have removed the memories? Who even are my parents?"

Calista felt both glad and horrified. Glad everything was going according to plan and that Hermione was now questioning her identity, horrified at Hermione's reaction and tears. "Everything will be alright, I promise Hermione." She said, acting completely out of character which worried herself. It was no use pretending, and it did feel good just to hold her, to calm her. Oh Merlin she was in it deep. She couldn't even pretend to be badass now, she just wanted Hermione to stop crying.

Daphne, Ginny and Luna did more research while Calista struggled with her feelings. "Surely Hermione should remember the orphanage, but they were removed as well, this was a pretty professional job as well, only a few loose ends, and that's partially because of the Granger's." Luna said, surprising everyone with her attention to detail. "Someone's come in at a later point, and erased Hermione's memories again. I don't see why her father would come back if not to take her back, and everything I can find suggests the Granger's are just regular muggles, no magical connections unless you go back hundreds of years maybe, and that's a big maybe." She continued, doubling down on trying to figure something out.

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Dumbledore and Sirius continued to speak to each other, discussing the situation, what might have happened to Harry, and what they could do. if Dumbledore himself had forgotten there was no chance anyone else would recall anything but the vaguest sense of Harry. It was hard not to panic, and Dumbledore had plenty of other things to think about. Man things weighed his mind, including Miss Granger, but that was a thought for another time. "Before we continue Albus, I have to ask, what's with the hand?" Sirius asked, pointing at Dumledore's shriveled dead hand.

Dumbledore sighed and recounted his tale. "I was investigating artifacts in the old Gaunt house. Do you remember Marvolo Gaunt?" He asked Sirius, and it took some time for Sirius to recall. "Yeah, I remember a bit. Never saw him, but among people like my parents he was the laughingstock of the Pureblood world. Tried to keep their blood TOO pure, and they were destitute by the time Marvolo's Great Great Grandfather was born. Used to be a huge name in the Wizarding world according to the books."

"Yes. He is also the maternal grandfather to Voldemort. The tale is simple, his daughter Merope ran off with a muggle she bewitched with a love potion, and died in childbirth. There was something I believed Voldemort was doing, something horrible, and I believed I knew what objects he was using for it. Voldemort was making Horcruxes, and I believed he used his Grandfather's old family ring for one of them."

Sirius looked sick, and shaken. "Albus are you sure? Horcruxes? Bloody fucking hell, not even Grindelwald sunk that low. I never considered it possible, just some myth. So his grandfather's ring.... No. Albus no! Please tell me you didn't wear it! I don't want to consider that you did something so utterly stupid!"

Albus let out a wry smile and a sigh. "I was a fool, I forgot myself for a moment, and when I realized it was to late. Severus and I have a contingency plan. I have exhausted all means of curing it. By the end of the year I'll be dead anyway, or worse then dead, and Severus needs a way to prove to Voldemort he is loyal. I told Harry about the Horcruxes, but with him missing someone else needed to know. I need you to hunt them down with me before I die Sirius, otherwise there is no chance Voldemort can be stopped. I understand you are concerned about Harry, but we have no leads whatsoever. Our best chance is to destroy the Horcruxes and hope for a miracle."

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

Hermione eventually calmed down with the help of Calista and began to do research herself, getting further the any of the others could. "I know of this test, to see who someone is related to which is done by the Ministry. It's old, really old, barely ever used anymore and then only when it comes to specific circumstances, like a.. bastard orphan alone in the world." She said quietly, still not completely over what had happened. Setting up the test took a few days to organise with the Ministry, mostly because there was almost no one with the knowledge anymore to actually do it. But after a few key bribes, they had someone ready and willing to perform it.

"Alright, I'll need a prick of your blood." The Ministry official said, using a small magical knife to gain a drop of blood, which was emptied into a golden hand-basin. After a few minutes the official spoke. "Alright, says here your parents were..."
141222121111122212121.title: Hermione is a secret Pureblood

Calista hunted for an empty class, she needed to fill the blanks.
14122213.content:
Densaugeo

Calista cast the spell, quickly and efficiently, unwilling to consider the attractiveness of the Gryffindor girl. A well of jealousy she didn't want to examine. But she smiled, as Hermione's expression shifted to horror. Her hands rising to her face covering it, covering her mouth.

Her face was a picture a battle between sympathy, horror and humiliation flickering across it, as her two top teeth extended. Her speech impaired, she couldn't talk, she couldn't curse. Calista watched, as tears welled in Hermione's eyes, before she reached out a hand to her... to her!

Calista half-recoiled, then stood her ground. But her tentative hand withdrew, she drooled a mangled word or two and fled running down the corridor away from her. A vague thought occurring, a little too late, that a foot binding curse might have helped bunny Granger, hop away, a match to her buck teeth. Her scuffling feet slipping as she darted around the corner and out of sight.

As she roared with laughter, a little unladylike but well earned, a twinge of regret... or sympathy made it die in her throat.
14122213.title: She draws her wand and curses Hermione.

142.content:
Shouts and screams erupted behind Harry, and behind him there was a -pouf-, and a cloud of pale green smoke billowed around the corner. He watched from beneath the cloak for a moment, until coughing and spluttering he spotted a host of Slytherin girls gust into the corridor. Their expressions a mix of horror, shock, disgust, and from some others a mix of amusement.

More and more girls spilled into the corridor, until Harry felt it was too full. That he might be discovered even cloaked, an accidental bump could reveal his presence. He also felt the slightest hint of a cough approaching, perhaps a side-effect of the gas he mused. A cough would certainly give him away.

He paused only for a moment, as a gorgeous platinum blonde girl staggered into the passageway. She was tall with curves in just the right places. She was flanked by a couple of chubbier girls. Still nicely rounded, but not as tall, nor so beautiful. But there was something odd about her. At first Harry thought she was struggling to breathe. But it became clearer she was holding her bust. Her eyes wide in horror, her fingers softly, blankly squeezing and releasing her chest. She slowly shook her head in denial, her lips silently mouthing something.

She gave a squeal of surprise, as Millicent Bullstrode joined her, and gave her a sisterly hug, forcefully pulling them together.

It was only the tickle building at the back of Harry's throat, and the desire not to get mixed up in whatever the Weasley's had done. She left them, and headed off for somewhere with fewer people, and more privacy.

The answer was obvious, Harry headed for the Room of Requirement. It had always fascinated him, since he'd first heard of it. The random detritus stored there told a more interesting history of the castle than that book Hermione kept trying to get him to read.

He watched patiently, as the wall took on the deep engraving of a double door, and doorway, sinking backwards until part transfigured from stone to wood. With a careful glance to either side, Harry checked he was unobserved and entered. Slipping the cloak off, and forcing it into his bag.

It was an immense hall broken up with tall, sturdy pillars reaching high up to the ceiling. Most of the room was hidden beyond the chaotic piles of rubbish. Despite it's size, it reminded him of his cupboard beneath the stairs. But with that twist of magic, he felt home.

His breathing slowed and deepened as he wandered further into the room. His hands tracing their way over the surface of the various artefacts as he went. Mostly his fingers left dusty trails where the passed. But occasionally, a spark of residual magic sparked weakly into life.

He smiled at his reflection as he passed a mirror. Pausing only briefly as the image shimmered. With a snort of surprise he recognised the mirror of erised. But with determination he walked on beyond it's siren call. His feet only stumbling briefly.

The musty smell built until Harry felt sure he must be near the centre of the room. If it even had a centre. A dark mahogany wardrobe loomed over him, ornately carved, and despite the lack of attention still free of dust. The mirror on the door thankfully didn't shimmer or show any magical properties.

His fingers went unbidden to the wardrobe door. Opening it with only a slow, quiet creak. Inside was a corset just hanging there. His imagination already racing to picture Ginny wearing it. Her waist pulled in tight... and the girls lifted and put even more on display. Swallowing as he imagined their liquid dancing movement with even the simplest of breaths keeping the girls mobile and eye-catching, he felt himself stiffen.

He pulled his hand back from feeling the soft material, unaware of even having reached out. Despite it's size it looked strong and confining, with it's bright buckles and shiny chrome fasteners. Trying to dismiss the thoughts racing through his mind. Harry doubted it would ever fit. It was clearly tailored for a tiny waist, and a large bust. It just didn't look like it would fit Ginny. Not that Ginny was fat... or flat... He felt his cheeks turn a deeper red, warming as his thoughts spiralled once more in intriguing directions.

Deciding to end them, he pulled the corset out. The red and black looking less comfortable, the shaping struts less forgiving than ever. The hanger fell free, clattering against the wooden floor of the wardrobe. His breathing was more rapid, now he held it, it was clearly too small. He doubted it would fit even the diminutive Cho Chang.

Fingers trembling he eased the door closed, and held it against his own body for comparison. Just to see how small it truly was.

A flash, a flare of brilliant light forced Harry to squeeze his eyes shut against the pain. Even his breathing caught. As the painful redness vanished from his eyelids, and he felt the heat of the light diminish. He blinked his watering eyes. His blurry vision returning only slowly as the tears streaked down his cheeks.

He still had the same difficulty taking a deeper breath. But, as his vision cleared, he doubted what he saw.

There reflected in the mirror was a startled and horrified witch. Her bust rising and falling with each shallow breath. Her mane of midnight hair cascading over her shoulders. It didn't take Harry long to recognise the same tickling sensation from his own shoulders.

His uniform gone, nothing more than a memory. All his clothes now reduced to a corset and some ruffled black panties.

https://68.media.tumblr.com/2bbcd8031da2...

He... No, She. She wanted to scream, but the corset was too tight, her lungs incapable of taking enough air for the scream she release to the uncaring ceiling.
